WHITEPAPER:7 also still described `blit-cli` as the "user binary" without noting the `[[bin]] name = "blit"` rename + admin-verb merge — corrected. **Low**: `README.md:149` documented the daemon-backed completion fallback as `blit completions remote <prefix>`, but the actual CLI signature is `blit completions remote <REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` (matching `docs/cli/blit.1.md:21`). Rewrote the README line to show the correct positional `<REMOTE>` plus `--prefix` flag form. Two intentionally-left-alone files: `docs/plan/POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md:85` (real-world incident report dated 2026-05-01 capturing the exact pre-rename command that triggered the bug — historical evidence) and `docs/bugs/{single-file-source-silent-noop,copy-destination-semantics}.md` (bug reports also dated against the pre-rename binary). Verified by `grep -rn 'release/blit-cli\|/tmp/blit-cli\|--bin blit-cli'` across `docs/`, `scripts/`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md` — only the three intentional historical references remain. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1895:**2026-05-05 21:30:00Z** - **DOCS**: §2.2 + §4 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — closed the last P0 by removing/contextualising every `blit-utils` doc reference, since the admin verbs ship as subcommands of the single `blit` binary. **Per the §4 table**: (a) `docs/plan/PROJECT_STATE_ASSESSMENT.md` — added top-level "Superseded" callout pointing at the release plan; CLI Surface and Documentation sections rewritten so the `blit-utils` line is gone; Architecture Overview ASCII diagram now lists `blit` (admin verbs included) alongside `blit-daemon` only. (b) `docs/plan/README.md` — Current Status block flipped to point at the release plan as live source of truth; document index labels PSA as superseded snapshot, calls out BLIT_UTILS_PLAN as design-rationale-only. (c) `docs/plan/MASTER_WORKFLOW.md` — Feature-Completeness Goals, Phase 3 gates, and Telemetry row reword "blit-utils" → "blit" admin subcommands. (d) `docs/plan/BLIT_UTILS_PLAN.md` — added prominent Superseded header at top stating the `blit-utils` artifact never shipped and verbs live as `blit <verb>`; document body retained as design rationale per plan §4. (e) `docs/plan/WORKFLOW_PHASE_3.md` and (f) `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` — added post-phase notes at the top so historical body text describing `blit-utils` stays as workflow record but is not read as current state. (g) `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` — User Layer ASCII diagram redrawn (no blit-utils column), `### blit-cli (produces the blit binary)` rewritten with the merged structure including all admin verb modules, `### blit-utils` section replaced with `### Admin verbs` cross-referencing BLIT_UTILS_PLAN as superseded for artifact shape. (h) `docs/cli/blit.1.md` — SYNOPSIS adds `list-modules`, `ls`, `completions shell <SHELL>`, `completions remote <REMOTE>`, and `profile`; Admin Commands section documents all of them plus the `list` smart-dispatch wording matching §2.3 implementation. (i) `README.md` — annotated `crates/blit-cli/` repo-structure entry to clarify it produces the `blit` binary with admin verbs included. **Drive-bys outside the §4 table**: `docs/plan/AI_TELEMETRY_ANALYSIS.md` Phase 3 ("blit-utils Integration") section retitled and rewritten — the standalone-utils-binary integration path is moot post-merge; `docs/plan/TUI_DESIGN.md` ScanResponse-effort estimate updated to point at `blit-cli`'s scan parsing (was "blit-utils-merged"). Files explicitly **left alone**: `TODO.md` historical completed-task lines, `CHANGELOG.md` (already correctly says "merged into blit"), `docs/plan/greenfield_plan_v6.md` (architectural plan, treated as historical), the GPT/audit review artifacts under `docs/`, and the `RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` itself (the source-of-truth describing the cleanup). Verified by `grep -rln blit-utils docs/ README.md TODO.md CHANGELOG.md` — every remaining hit is either an explicit "no separate blit-utils" callout, a historical TODO completed line, the CHANGELOG merge note, or the design-rationale BLIT_UTILS_PLAN now headed by its Superseded banner. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1897:**2026-05-05 20:30:00Z** - **FIX**: Round 45 review of commit `f83a208`. **R45-F1 (High)**: while plumbing scanned features I left a `let total_bytes = scanned_bytes` alias in `crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs:480` and the streaming summary at `:567` then read that local for `summary.total_bytes`. Net effect on the wire: `summary.total_bytes` (contracted as "bytes the pipeline wrote") was reporting scanned bytes, so any incremental skip-unchanged run with all-up-to-date files would have reported the full source size as bytes-written and overcounted throughput. Fix: removed the alias; streaming summary now reads `pipeline_outcome.bytes_written` from `SinkOutcome` directly. The verbose "Planning enumerated" line correctly references `scanned_bytes`. **R45-F2 (Low)**: planner + transfer sub-predictions were still passing `(all_headers.len(), total_bytes)` (which were aliases of scanned_* but only by accident) while the total prediction passed `(scanned_files, scanned_bytes)` explicitly. Made all three predictor calls share the explicit `(scanned_files, scanned_bytes)` feature vector so a future maintainer editing one branch can't reintroduce drift by missing another. New regression test `incremental_run_total_bytes_excludes_skipped_files` in `orchestrator::orchestrator::async_runtime_tests` runs the full local mirror pipeline twice (mirror=true forces streaming planner; skip_unchanged=true means run 2 writes 0 bytes) and asserts `summary.total_bytes == 0` on the second run. Verified the test catches the bug by reintroducing the alias — assertion fires with `total_bytes=4096, scanned_bytes=4096` matching the pre-R45 state. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (was 406, +1 new R45 regression test).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1899:**2026-05-05 19:30:00Z** - **FIX**: Round 44 review of commit `da6ced2`. **R44-F1 (Medium)**: predictor train/query feature mismatch. The pre-pipeline query in `crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs` used `(all_headers.len(), total_bytes)` (scan-side workload), but `record_performance_history` recorded `summary.copied_files` for `PerformanceRecord.file_count`. On any incremental run the predictor saw "predict 1000 files" then trained on "5 files actually copied" — estimates drifted badly. Fix: added `scanned_files: usize` and `scanned_bytes: u64` to `LocalMirrorSummary` (`crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/summary.rs`) with a load-bearing rustdoc explaining the scan-vs-copy split; populated at every summary construction site (streaming pipeline, NoWork, Tiny, Huge, JournalSkip, single-file copy); `record_performance_history` now reads `summary.scanned_{files,bytes}` for the record's feature fields. The bucket-shape counters (`tar_shard_*`, `raw_bundle_*`, `large_*`) still reflect actual writes — they feed `derive_local_plan_tuning` which heuristically sizes destination buckets from past apply behavior, not scan size. Extracted `build_performance_record` from `record_performance_history` so the contract is unit-testable without polluting the global JSONL. Two new tests in `orchestrator::history::tests`: `record_uses_scanned_features_not_copied` (would have failed pre-fix on a 1000-scanned/5-copied incremental) and `bucket_counts_still_reflect_actual_writes` (regression guard against accidentally rerouting bucket counters through scan stats). **R44-F2 (Low)**: `blit profile --json` shape was unasserted. The pre-fix commit message claimed top-level `"predictor": null` for the empty-state, but actual output emits `{"copy": null, "mirror": null}` (predictor file loads even when no profile yet has training data). Picked the structured shape — strictly more informative — and pinned it in `test_utils_profile_json` (`crates/blit-cli/tests/blit_utils.rs`) with assertions covering both empty and trained states. **R44 plan note**: §2.8 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` is correctly framed now as "predictor observability and training," not "adaptive planning behavior" — the predictor is queried, surfaced via `--verbose` and `blit profile --json`, and audit-able, but the Tiny fast-path still does not consult it for routing. Added a Status block to the plan section documenting this. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 406 pass / 0 fail (was 404, +2 history regression tests).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1901:**2026-05-05 18:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.8 phase 2 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — orchestrator and `blit profile` now actually query the predictor. Three load-bearing changes: (1) `crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs` introduces a `plan_done = Instant::now()` checkpoint between the planner stage and the pipeline so `planner_duration_ms` and `transfer_duration_ms` are correctly attributed; pre-fix both fields fed `record_performance_history` got the same whole-run time, so v1 predictor training collapsed into a single regressor. (2) Just before kicking off `execute_sink_pipeline`, the orchestrator queries `predictor.predict(DurationKind::Total/Planner/Transfer, ...)` with the just-scanned `all_headers.len()` and `total_bytes`; populates `LocalMirrorSummary.predictor_estimate` (new field of type `PredictorEstimate { planner_ms, transfer_ms, total_ms, observations, fallback_depth }` in `summary.rs`). `--verbose` now emits two lines: a "Predictor estimate: ..." pre-run line and a "Predictor delta: ..." post-run line showing predicted-vs-actual percent error per phase. (3) `crates/blit-cli/src/profile.rs::run_profile` now calls `predictor.coefficients_for(mode-only, ...)` for both Copy and Mirror, surfaces the result as a coefficients block in human output (`alpha_ms_per_file`, `beta_ms_per_mb`, `gamma_ms` per planner/transfer pair) and as a `predictor.{copy,mirror}` JSON object under `--json`. Smoke test: `blit profile --json` returns the new `predictor` key (null when no v2 profile yet); `blit copy` --verbose under streaming-planner workloads will emit the new predictor lines once 5+ observations accumulate. Tiny fast-path extension (the planner-overhead-dominance signal in the plan) deferred to post-0.1.0 — extending the fast_path scan budget risks edge cases on memory-bounded large-tree workloads, and the predictor's now-load-bearing model is only freshly trained on v2 data; revisit once we have multi-week observability under the new schema. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 404 pass / 0 fail.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1903:**2026-05-05 18:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.8 phase 1 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — extended the predictor data model so its predictions can actually drive decisions. Schema bumped to v2; existing `PerformancePredictor::load` already drops state on version mismatch, so the bump is transparent. New types: `DurationCoefficients` (planner + transfer coefficient pair per profile), `DurationKind { Planner, Transfer, Total }`, `Prediction { predicted_ms, observations, fallback_depth }`. New API: `predict(kind, mode, src_fs, dst_fs, fast_path, skip_unchanged, checksum, file_count, total_bytes) -> Option<Prediction>` walks a fallback chain — exact key → drop fast_path → drop dest_fs → drop src_fs → mode-only — gated at each step by `MIN_OBSERVATIONS_FOR_CONFIDENCE = 5`. `predict_planner(record)` / `predict_transfer(record)` / `predict_total(record)` are convenience wrappers around `predict(...)`. `coefficients_for(...)` returns the same `DurationCoefficients` clone that `blit profile --json` will surface in phase 2. `observe(record)` now updates BOTH coefficient sets in lockstep (single shared observation counter) so `transfer_duration_ms` becomes a load-bearing input. Removed the old `predict_ms(record) -> f64` and `predict_planner_ms(...) -> Option<(f64, u64)>` — both pre-v2 entry points returned values without confidence gating. Eight new unit tests added: `predict_below_confidence_threshold_returns_none`, `predict_above_confidence_threshold_returns_value` (verifies `fallback_depth == 0`), `predictor_learns_planner_and_transfer_independently`, `predict_total_sums_planner_and_transfer`, `fallback_chain_drops_fast_path_then_dest_then_src`, `schema_version_mismatch_resets_state_on_load`. Existing tests rewritten to use the new API. Phase 2 (orchestrator wire + verbose surface + `blit profile --json` exposure + Tiny fast-path extension when planner-dominant) lands in the next commit. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 404 pass / 0 fail (was 399, +5 new predictor tests + 3 retained-with-new-API).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1905:**2026-05-05 17:00:00Z** - **FIX**: R43 follow-up to R42-F2. The R42 commit added a comment on `MultiStreamSender::finish` claiming it routed through `drain_pipeline_error`, but the body still hand-rolled the same match arms — comment lied about the factoring. Real fix: extracted `drain_pipeline_outcome(handle: JoinHandle<...>) -> Result<SinkOutcome>` as the underlying primitive (Ok-on-clean / wrap-on-err / panic-prefix-on-join). `drain_pipeline_error` now delegates to it for the failure cases and only adds the "Ok-but-channel-closed" race-diagnostic on top. `finish()` calls `drain_pipeline_outcome(handle).await?` directly. Added 3 new unit tests in `drain_pipeline_error_tests`: `drain_outcome_returns_value_on_clean_finish` (passes the SinkOutcome through), `drain_outcome_wraps_pipeline_error` (same "data plane pipeline failed" wrapping that `queue()` produces, regardless of which call site triggered it), `drain_outcome_surfaces_panic_message`. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 399 pass / 0 fail (was 396, +3).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1907:**2026-05-05 16:30:00Z** - **FIX**: Round 42 review of commit `96cbb10`. **R42-F1 (Medium)**: §1.1 sweep was incomplete — three sites still silently dropped metadata errors. (a) `crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/tar_safety.rs::write_extracted_file` mtime + permissions (used by both pull tar-shard receive and the daemon gRPC push fallback); (b) `crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs::execute_single_file_copy` mtime — single-file copy fast-path was bypassing the warn-don't-silence policy. All three converted to `if let Err(e) = ... { log::warn!(...) }`. Final sweep confirms no `let _ = ` over `set_file_mtime` / `set_permissions` / `set_keepalive` / `.flush()` remains in production paths (the two leftovers — `diff_planner.rs::sync_mtimes` test helper and `win_fs.rs::clear_readonly_recursive` cleanup utility — are appropriately best-effort). **R42-F2 (Low)**: the new pipeline-streaming regression test only exercised `execute_sink_pipeline_streaming` directly, leaving `MultiStreamSender::queue`'s producer-side join-error-drain logic untested. Extracted the load-bearing match arms into a free `drain_pipeline_error(handle: JoinHandle<...>) -> eyre::Report` helper in `crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/mod.rs`. `MultiStreamSender::queue` and `MultiStreamSender::finish` both now route their failure paths through it, so the wrapping reads consistently as "data plane pipeline failed: <cause>" or "data plane pipeline panicked: <join>" regardless of which branch noticed the failure first. Three new unit tests in a fresh `drain_pipeline_error_tests` mod: `drain_returns_pipeline_error_with_failed_prefix` (Ok(Err(_)) path), `drain_returns_panic_message_for_aborted_task` (Err(JoinError) panic path), `drain_diagnoses_clean_close_race` (Ok(Ok(_)) clean-close-race diagnostic — proves we don't silently swallow it). **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 396 pass / 0 fail (was 393, +3 drain-helper tests).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1909:**2026-05-05 16:00:00Z** - **FIX**: §2.7 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — closed `docs/plan/POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md` Round 1. **§1.1**: `let _ = file.flush().await` in `sink.rs::write_file_stream` now propagates as a `Status::internal`-style error via `with_context("flushing {dst}")?` — flush failure is a data-loss signal (user believes file is durable when it isn't). Six other "best-effort metadata" sites in `sink.rs` (block-complete mtime/perms, tar-shard mtime/perms, file-payload mtime, write_block_complete mtime/perms) plus three TCP socket tuning sites in `data_plane.rs` (`set_keepalive`, `set_send_buffer_size`, `set_recv_buffer_size`) plus two `local_worker.rs::single_file_mmap_copy` mtime sites converted from silent `let _ = ...` to `if let Err(e) = ... { log::warn!(...) }` so misconfigurations are visible without making them hard transfer errors. **§1.1b**: `MultiStreamSender::queue` in `crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/mod.rs` no longer turns "tx.send failed" into the generic `"data plane pipeline closed unexpectedly"` string. `pipeline_handle` is now `Option<JoinHandle<...>>`; on send-failure, `take()` it, await, and surface the underlying error (`Ok(Err(e))` → `e.wrap_err("data plane pipeline failed")`, `Err(join)` → panic message). New regression test `pipeline_streaming_surfaces_underlying_sink_error` in `pipeline.rs::tests` uses a `FailingSink` to assert the underlying error message reaches the caller verbatim. **§1.2**: `TarShardExecutor` decision — kept with a thorough docstring explaining (a) gRPC-fallback streaming framing differs from TCP path's contiguous-buffer apply, requiring a `TarShardReceiver` trait extraction to unify, and (b) buffer-pool sizing benefits from real fallback throughput numbers. Deferred to post-0.1.0; tracked in `POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md` §1.2. **§1.3**: `WHITEPAPER.md` §3 "Data plane wire format" updated — `BLOCK_COMPLETE := 0x03 path_len:u32 path:bytes total_size:u64 mtime:i64 perms:u32` (was missing the trailing `mtime + perms` shipped in `a7d659f`); added an explanatory paragraph on why the metadata trails the terminator. `POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md` Round 1 status block flipped to "Closed 2026-05-05". **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 393 pass / 0 fail (was 392, +1 new pipeline-streaming regression test).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1911:**2026-05-05 15:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.5 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — `blit completions` now generates real shell-completion scripts via `clap_complete`, honoring the `README.md:33` "shell completions" promise. Reshaped `Completions(CompletionArgs)` into a subcommand group: `blit completions shell <SHELL>` (bash/zsh/fish/powershell/elvish) writes a clap_complete-generated script to stdout; `blit completions remote <REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` is the existing daemon-backed CompletePath RPC the generated shell scripts call internally for `server:/module/<Tab>` style remote-path completion. New dep `clap_complete = "4"` on `blit-cli`. Three lib unit tests added: `clap_definition_validates` (forces clap's `debug_assert`), `shell_completions_generate_for_bash` (script defines `_blit()` and references known verbs), `shell_completions_generate_for_each_supported_shell` (totality across `clap_complete::Shell`). New integration test `test_utils_completions_shell_bash_emits_script`. Existing tests `test_utils_completions`, `test_utils_completions_dirs_only`, `test_admin_completions_*` updated to call `blit completions remote <target>` (subcommand split). README.md `### Admin Utilities` has new install snippets for bash/zsh/fish; `find --pattern ".csv"` updated to `"*.csv"` to match the post-§2.4 glob semantics. CHANGELOG entry rewritten to describe the two completion subforms. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 392 pass / 0 fail (was 386, +3 cli tests + 2 list smart-dispatch from §2.3 + 1 shell-bash integration).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1913:**2026-05-05 14:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.3 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — `blit list` now smart-dispatches by target shape. Bare-host targets (`server`, `server:9031`, `[::1]:9031`) route to the `list-modules` RPC; targets with a module/path tail (`server:/module/`, `server:/module/sub/`) keep going through the `ls` directory listing. Implementation: factored `run_list_modules`'s body into `pub(crate) async fn list_modules_remote(remote, json) -> Result<()>` in `list_modules.rs`; `ls::list_remote_path` short-circuits to that helper when `remote.path == RemotePath::Discovery`. Both code paths reuse the same `--json` and human shapes, so `blit list-modules <host>` and `blit list <host>` produce identical output. Two new integration tests in `crates/blit-cli/tests/admin_verbs.rs`: `test_admin_list_smart_dispatch_bare_host` (bare host gets the "Modules on …" header), `test_admin_list_smart_dispatch_module_path_routes_to_ls` (module path stays as `ls`-style streamed entries; explicit regression guard against routing everything to list-modules). The existing `test_admin_list_modules`, `test_admin_list`, `test_admin_list_subdirectory` continue to pass. `docs/cli/blit.1.md:13` (`blit list <REMOTE>`) and `:75` already documented the smart-dispatch wording — no manpage change needed. **Tests**: `cargo test -p blit-cli --test admin_verbs test_admin_list` 5 pass.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1915:**2026-05-04 22:00:00Z** - **FIX**: Round 41 review (R41-F1, R41-F2, R41-F3) of commits `0ca489b` + `090f5cd`. **R41-F1 (High)**: binary-rename sweep was incomplete — five test files (`diagnostics_dump.rs`, `remote_tcp_fallback.rs`, `remote_remote.rs`, `remote_checksum_negotiation.rs`, `remote_pull_mirror.rs`) still constructed `bin_dir.join("blit-cli[.exe]")`, so `cargo test --workspace` on a clean target tree failed with `spawn: No such file or directory`; the previous "385 pass" report only held because stale `target/release/blit-cli` was still around. All five updated to `"blit" / "blit.exe"`. Cosmetic panic-message strings in `remote_resume.rs`, `remote_regression.rs`, `remote_parity.rs`, `remote_push_single_file.rs` swept for consistency. **R41-F2 (High)**: bench scripts still pointed at the old binary — `scripts/bench_remote_remote.sh` (BLIT default + comment), `scripts/bench_10gbe.sh` (BLIT path), `scripts/bench_local_mirror.sh` (build invocation `--bin blit-cli` and resulting BLIT_BIN path) all updated. **R41-F3 (Medium)**: `find --pattern` glob set `literal_separator(false)`, letting `*` cross `/`. With the basename fallback this caused over-matches like `foo*.csv` matching `foo/bar.csv`. Switched to `literal_separator(true)` (POSIX shell-glob semantics; `*` does NOT cross `/`); basename fallback still gives "find files at any depth" via `*.csv`. Added regression test `test_admin_find_glob_star_does_not_cross_path_separator`. CLI `FindArgs.pattern` rustdoc rewritten to spell out the no-cross-separator behavior and the basename fallback. **Tests**: clean `target/`, `cargo test --workspace` 386 pass / 0 fail (was 385, +1 new R41-F3 test).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1917:**2026-05-04 21:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.1 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — renamed the produced CLI binary from `blit-cli` to `blit`, matching every doc/manpage/README example. `crates/blit-cli/Cargo.toml` keeps the workspace-member name `blit-cli` and adds `[[bin]] name = "blit"` so only the produced binary renames; cargo `--package blit-cli` and `-p blit-cli` references stay valid. Test helpers (`tests/common/mod.rs::TestContext::new`, `tests/single_file_copy.rs::cli_bin`) walk the build dir and previously hardcoded `"blit-cli"` / `"blit-cli.exe"`; updated to `"blit"` / `"blit.exe"`. CI artifact path in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` and the binary list in `scripts/build-release.sh` + `scripts/windows/build-release.ps1` flipped accordingly. Per-platform run/bench scripts (`scripts/{linux,macos,windows}/run-journal-fastpath.{sh,ps1}`, `run-remote-fallback.sh`, `bench-local-mirror.ps1`) now reference `target/.../release/blit{.exe}`. Build-instruction strings in scripts updated from `--bin blit-cli` to `--bin blit`. Stale `target/release/blit-cli` and `target/debug/blit-cli` artifacts removed to confirm nothing was silently relying on them. **Tests**: `cargo build --release -p blit-cli --bin blit` produces `target/release/blit`; `cargo test --workspace` 385 pass / 0 fail.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1919:**2026-05-04 21:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: §2.4 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` — `find --pattern` now glob-matches via `globset`, replacing pre-0.1.0 substring containment. Daemon-side `stream_find_entries` in `service/admin.rs` builds a `globset::GlobBuilder` matcher once with `case_insensitive(!case_sensitive)` and `literal_separator(false)`; matcher tested against both the entry's relative path and its file-name basename so `--pattern '*.csv'` finds nested files via the basename fallback even without `**/`. Invalid globs surface as `Status::invalid_argument` from the daemon and propagate to a non-zero CLI exit. CLI rustdoc on `FindArgs.pattern` updated to describe glob syntax. CHANGELOG: removed "find uses substring matching" known-limitation row, added a glob behavior note under Admin Utilities. Two new integration tests in `crates/blit-cli/tests/admin_verbs.rs`: `test_admin_find_glob_invalid_pattern_rejected` (malformed `[unterminated` rejected) and `test_admin_find_glob_nested_with_double_star` (`**/*.csv` finds top + deep entries). Updated existing `test_admin_find_with_pattern` and `test_utils_find` / `test_utils_find_json` to use glob patterns (`*.csv`, `*.md`, `*.log`) instead of substring fragments. New daemon dep: `globset = "0.4.18"`. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 385 pass / 0 fail (was 383, +2 new glob tests).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1921:**2026-05-04 19:55:00Z** - **FIX**: Closed F14 from `docs/reviews/codebase_review_2026-05-01.md`. Migrated `crates/blit-core/src/change_journal/snapshot.rs::macos::capture_snapshot` from `fsevent_sys::FSEventsGetCurrentEventId` (deprecated by upstream in favour of `objc2-core-services`) to `objc2_core_services::FSEventsGetCurrentEventId` under the `FSEvents` cargo feature. Same call shape (`unsafe { ... }`), same `u64` return — only the binding crate changed. `crates/blit-core/Cargo.toml` macOS-target dep flipped from `fsevent-sys = "5"` to `objc2-core-services = { version = "0.3", features = ["FSEvents"] }`. Both deprecation warnings on macOS builds are gone (`cargo build -p blit-core` is now warning-free apart from the pre-existing unused-variable in `fs_capability/macos.rs`). With F14 closed, **14 of 15 baseline findings** are closed; only F15 (deferred structured-logging epic) remains. **Validation**: `cargo check --workspace`; `cargo build -p blit-core` (no fsevent/deprecation warnings); `cargo test --workspace` (383 pass / 0 fail).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1923:**2026-05-03 01:25:00Z** - **DOCS/TOOLS**: Completed Phase 3 cleanup/harness items from `docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md` except live benchmark execution. Documented `RemoteTransferSource` as the explicit legacy `--relay-via-cli` primitive now that default remote→remote uses destination-side `DelegatedPull`. Added `scripts/bench_remote_remote.sh` to compare default direct delegation against explicit relay using identical remote payloads and the existing `BLIT_TEST_COUNTER_FILE` byte-path counter. Added `docs/perf/remote_remote_benchmarks.md` as the results capture template. Updated the plan status/checklist; remaining unchecked item is running the benchmark on the target network and filling real numbers. **Validation**: `cargo fmt -- --check`; `cargo test -p blit-cli remote_remote_direct`; `bash -n scripts/bench_remote_remote.sh`.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1925:**2026-05-03 01:05:00Z** - **FIX**: Closed Round 37 findings from `docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md`. **R37-F1**: `RemotePullClient::pull_sync_with_spec` now preserves a typed `PullSyncError::Negotiation` for source-side pull-sync refusals before negotiation completes, and the destination `DelegatedPull` handler maps that to `DelegatedPullError::NEGOTIATE` instead of generic `TRANSFER`. Added core wire test coverage for initial RPC rejection classification and remote→remote CLI integration coverage where a fake source rejects `pull_sync`; the CLI surfaces "source refused delegated pull" and relay counters stay zero. **R37-F2**: delegated `BytesProgress` is now treated as cumulative state and converted to deltas before feeding the CLI progress accumulator; duplicate cumulative updates are not double-counted. Proto comments now document the cumulative semantics. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo fmt -- --check`; `cargo test -p blit-core pull_sync_with_spec`; `cargo test -p blit-cli remote_remote_direct`; `cargo test -p blit-cli --test remote_remote`; `cargo test -p blit-daemon delegated_pull`; `cargo test --workspace` (all passed; existing macOS FSEvents deprecation/F14 warnings and the pre-existing macOS test unused-variable warning remain).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1927:**2026-05-03 00:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Implemented Phase 2 of `docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md`. Remote→remote `copy`/`mirror`/`move` now dispatch to destination-side `DelegatedPull` by default, with `--relay-via-cli` as the only legacy relay escape hatch. Added `crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/remote_remote_direct.rs` to build the same `TransferOperationSpec` as pull-sync, call `DelegatedPull`, surface stale-daemon/gate/connect/transfer errors without silent fallback, and print delegated summaries. Added env-gated test instrumentation in `blit-core::remote::instrumentation` so integration tests can assert the CLI sent zero data-plane payload bytes and never constructed `RemoteTransferSource` on the direct path, while `--relay-via-cli` does both. Reworked `crates/blit-cli/tests/remote_remote.rs` to cover direct byte-path isolation, gate-rejection no-fallback, stale destination `Unimplemented` no-fallback, and explicit relay. Updated CLI docs/help for `--relay-via-cli`. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo check -p blit-cli`; `cargo test -p blit-cli remote_remote_direct`; `cargo test -p blit-cli --test remote_remote`; `cargo test -p blit-core pull_sync_with_spec`; `cargo test -p blit-daemon delegated_pull`; `cargo test --workspace` (all passed; existing macOS FSEvents deprecation/F14 warnings remain).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1931:**2026-05-02 23:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed F13 + F3 from `docs/reviews/codebase_review_2026-05-01.md` — the last remaining baseline polish items. **F13** (docs drift): with F2 making canonical-path containment always-on, the `use_chroot` config field was loaded-but-unused and the docs claimed enforcement that pre-F2 didn't exist. Removed `use_chroot` and `root_use_chroot` entirely from `ModuleConfig`/`RootExport`/`RootSpec`/`RawModule`/`RawDaemonSection`, the daemon startup banner, and `service::util::resolve_module`. TOML configs containing those keys are silently ignored (serde unknown-field tolerance). `docs/DAEMON_CONFIG.md`: removed the option from example, table, and added a new "Path containment" subsection explaining always-on enforcement with an honest TOCTOU caveat. Updated `docs/plan/WORKFLOW_PHASE_3.md`, `MASTER_WORKFLOW.md`, `BLIT_UTILS_PLAN.md`, `greenfield_plan_v6.md` to describe the post-F2 reality. Drive-by cleanup: deleted the orphaned `crates/blit-daemon/src/types.rs` and `crates/blit-daemon/src/config.rs` files (never declared as modules, dead code from a prior refactor). 5 test files migrated to drop `use_chroot` from their `ModuleSection` definitions. **F3** (reframed in triage as docs-only): added a "Trust Model and Network Exposure" section to `docs/DAEMON_CONFIG.md` stating explicitly that `0.0.0.0` default is intentional for a network file daemon, security comes from operator network controls + per-transfer tokens (not loopback-by-default), and TLS/mutual-auth fronting is out of scope. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (188 lib + 23 daemon + integration suites, all green). With F13 + F3 closed, the original 2026-05-01 baseline review has 13 of 15 findings closed; remaining are F14 (FSEvents deprecation) and F15 (structured logging — explicitly deferred).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1933:**2026-05-02 22:15:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 15 finding R15-F1 (Low) — F11's user-facing closure. The previous F11 fix added daemon ack-mismatch handling in `RemotePullClient::pull_sync`, but `transfers/endpoints.rs::ensure_remote_transfer_supported` rejected `--checksum` for ALL remote transfers before the new code could fire. F11's CLI behavior was therefore unreachable. Made the gate direction-aware: `ensure_remote_pull_supported` allows `--checksum` (the F11 ack negotiates), `ensure_remote_push_supported` keeps it rejected for push/remote-remote (no equivalent capability negotiation in the push protocol yet). New integration test `remote_checksum_negotiation.rs` spins up a daemon with `--no-server-checksums`, runs `blit copy --checksum server:/test/file.txt dest`, and asserts (a) the client refuses with a clear ack-mismatch error, (b) no file is copied via silent fallback. Companion test confirms the same command succeeds against a daemon with checksums enabled. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (188 lib + 23 daemon + 27 metrics-test bin + 2 new checksum-negotiation integration tests, all green).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1935:**2026-05-02 21:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed F5, F11, F12, F9 from `docs/reviews/codebase_review_2026-05-01.md` — the lower-priority items from the baseline triage. **F5** (Medium): replaced inc_active/dec_active scattered around `service/core.rs` with `TransferMetrics::enter_transfer() -> ActiveGuard`, an RAII handle that decrements on Drop so panics/cancellation can't leak the gauge. Moved `inc_purge` to dispatch boundary so it counts attempts (matching push/pull semantics) instead of successes. Module doc updated with the attempts/errors/gauge contract. 5 metrics tests including panic-recovery assertion. **F11** (Medium): pull_sync receive loop now stores `ack.server_checksums_enabled` on `RemotePullReport` and bails early at the ack if the user requested `--checksum` but the daemon has checksums disabled — silent fallback to size+mtime would lie about the comparison strength the user asked for. **F12** (Low–Medium): documented `blit check` equivalence model in CLI `--help` (new `CHECK_SEMANTICS_HELP` constant, `after_long_help` on `CheckArgs`) and `CheckArgs` rustdoc. Pinned the documented behavior with 6 unit tests covering: matching-files-zero-diffs, empty-dirs-not-in-equivalence, symlinks-skipped, file-vs-dir-mismatch-on-file-side, missing-on-dest, --one-way ignores extras. **F9** (Medium): split `execute_local_mirror` into a sync wrapper + new `execute_local_mirror_async`. The async core is callable from any Tokio runtime; the sync wrapper just builds a runtime and block_ons. 2 tests prove async-from-async-context works and the sync wrapper is unchanged. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (188 lib + 23 daemon + 27 metrics-test bin + integration suites, all green).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1937:**2026-05-02 20:15:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 13 finding R13-F1 (Medium). The F2 fix protected per-file writes via `resolve_contained_path`, but the push-mirror purge path enumerates `module.path` (post-mutation by `destination_path`) before the per-entry containment check fires. If the supplied `destination_path` traversed an in-module symlink pointing outside the module root, the purge would enumerate outside the canonical root before refusing any individual delete. Two-layer fix: (1) `push/control.rs` now calls `verify_contained(&canonical_root, &new_path)` immediately after the destination_path mutation, rejecting the push at handshake; (2) `purge_extraneous_entries` calls `verify_contained` before enumeration, defense-in-depth for any future caller that bypasses the handshake check. New integration test `f2_push_destination_path_symlink_rejected` spins up a daemon, places `module/escape -> /sibling`, attempts `blit mirror src/ server:/test/escape/`, and asserts both the daemon refusal and that `/sibling`'s files were not touched. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (186 lib + 20 daemon + 3 F2 integration tests, all green).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1939:**2026-05-02 19:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed F2 from `docs/reviews/codebase_review_2026-05-01.md` — the highest-severity remaining baseline finding. The lexical `safe_join` chokepoint catches `../`/abs/UNC/Windows-shaped wire paths but doesn't follow symlinks, so a pre-existing symlink inside a module pointing outside the root (e.g. `module/escape -> /etc`) would have any daemon read/write follow it. New `path_safety::contained_join` (and `verify_contained` for already-resolved targets) walks to the deepest existing ancestor, canonicalizes, and confirms `starts_with(canonical_module_root)`. 10 unit tests cover symlink-escape rejection (top-level, deep, parent-of-nonexistent), legit intra-module symlinks, lexical-traversal rejection, and the empty-rel single-file case. Wired into every daemon site that touches a path under a module root: `service/core.rs` (list, find, du, purge), `service/admin.rs` (delete_rel_paths_sync, stream_disk_usage, stream_find_entries), `service/pull.rs` and `service/pull_sync.rs` (pull entry-point), `service/push/data_plane.rs` (file dest + tar shard apply), `service/push/control.rs` (file_requires_upload). Added a separate `canonical_root: PathBuf` field on `ModuleConfig` because the push handler mutates `module.path` to bake in destination subpaths (rsync "copy into here" semantics) — containment must check against the never-mutated original. New integration tests in `crates/blit-cli/tests/f2_chroot_containment.rs` spin up a real daemon, place a symlink inside the module pointing outside, attempt to pull through it, assert the daemon refuses with a containment error, and verify the sensitive sibling file was not copied. Companion test confirms legit intra-module symlinks (`latest -> v1`) still work. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (186 lib + 20 daemon + integration suites, all green). F2 closes the last release-blocking security finding from the original baseline review.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1941:**2026-05-02 18:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 11 finding R11-F1 (High) — completed F7 properly. The previous F7 fix bounded the *reservation* via `try_reserve_exact` but the actual `read_to_end` call would still grow the Vec past that bound if the remote source streamed extra bytes. A peer could declare `header.size = 4` and stream 1 GiB; the post-read length check would catch the discrepancy but only after the buffer had grown past the bound. Fix: extract `read_remote_entry_bounded` as a generic helper that wraps the reader with `tokio::io::AsyncReadExt::take(size + 1)` so the read itself is capped; the +1 is the over-read canary that triggers the post-read length check. Helper is `R: AsyncRead + Unpin` so tests use `std::io::Cursor` instead of a real `RemotePullClient` mock. 5 new tests in `remote_bounded_read_tests`: happy path, under-read, over-read (verifies buffer caps at size+1 even when source sends 1 MiB), defensive cap rejection, empty file. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (176 lib + 20 daemon + integration suites, all green). F7 now fully closed.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1943:**2026-05-02 17:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed F7 + F8 from `docs/reviews/codebase_review_2026-05-01.md` (the original baseline review) in one small hardening pass. **F7** (now elevated to High in the triage): `RemoteTransferSource::prepare_payload` was doing `Vec::with_capacity(header.size as usize)` from a daemon-controlled `FileHeader.size` — the send-side mirror of R6-F1 that affects remote→remote transfers. Extracted `validate_remote_tar_shard_sizes` as a private helper that bounds both per-entry and cumulative shard sizes against `tar_safety::MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES`, uses `checked_add` for the sum, and is unit-testable without a `RemotePullClient` mock. Replaced the unbounded `Vec::with_capacity` with `try_reserve_exact`, plus an explicit length-match check after `read_to_end` so a remote source that lies about a file's size produces a hard error rather than a malformed tar. **F8**: `MAX_WIRE_TAR_SHARD_BYTES` in pipeline.rs was 1 GiB while the helper's `MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES` is 256 MiB — the wire reader accepted shards the helper would reject. Single source of truth: `MAX_WIRE_TAR_SHARD_BYTES = tar_safety::MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES as usize` (const). 5 new tests in `remote_tar_size_tests` (within bounds, at cap, per-entry above cap, cumulative above cap, u64 overflow). **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (171 lib + 20 daemon + integration suites, all green).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1945:**2026-05-02 16:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 9 finding R9-F1 (Low). The R8-F2 fix (rejecting stream EOF without UploadComplete) was previously verified by code review only — the new framing-helper unit tests covered R8-F1 size/cap rules but not the actual EOF path through `receive_fallback_data`. To make the function unit-testable without a real gRPC server, generic-ified its signature over `tokio_stream::Stream<Item = Result<ClientPushRequest, Status>>` (tonic::Streaming already implements this trait, so production callers are unchanged) and switched the loop body from `.message()` to `.next().await.transpose()` — semantically identical. 3 new regression tests in `data_plane::tests` drive the function over `tokio_stream::iter` of synthetic message vectors: EOF after `FileManifest`, EOF after `TarShardHeader`, and EOF on an empty stream (no UploadComplete ever). All three assert the rejection now lives in the test suite, not just code review. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (166 lib + 20 daemon + integration suites, all green).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1947:**2026-05-02 15:45:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 8 review findings R8-F1 and R8-F2 from `docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md`. Both bugs were in the daemon's gRPC push fallback receive loop (`receive_fallback_data` in `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs`) — the framing layer that runs *before* the shared `tar_safety` helper sees the buffer. **R8-F1** (Medium — DoS): the chunk accumulator was capped only by an 8 MiB initial reservation; the actual `Vec` grew unbounded. `archive_size == 0` skipped the overflow check entirely, and `archive_size > MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES` was bypassed at the framing layer. Reject zero archive_size when files are present, reject `archive_size > MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES` at TarShardHeader, and enforce both the declared size and the local cap on every chunk via a new `check_fallback_chunk_overflow` helper. **R8-F2** (Medium — silent partial success): stream EOF was treated as graceful end. But FileManifest and TarShardHeader remove entries from `pending` before bytes arrive, so a client could send a header, close the stream, and the final `pending.is_empty()` check would pass despite no data ever landing. Track `upload_complete_seen` and bail on EOF without UploadComplete or with `active.is_some()`. Extracted `validate_fallback_shard_archive_size` and `check_fallback_chunk_overflow` as private helpers so the rules are unit-testable without spinning up a full gRPC server. 7 new framing tests pin every boundary condition (zero size, at-cap, above cap, declared overflow, local-cap overflow, u64 overflow, within-bounds). **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (166 lib + 17 daemon + integration suites, all green).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1949:**2026-05-02 14:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Round 7 cleanup — extracted shared `tar_safety` primitive in `blit-core/src/remote/transfer/tar_safety.rs` and migrated all three tar-shard receive sites onto it: `pull.rs::apply_pull_tar_shard` (gRPC fallback), `sink.rs::write_tar_shard_payload` (TCP data plane + local-local), `blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs::apply_tar_shard_sync` (daemon push receive). The migration closed a previously-latent High-severity bug on the daemon side: `apply_tar_shard_sync` was calling `Entry::unpack`, so an authenticated push client could ship a tar shard with a Symlink entry at a benign path pointing at `/etc/passwd` and a later push to that path would write through the symlink — same R5-F2 class as the pull side, but server-side. New regression test `apply_tar_shard_rejects_symlink_entry` proves the daemon now rejects non-regular entries up front. The shared helper also brought R6-F1 size bounds and R6-F3 mtime/permissions preservation to `sink.rs` and the daemon, where they were missing. 8 new unit tests in `tar_safety::tests`; net +8 lib tests workspace-wide (166 total). **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` all green.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1951:**2026-05-02 13:15:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 6 review findings R6-F1 through R6-F3 from `docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md`. **R6-F1** (Medium — DoS via per-entry size): `apply_pull_tar_shard` was preallocating with `Vec::with_capacity(header.size as usize)`, where `header.size` came from the daemon-authored `TarShardHeader.files`. A daemon could keep the shard `archive_size` under the 256 MiB cap while putting `u64::MAX` in a per-file header, forcing a huge initial allocation. Now validates `entry.size() == header.size` and `header.size <= shard.declared_size <= MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES` before any allocation, then uses `Vec::try_reserve_exact` so a still-pathological size returns `AllocError` instead of aborting. **R6-F2** (Medium — silent partial success): if the gRPC stream ended with `active_shard = Some(_)` (Header sent, no Complete), the receive loop dropped the buffered shard and returned success. Extracted the post-loop check into `ensure_no_open_shard` and made it bail with a wire protocol error — same treatment as `FileData` without a preceding `FileHeader`. **R6-F3** (Medium — metadata desync): manual tar-shard writes weren't applying `header.mtime_seconds` or `header.permissions`, so a forced-gRPC pull of many small files succeeded byte-for-byte but left destination mtimes at "now", causing the next size+mtime sync to re-transfer everything. Now applies mtime via `filetime::set_file_mtime` when `> 0` and Unix permissions when nonzero, mirroring `FsTransferSink`. 3 new unit tests in `tar_shard_safety_tests` (rejects oversized per-entry size, rejects open shard at end-of-stream, preserves mtime). **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (158 lib tests + integration suites, all green).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1953:**2026-05-02 11:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 5 review findings R5-F1 through R5-F5 from `docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md`. **R5-F1** (High — data-loss vector): `delete_listed_paths` used a lexical `target.starts_with(dest_root)` check that lets `dest_root.join("../victim")` pass. Routed every wire-supplied path through `path_safety::safe_join`, switched `RemotePullReport::paths_to_delete` from `Vec<PathBuf>` to `Vec<String>` so wire input never gets blessed into a path before validation, and added 4 unit tests in `delete_list_safety_tests` covering parent-traversal, absolute paths, root-self-reference, and the happy path. **R5-F2** (High — wire-controlled symlink/hardlink creation): `apply_pull_tar_shard` was calling `Entry::unpack` which honors tar's symlink/hardlink/device entry types. Rewrote to reject anything that isn't `EntryType::Regular` or `EntryType::Continuous`, validate the path through `validate_wire_path`, copy bytes manually, and verify per-entry size matches the corresponding `FileHeader.size`. Added 4 unit tests including a hand-crafted malicious tar with `../escape.txt` in its header. **R5-F3** (Medium — DoS via huge `archive_size`): `Vec::with_capacity(header.archive_size as usize)` was a daemon-controlled allocation. Added `MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES = 256 MiB` cap, modest initial reservation (capped at 1 MiB), per-chunk overflow check against `declared_size` and the local cap, and a final length check on `TarShardComplete`. **R5-F4** (Low — comment lied about validation): `filter_from_spec` claimed to validate glob patterns but only copied them into `FileFilter` for later silent dropping. Now compiles each include/exclude pattern through `globset::Glob::new` at the spec normalizer; 2 new tests pin rejection of malformed brackets. **R5-F5** (Low — DEVLOG ordering): added this header and moved newest entries to the top so review workflow doesn't depend on knowing how to find appended-at-the-bottom changes. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (152+ lib tests, all integration suites green).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1955:**2026-05-02 09:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Step 4C — tar-shard support on the gRPC pull fallback. The push gRPC fallback runs the unified planner and emits TarShardHeader/Chunk/Complete for batched small-file workloads; the pull side previously sent files one-by-one via a hand-written loop. New `GrpcServerStreamingSink` (mirror of `GrpcFallbackSink`) in `blit-core/src/remote/transfer/sink.rs` lets the daemon's `stream_via_grpc` run `execute_sink_pipeline` with the same planner output as the TCP data plane. Proto adds three new ServerPullMessage variants (fields 13/14/15) reusing the existing TarShard message types. Client receive loop in `pull_sync` adds three match arms with an `InProgressShard` buffer and an `apply_pull_tar_shard` extractor (mirror of the daemon's push-side `apply_tar_shard_sync`). New integration test `test_pull_grpc_fallback_many_small_files` exercises 50 small files via `--force-grpc`. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (all green); `cargo test -p blit-cli --test remote_parity` (5 tests including the new one, all pass).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1957:**2026-05-02 08:45:00Z** - **ACTION**: Step 4B — closed F10 (filter parity on pull) and F4 (mirror deletion correctness). The daemon now builds a `FileFilter` from `spec.filter` and threads it into `collect_pull_entries_with_checksums` so source enumeration honors `--exclude/--include/--min-size/--max-size/--min-age/--max-age/--files-from` exactly as a push would. After diff, a new `scope_deletions(...)` helper applies `MirrorMode` + filter scope and produces the authoritative deletion list, which the daemon ships to the client via a new `ServerPullMessage::DeleteList` variant. The CLI replaces the prior dest-tree-walking `purge_extraneous_local` with `delete_listed_paths`, which executes exactly what the daemon said (with bounded-path safety). New `--delete-scope <subset|all>` flag selects between `MirrorMode::FilteredSubset` (default — out-of-scope files preserved) and `MirrorMode::All` (every absent file purged). 6 new unit tests for scope_deletions; 2 new integration tests in `remote_pull_mirror` covering FilteredSubset preservation and `--delete-scope all` purge behavior. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (all green); `cargo test -p blit-cli --test remote_pull_mirror` (3 integration tests, all pass).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1959:**2026-05-02 06:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed R2-F1, R4-F1, R4-F2, R4-F4 from `docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md`. (1) DiffPlanner's `local_needs_copy` now has concrete behavior for every `ComparisonMode` (SizeOnly, IgnoreTimes, Force, Checksum, SizeMtime/Unspecified) instead of silently falling through to size+mtime; 10 new per-mode tests. (2) Daemon PullSync handler routes through `NormalizedTransferOperation::from_spec` so spec_version mismatch and unknown enum values become hard `invalid_argument` errors at the wire boundary. (3) `ignore_existing` is now an orthogonal `bool` field on `TransferOperationSpec` (proto field 10; old enum value 5 reserved); `compare_mode = Force && ignore_existing` is rejected at the spec normalizer; CLI adds `conflicts_with = "force"` on `--ignore-existing`; orchestrator threads the flag through `LocalMirrorOptions → LocalDiffInputs` so local-local copies enforce it through the same planner as remote paths. (4) Refreshed stale proto comments referring to removed `PullSyncHeader` and the old "supports_filter_spec fallback" plan. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (all green, 144+ core tests).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1967:**2026-05-02 02:30:00Z** - **STEP 3a (DiffPlanner extraction, orchestrator migration)**: New `blit-core::remote::transfer::diff_planner` module consolidates the diff + payload-planning stage. Public API: `plan_local_mirror(headers, LocalDiffInputs) -> PlannedPayloads` is the single entry point the orchestrator's local-mirror path now calls; under the hood it composes `filter_unchanged` (the comparison-filter stage previously named `filter_headers_for_copy` and inlined in `orchestrator.rs`) with the existing `plan_transfer_payloads`. Translates the proto `ComparisonMode` enum onto the `Option<ChecksumType>` that the existing comparison primitives consume — `Checksum` enables Blake3, all other variants (`Unspecified`, `SizeMtime`, `SizeOnly`, `IgnoreTimes`, `IgnoreExisting`, `Force`) currently fall back to size+mtime. The non-`SizeMtime` variants are accepted at the API surface so callers can pass them through; their proper behavior arrives with step 4 (pull_sync.rs migration brings the comparison strategies that today live as `PullSyncHeader` bools). Orchestrator's local-mirror path migrated: the previous inline scan → spawn_blocking(filter_headers_for_copy) → plan_transfer_payloads sequence is now scan → spawn_blocking(plan_local_mirror) — same effect, single chokepoint, smaller call site. Dead `filter_headers_for_copy` removed. `ChecksumType` gained `PartialEq + Eq` so the diff_planner unit tests can assert mappings. **Push and pull origin paths NOT yet migrated** — those land in step 3b and step 4 respectively. **Tests**: 213 pass (+4 new diff_planner unit tests covering the ComparisonMode→ChecksumType mapping for every variant, and the filter_unchanged identity-vs-modified behavior), 0 failures. Behavior preservation verified by the unchanged orchestrator integration tests (admin verbs, remote_remote, remote_pull_mirror, remote_resume, remote_tcp_fallback all still pass).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1969:**2026-05-02 01:45:00Z** - **STEP 2 (operation spec contract)**: Added the canonical `TransferOperationSpec` message to `proto/blit.proto` along with `FilterSpec`, `ComparisonMode` enum, `MirrorMode` enum, `ResumeSettings`, `PeerCapabilities`. Per `docs/plan/PIPELINE_UNIFICATION.md` step 2: contract only, no behavior change yet. PushHeader and PullSyncHeader stay as the today-active control messages until the DiffPlanner extraction (step 3) and pull_sync.rs refactor (step 4) port to the new spec. Notable design choices: (1) `ComparisonMode` is an enum rather than the bool soup currently in PullSyncHeader (`size_only`, `ignore_times`, `ignore_existing`, `force`, `checksum` — five booleans that admit nonsensical combinations), (2) `MirrorMode` enum makes F4's filtered-mirror-delete-scope question explicit at the wire boundary (`OFF`, `FILTERED_SUBSET`, `ALL`), (3) `optional` proto3 scalars on `FilterSpec` size/age fields so callers can distinguish "not set" from "set to 0", (4) `PeerCapabilities` is additive — new capabilities default to `false` so a missing field on an old peer reads as "not supported." Re-export module `blit-core::remote::transfer::operation_spec` so callers don't reach into the generated `blit.v2` namespace directly. **Verification**: `cargo check --workspace` clean; `cargo test --workspace` 209 pass (unchanged); generated bindings include all six new types in the build artifact.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1971:**2026-05-02 01:15:00Z** - **F1 (path safety)**: Centralized receive-side path validation in `blit-core/src/path_safety.rs`. Two helpers: `validate_wire_path(s) -> PathBuf` (pure validation, normalized form) and `safe_join(root, s) -> PathBuf` (validate + join, with empty input returning root unchanged for the single-file destination case). Rejects: `..` components (not just substrings — the previous tar-shard `rel.contains("..")` check was too weak and also rejected legitimate filenames containing literal `..`), absolute paths, Windows drive prefixes (`C:\…`), UNC and DOS device paths (`\\?\…`, `\\server\share\…`), root components, embedded NUL bytes. Migrated `pull.rs::sanitize_relative_path` and daemon `service/util.rs::resolve_relative_path` / `resolve_manifest_relative_path` to thin wrappers that delegate to the shared module — single chokepoint, no drift between sanitizers. Applied at all five receive-side sites the review identified: `sink.rs:218` (streamed file write), `sink.rs:404` + `:426` (tar shard entry validation + per-entry join), `sink.rs:462` (resume block write), `sink.rs:498` (block completion), and the gRPC fallback block-hash path in `pull.rs:532`. Wire-boundary defense in depth: `pipeline.rs::read_file_header` and per-entry path read in `read_tar_shard` validate at the moment paths arrive off the socket, so unsafe headers never enter the FileHeader stream. **Tests**: 209 passed (was 185 baseline) — 16 new `path_safety::tests` covering the validator surface (`..`, absolute Unix, Windows drive, UNC, NUL, valid `..`-containing filenames, deep paths, Unicode, trailing slash, just-dot), 8 new sink-level integration tests confirming `FsTransferSink::write_file_stream` rejects malicious wire paths before any filesystem write and that the legitimate single-file empty-path case still works. **Verification**: `cargo check --workspace` clean; `cargo test --workspace` 0 failures.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1975:**2026-05-01 23:43:57Z** - **REVIEW**: Completed a repo-level codebase review and saved it to `docs/reviews/codebase_review_2026-05-01.md`. Highest-priority findings: receive-side path sanitization is incomplete across streamed files, tar shards, and resume block records; daemon `use_chroot` is documented/configured but not enforced; filtered mirror delete semantics need an explicit product decision; metrics lifecycle and endpoint behavior need hardening. Added a short `TODO.md` follow-up section and marked Phase 4 as in progress with the review artifact linked. **Verification**: `cargo check --workspace` passed; `cargo test --workspace` passed with 185 tests after rerunning outside the sandbox to allow local test port binding.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1989:**2026-04-07 16:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Completed blit-utils hardening (P1). Added 21 integration tests in `crates/blit-cli/tests/blit_utils.rs` covering all 9 blit-utils commands: scan (mDNS exit), list-modules (text+JSON), ls (remote/local/JSON), find (pattern/JSON/dirs-only/limit), du (text+JSON), df (text with human-readable check, JSON), rm (file/directory/module-root refusal), completions (prefix/dirs-only), profile (text+JSON). Added human-readable byte formatting to `df` output using `format_bytes()` alongside raw byte counts. **Tests**: `cargo test -p blit-cli --test blit_utils` (21 passed); `cargo test -p blit-cli --test admin_verbs` (10 passed).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2003:**2025-01-29 12:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Added resume integration tests (`remote_resume.rs`) covering partial file resume, identical file optimization, and gRPC fallback path. Security hardening: MAX_BLOCK_SIZE (64 MiB) enforced on both client and server to prevent OOM attacks from malicious block_size values. Export added to `copy/mod.rs`. Updated TODO.md to mark P0 parity tests and refactoring items as complete. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` (64 passed).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2007:**2025-01-28 16:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Implemented remote block-level resume for TCP data plane (primary path). Added `DATA_PLANE_RECORD_BLOCK` and `DATA_PLANE_RECORD_BLOCK_COMPLETE` record types to data plane protocol. Server-side: `stream_via_data_plane_resume` uses gRPC for block hash exchange then TCP data plane for block transfer - combines best of both worlds. Client-side: `handle_block_record` and `handle_block_complete_record` in data plane receiver handle seek+write for in-place block updates. Remote resume now works with default `--resume` flag (no `--force-grpc` needed). **Tests**: `cargo test -p blit-core` (63 passed).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2025:**2026-07-04 21:46:39Z** - **CODER (design-3-unbounded-data-plane-connects, claude)**: Landed design-3 through the codex loop (same session, fourth slice; coder's pick of the long-sanctioned smaller alternative over the large w9-3 harness consolidation — queue policy leaves sequencing to the coder). Both TCP data-plane client connects ran unbounded — the audit-2 wave bounded every control-plane connect at 30 s but never reached the data plane, so a firewalled/black-holed data port (the daemon advertises a fresh ephemeral port per transfer; asymmetric firewalls passing 9031 but blocking ephemerals are common) hung for the kernel SYN timeout (60–127 s) with no message. Sites re-verified at HEAD: the pull site is now `connect_pull_stream` (split at ue-r2-2, shared by resize-ADD dials), the push site `DataPlaneSession::connect_with_probe` (elastic dials included). Slice `49dcec6`: `remote::transfer::socket::dial_data_plane(addr, handshake, tcp_buffer_size)` — the client-side mirror of the daemon's bounded accept, in the w1-family policy module: connect bounded by the shared `DATA_PLANE_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT` (the row's sanctioned constant — no fifth 30 s literal), `configure_data_socket` applied, handshake write bounded by `DATA_PLANE_TOKEN_TIMEOUT` (mirrors the acceptor's bounded token read — the finding's "also the token write" clause); on either timeout the chain carries an `io::ErrorKind::TimedOut` source with text naming addr + the likely-firewall cause, so `remote::retry::is_retryable` classifies it transient and `--retry` re-dials. Both call sites collapsed onto the helper; socket.rs's w1-2-era "connect timeouts live at the call sites" module-doc paragraph rewritten (comment-truth). Tests +3 (blit-core 389 → 392): happy path (policy + handshake delivery), deterministic timeout SHAPE via an accepting-but-never-reading peer against a 64 MiB handshake (TimedOut chain + retryable — mutation-verified: swapping the timeout error for a plain eyre message fails the pin), TEST-NET black-hole connect bounded (environment-tolerant: fast-reject networks skip the shape assertions, the bound is asserted always). Review: codex **PASS, zero findings** (independently confirmed the pull resize-ADD non-fatal-dial posture survived and StallGuard/cancellation/byte accounting untouched). Validation: fmt/clippy clean, `cargo test --workspace` 1476 → 1479/0/2 across 37 suites. All on master, unpushed. Session total: w6-1 (+design-1), w6-2 (filed w6-2a/b/c), w4-4, design-3 — four rows closed, six commits of records. Next: w9-3 (test-harness builder) is the topmost ratified open row and the right size for a fresh session; filed alternatives w6-2a/b/c + relay-1.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2027:**2026-07-04 21:33:31Z** - **CODER (w4-4-blocking-work-off-runtime, claude)**: Landed w4-4 through the codex loop (same session, standing "reviewloop" go → topmost ratified open row after w6-1/w6-2). Both audit halves re-verified at HEAD before coding — the 2026-06-11 sites moved: daemon pull.rs died at ue-r2-1h, its enumeration relocated into pull_sync.rs; the push manifest loop's per-entry blocking is control.rs `file_requires_upload` (canonical-containment ancestor walk ≥2 canonicalize syscalls + stat, inline on a tokio worker — ~3M+ blocking syscalls for a 1M-file push). Slice `0feca34`: **(A)** manifest entries buffer into `PendingManifestEntry` and drain through `drain_manifest_checks` — ONE spawn_blocking per chunk runs the batch's checks (batch moved in/out, no clones), need-list pushes follow in async context in manifest order; `MANIFEST_CHECK_CHUNK` = FILE_LIST_EARLY_FLUSH_ENTRIES (128); the mid-manifest TCP data-plane spin-up moved from per-entry to post-chunk-drain (same guard — design-4's no-mid-manifest-fallback-negotiation invariant untouched); ManifestComplete drains the sub-chunk remainder (no spin-up — post-manifest negotiation owns it); the spec's lexical-containment alternative was REJECTED as weakening the F2 canonical posture (an in-module symlink could redirect the stat outside; check stays canonical, now on a blocking thread). **(B)** `collect_pull_entries_with_checksums` now runs entirely on one spawn_blocking thread (thin async wrapper + `collect_pull_entries_sync`; the directory branch's inner spawn_blocking unwrapped): previously the single-file branch ran its metadata probes and — with --checksum — a full synchronous Blake3 of an arbitrarily large file inline on a runtime worker, plus the top-level is_file/is_dir probes. Review: codex **NEEDS FIXES (1 Medium, accepted — a genuinely good catch)**: chunk-only draining muted the need-list batcher's 64 KiB/5 ms early-flush triggers between chunk boundaries (they only evaluate inside push(), which chunking confines to drain time) — a slowly-enumerating client's first FilesToUpload + TCP spin-up would wait for 128 trickled entries instead of ~5 ms; fixed `768e7e3` with `manifest_drain_due(pending_len, oldest_buffered)` — drain on chunk-full OR oldest-entry age ≥ MANIFEST_CHECK_MAX_DELAY (= the batcher's own delay bound), evaluated on next arrival exactly like the batcher's push-time semantics; fast streams still hit the cap first. Tests: +4 `manifest_check_batch_tests` (decision parity incl. POSIX wire paths + order, empty-drain no-op, containment-escape rejection through the batched path, trigger contract), mutation-verified (all-true check → parity pin fails); half B rides existing pins (single_file_filter_tests; the 500-file design-5 e2e drives ≥3 chunk drains + remainder + spin-up end-to-end). Validation both commits: fmt/clippy clean, `cargo test --workspace` 1472 → 1476/0/2 across 37 suites. All on master, unpushed. Next: strict design-queue order gives w9-3 (test-harness builder) as topmost ratified open row; filed alternatives w6-2a/b/c + design-3 + relay-1, coder's pick.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2031:**2026-07-04 21:02:20Z** - **CODER (w6-1-progress-event-contract, claude)**: Landed w6-1 through the codex loop (owner go: "continue. reviewloop with codex as each slice lands" → topmost open row per the 14th handoff). Slice `8fd8978`: contract defined ON the enum in blit-core — **bytes ride `Payload` only; `FileComplete` loses its `bytes` field entirely** (the design-1 class is now unrepresentable at the type level, not avoided by convention); files count exactly once via either one byteless `FileComplete{wire-relative path}` (per-file lane) or `Payload.files` deltas (aggregate lane: delegated bridge, gRPC tar-shard applier); `ManifestBatch` documented as the direction-flavored denominator (pull full-manifest / push need-list / delegated post-hoc — unification is wire/UX scope, deliberately not this slice). All producers normalized: receive FILE double-emit fixed; receive TAR_SHARD gains per-member completions; BLOCK/BLOCK_COMPLETE lanes gain `Payload`+`FileComplete` (TCP resume was progress-invisible); send-side sink worker (push TCP + gRPC fallback share it) moves planned sizes onto `Payload`; gRPC pull TarShardComplete counts via `Payload{stats.files,0}`, BlockComplete completes per-file, finalize carries the wire path through `active_file`; dead `send_payloads_with_progress` + `transfer_payloads_via_control_plane` conformed pending w8 deletion. Consumers collapsed onto new `ProgressTotals` (blit-core, next to the enum, `started()` gate included): CLI monitor rewritten (fixes design-1's ~2× `[progress]` lines; `--json` `file_complete` keeps its key shape with constant `bytes:0`; verbose gRPC-pull lines now print wire-relative paths) and all three TUI forwarders; the TUI's three `accumulate_*` rules deleted, its 7 tests rewritten in place against `ProgressTotals`. Tests +12 in blit-core (6 contract, 4 pipeline emission incl. exact-sequence pins driven over in-memory wire records via a new `RecordingSink`, 2 finalize) — the emission tests exposed that the fuzz helper `encode_block_complete` wrote truncated records (missing mtime/perms vs the reader), fixed; two mutation checks verified the pins bite (drop FileComplete counting → 3 fail; drop the FILE arm's `report_payload` → emission pin fails). .review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2033:**2026-07-04 20:04:30Z** - **CODER (w3-1-memory-aware-buffer-pool, claude)**: Landed w3-1 through the codex loop (owner go: "continue" → topmost open row per the 13th handoff; its "after W2.2 settles the tuning owner" prerequisite held — owner is `engine::TransferDial`, so the spec's `for_data_plane(tuning, streams)` became `for_data_plane(chunk_bytes, streams)`, the chunk read from the dial at each site). A 5-agent audit workflow re-verified the 2026-06-11 evidence against post-REV4 HEAD before coding: exactly three formula sites survive (push client, pull_sync multistream, pull_sync resume — the daemon pull.rs copy died with the Pull RPC at ue-r2-1h); the resume site is a hand-rolled `pool_size = 4` whose pool is **inert at runtime** (resume only sends via `send_block*`, which write caller slices — `pool.acquire` is reached solely from `send_file_double_buffered`), so unifying it to the formula's 6 is behavior-neutral; the double-buffered sender holds TWO pool buffers acquired sequentially (hold-one-wait-for-second), so any memory cap admitting fewer than 2 buffers per live stream can deadlock a sender against itself; shrinking pool buffers below the session's chunk_bytes is wire-safe (file bytes travel raw under a size-carrying header — no per-chunk framing; the effective send granularity already IS the pool buffer size); and `dial.ceiling_max_streams()` is a hard 3-layer-enforced bound on live streams under resize, with ADDed streams sharing the epoch-0 pool on both elastic paths. Slice `f49f8f6`: `BufferPool::for_data_plane` in buffer.rs backed by pure `data_plane_pool_params(chunk_bytes, streams, available_memory)` — formula unchanged when memory is plentiful (pinned pure-hoist parity), budget capped at available/4 (the pool's own doc example, ignored by every call site until now), liveness floor `budget ≥ buffer_size × streams × 2` always beats the cap with buffer_size shrinking toward the shared floor instead of concurrency; elastic paths authorize `ceiling_max_streams()` up front (lazy allocation makes the ceiling authorization free until resize ADDs streams — closes both sites' "growing the pool live is a W3.1 concern" deferral without live-growth machinery); resume passes 1. **Bonus real bug fixed**: the hoisted `available_memory_bytes()` drops the old helper's `* 1024` — sysinfo 0.38 returns BYTES not KiB (verified against the vendored crate source), so available memory was over-reported 1024×, which had made BufferSizer's /10 cap vacuous forever and would have made the new /4 cap vacuous too; also `System::new()`+`refresh_memory()` replaces the process-table-walking `new_all()`, zero-report 512 MiB fallback kept. `DATA_PLANE_BUFFER_FLOOR` (64 KiB) exported and adopted at the same-semantic floor sites (session chunk clamp, receive-buffer clamp, dial inflight clamp — whose comment already said "matching the session's minimum buffer"); coincidentally-equal 64 KiB literals deliberately untouched. Comment-truth: `RECEIVE_CHUNK_SIZE`'s false "matches the send side" claim rewritten (receive is deliberately 1 MiB vs the sender's 16–64 MiB; the asymmetry is legal — no per-chunk framing); BufferPool header example now shows the constructor. Tests: +8 params-layer pins (legacy parity, floor, cap, shrink-preserving-liveness, tiny-cap liveness override, full-grid liveness/floor property sweep, zero-memory fallback, real-sysinfo smoke), mutation-verified (cap+liveness line reverted → 3 pins fail; restored → green). Review: codex **PASS, zero findings** (it independently walked the two-buffer acquisition, the audit-11 exact-size guard, tar-shard chunking, and the ceiling authorization; first invocation was killed by a session restart before output — the record is the complete re-run). Known gaps in the finding doc: receive-side dial tuning stays out of scope (rest of constants-receive-chunk-1mib-asymmetry, separate slice if wanted); resume path's inert pool + dead prefetch literal left as-is; no memory-capped-host e2e (capped regimes pinned at the params layer; the pull-sync deadlock canary passes). Validation: fmt/clippy clean, `cargo test --workspace` 1460/0/2 across 37 suites (baseline 1452). All on master, unpushed. Next: w6-1 (progress-event contract) tops the open queue; design-3 remains the sanctioned smaller alternative.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2035:**2026-07-04 15:24:23Z** - **CODER (w2-2-stream-ladder-owner, claude)**: Landed w2-2 through the codex loop (owner go: "continue" → topmost open row per the 12th handoff). The row as filed (2026-06-11) predates REV4, which already delivered its three stream-count legs: the `determine_remote_tuning` ladder died at ue-r2-1e (live dial), daemon `desired_streams` at ue-r2-1f (`engine::initial_stream_proposal` — byte- AND file-count-keyed, satisfying the spec's "takes file_count"), and `pull_stream_count` with the Pull RPC at ue-r2-1h; D-2026-06-20-1 recorded the absorption in v1 slice IDs. The remaining leg — the transfer_plan 16/32 MiB chunk ladder — turned out to be **entirely dead policy**, established by a 5-agent audit workflow + hand verification: every remote path overrode it with `Some(dial.chunk_bytes())` (push client 5 refresh sites + ensure_dial, pull_sync both literals); the only paths where the ladder won (local engine, test callers) discarded the value (`PlanUpdate` carries payloads only); the single workspace read of `PlannedPayloads.chunk_bytes` sat behind a `chunk_bytes == 0` guard no live caller can trigger (all pass the dial value, floored ≥ 64 KiB). The spec's "make transfer_plan take chunk_bytes as input" predates the dial — with zero consumers, threading a value through the planner would be plumbing with no reader, so the honest single-owner outcome was deletion. Slice `01209bc`: ladder + `Plan` wrapper deleted (`build_plan` → `Vec<TransferTask>`); `PlannedPayloads` deleted (`plan_transfer_payloads` → `Result<Vec<TransferPayload>>`, ripple through diff_planner/streaming_plan/pipeline tests/re-exports); `PlanOptions.chunk_bytes_override` + all refresh sites deleted (push `plan_options` now immutable default; two arms keep bare `ensure_dial` calls — first-need creation and first-wins ceilings unchanged); unreachable fallback guard in `stream_fallback_from_queue` deleted; `plan_to_daemon_format` deleted (git log -S: never called in repo history — its "server pull mode" comment was never true); orphaned `TuningParams` deleted (producer died at ue-r2-1e); write-only kickoff histogram collapsed to the `total_bytes` accumulator that was its only read. Comment-truth sweep: dial.rs mutability-model doc no longer claims chunk/prefetch are "read at each use site" (consumers snapshot at session/pipeline/batch setup; steps reach epoch-N sockets and later fallback batches); buffer.rs example cites the dial, not `TuningParams`. Behavior byte-identical on every live path. Tests: +4 transfer_plan unit pins (module had zero) — tier classification/interleave, single-small-file no-tar, force_tar single-file, count-target shard splitting with the 128 clamp; deletions are compile-guarded (w2-1 evidence shape); zero tests deleted. Review: codex **NEEDS FIXES (1 Low)** — the first bare ensure_dial comment said "fallback batch" inside the `TransferMode::DataPlane` branch; accepted (mislabel sits exactly on the invariant under review), fixed `27f53a0` (one word). W3.1's "after W2.2 settles the tuning owner" prerequisite is now settled: the owner is `engine::TransferDial`. New discoveries → STATE Open questions: `725aa07` tracked a 236-file stale worktree snapshot (`.claude/worktrees/vigilant-mayer/`) into the repo; WHITEPAPER still describes the pre-dial tuning world (stale since ue-r2-1e, w10 territory). .review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2043:**2026-07-04 13:06:08Z** - **CODER (w1-3-tcp-keepalive-honesty, claude)**: Landed w1-3 through the codex loop (same session as w1-2, fourth slice under D-2026-07-04-1). The audit's finding: both `set_keepalive(true)` sites ran OS-default timing (~2 h idle before the first probe) while their comments claimed idle-stream liveness — overselling by two orders of magnitude. w1-2 had already collapsed both sites into the shared helper (and satisfied the "daemon copy logs failure" clause structurally — the daemon copy no longer exists), so this slice took the ratified row's real-timing exit at that single site (`865fc1e`): `TcpKeepalive` idle 60 s / interval 10 s / retries 5 via `set_tcp_keepalive` (which also enables SO_KEEPALIVE — the bare call is replaced, not supplemented; best-effort-logged posture unchanged). Records `894f5e8`. Review: codex **PASS, zero findings** — independently confirmed via socket2 source that Windows sets retries through `TCP_KEEPCNT`, that healthy-idle connections answer probes (no false-positive kill class), and via ci.yml that all three OS runners compile the feature surface; verdict recorded, no fix commit.
