.review/results/bench-script-fix.codex.md:10295:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1020:- **Foundation/local-copy stratum (added 2025-10-17): synchronous std::fs code, `test_`-prefixed test names, terse one-line doc comments, aspirational performance docs, homegrown helpers that later got globset/walkdir equivalents layered on top** — The original greenfield layer: blocking local-copy engine, enumerator, buffer sizing, perf history skeleton. It still carries vestiges of an even earlier 'Windows focus' restart (references to a deleted streaming_batch.rs). Sync, rayon/thread-based, no async. Several whole modules from this layer are now unreachable (see dead list). Its FileFilter runs TWO glob systems in sequence — compiled globset AND a hand-rolled `glob_match` — evidence that a newer author added globset without removing the old matcher.
.review/results/bench-script-fix.codex.md:10411:docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:99:**Mechanism**: rg for PermissionsExt|std::os::unix|symlink across blit-cli/tests hits only f2_chroot_containment.rs, remote_regression.rs, remote_push_mirror_safety.rs, and local_move_semantics.rs — the other eight gated files are pure process/fs/CLI tests. The harness already handles Windows binary names (common/mod.rs:94-104, 'blit.exe'/'blit-daemon.exe'). CI runs `cargo test --workspace` on windows-latest (ci.yml test-windows job) and the manual parity runner scripts/windows/run-blit-tests.ps1 also just runs cargo test, so both inherit the same hole: on Windows, mirror purge, push mirror safety, resume (block-level + gRPC fallback variant), TCP/gRPC fallback negotiation, checksum negotiation, and remote-to-remote delegation are compiled out. AGENTS.md §5 says 'Windows parity matters', yet the validation suite structurally cannot observe a Windows regression in any of those paths — a green Windows CI job is a false parity signal.
.review/results/bench-script-fix.codex.md:10468:docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:1110:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-cli/tests/remote_remote.rs:540 — bare Server::builder() for the fake unimplemented destination (second copy at :578)
.review/results/bench-script-fix.codex.md:10474:docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:1187:Checked and found clean: (1) Local mirror deletion semantics — orchestrator.rs has a substantial cfg(test) suite (lines 1300+, 1971+) including mirror_still_deletes_truly_unrelated_destination_dirs (:1616), mirror_refuses_when_source_scan_incomplete (:1448), local_mirror_all_scope_deletes_through_filter (:1753), compare-mode and ignore-existing tests; diff_planner.rs has 14 unit tests covering plan_local_mirror; local_move_semantics.rs covers move-vs-mirror regression (R46-F1). (2) Force-gRPC paths ARE integration-tested (contrary to the dimension brief's suspicion): remote_parity.rs test_pull_grpc_fallback/:92 and test_push_grpc_fallback/:133, remote_tcp_fallback.rs --force-grpc-data/:137 and --force-grpc/:168, remote_resume.rs gRPC-fallback resume/:91 — but ALL unix-gated (folded into the cfg(unix) finding). (3) Remote pull-mirror purge semantics well covered on unix (remote_pull_mirror.rs:46,:342,:404 cover purge, FilteredSubset preservation, delete-scope-all). (4) Push mirror safety F1/F2 (incomplete-scan refusal, filtered enumeration) covered in remote_push_mirror_safety.rs. (5) Daemon unit-test density is good: active_jobs.rs 29, delegation_gate.rs 31, core.rs 25, pull_sync.rs 6, push/data_plane.rs 13. (6) Checksum negotiation ack flow covered (remote_checksum_negotiation.rs). Notable non-finding per instructions: multi-stream pull is NOT a test gap — the live PullSync path hard-codes stream_count=1 (pull_sync.rs:568, :707) and the multi-stream machinery lives only in the deprecated Pull client (blit-core pull.rs:251/1600) which has zero callers outside its own file (rg verified) — that is a Phase B dead-code question, already adjacent to the map's 'deprecated pull client path' dead-weight list. Map claim correction: 'all remote integration tests are cfg(unix)' is overstated — remote_move.rs, remote_pull_subpath.rs, admin_verbs.rs, and blit_utils.rs spawn daemons ungated and run on Windows CI; my cfg(unix) finding is re-derived around the files that are gated without unix APIs. Not covered: blit-tui test quality (light-pass rule; it has many in-crate tests, e.g. main.rs:8736+ purge-safety assertions, left to Phase 6), blit-prometheus-bridge tests, the .ps1 journal/USN scripts' coverage, and proto-level compatibility tests (no buf/breaking-change gate exists, but I could not size that without speculating). I did not run any cargo commands (read-only constraint), so test counts are from source inspection, not execution.
.review/results/bench-script-fix.codex.md:10633:docs/audit/inventory/plan-principles.md:646:> Tri-platform CI: `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test --workspace` on Linux, macOS, Windows. Release builds + artifact upload.
.review/results/bench-script-fix.codex.md:11008:docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:453:- **CI tri-platform matrix and release artifact build** — `cargo test --workspace` runs on ubuntu/macos/windows; release builds gated to master. Test totals 407/0 as of recent commits.
.review/results/bench-script-fix.codex.md:11018:docs/audit/inventory/code-core-orch.md:33:- **fast-path-mirror-checksum-force-tar-disable** — `crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/fast_path.rs:81-94` — fast-path is disabled when `mirror || checksum || force_tar`, AND when `compare_mode != SizeMtime`. R58-F7 comment: fast-path's `should_copy_entry` only understands SizeMtime/Checksum, so `--size-only`/`--force`/`--ignore-times` previously silently became SizeMtime here.
.review/results/bench-script-fix.codex.md:11021:docs/audit/inventory/code-core-orch.md:104:- **compare-mode-bool-translation-orch** — `crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs:520-532, 1159-1171` — Match arm translates `LocalCompareMode` → `generated::ComparisonMode` in TWO places (streaming path orch:520 AND single-file path orch:1159). Both honor backward-compat `if options.checksum && SizeMtime → Checksum`. _(notes: duplicated 12-line translation — if a new variant is added it must be patched in both places + `snapshot_compare_mode` in history.rs:11-25 + tuning-window query at orch:467-487 = 4 sites.)_
.review/results/bench-script-fix.codex.md:11061:docs/audit/inventory/code-core-io.md:152:6. **`copy/compare.rs` has 3 nearly-identical needs-copy predicates** — `file_needs_copy`, `file_needs_copy_with_checksum_type`, `file_needs_copy_with_mode`. The first two use 2s mtime tolerance; the third (for `SizeMtime`) delegates to the second so it inherits the tolerance — but `manifest.rs::compare_file` Default mode has no tolerance at all. Within-cluster inconsistency.

docs/audit/inventory/code-tests-scripts.md:62:- **ci-three-os-matrix** — `.github/workflows/ci.yml:28-56` — Test job runs `cargo test --workspace` on `ubuntu-latest`, `macos-latest`, `windows-latest` separately; build-release is gated to `master` only. _(notes: no concurrency cap, no `actions/cache` beyond Swatinem; no fmt/clippy on macOS or Windows — only on ubuntu `check` job)_
docs/audit/inventory/code-tests-scripts.md:116:- **fake-server-trait-boilerplate-x2** — `crates/blit-cli/tests/remote_remote.rs:592-892` — `UnimplementedBlit` and `RejectingPullSyncBlit` both implement the full `Blit` trait (~150 lines each), returning `Status::unimplemented` for everything except the one method under test. _(notes: severe duplication; if a new `Blit` method is added, both fake impls + the SpyServer fake in blit-core must be edited)_
docs/audit/inventory/code-tests-scripts.md:178:3. **`UnimplementedBlit` fake trait stub is enormous** — `remote_remote.rs:592-726` is 134 lines of `Status::unimplemented` returns; `RejectingPullSyncBlit` (`:728-892`) is a near-duplicate 164 lines, and `SpyServer` in `pull_sync_with_spec_wire.rs:40-181` is a third 142-line copy. Adding any method to the `Blit` trait requires editing 3 fakes. A shared `tests/common/fake_blit.rs` with default impls + override hooks would shrink this 10×.
docs/audit/inventory/code-tests-scripts.md:184:9. **CI has no concurrency cancellation** — no `concurrency:` block; rapid pushes pile up runs. With three OS × full-workspace `cargo test` each, this is expensive.
docs/audit/inventory/code-tests-scripts.md:186:11. **`scripts/test.sh` is mislabeled** — it generates a Codex prompt bundle (sources `~/source/venvs/superclaude`, copies `~/.claude/commands/sc` → `~/.codex/commands/sg`); it has nothing to do with Blit tests. Anyone running `./scripts/test.sh` expecting `cargo test` gets a 220-line Codex installer. Should be renamed or moved out of `scripts/`.
docs/audit/inventory/code-tests-scripts.md:202:27. **CI runs full `cargo test --workspace` on Windows but `audit-6e` test is the only `ignore`'d one** — implies the CI matrix needs to keep growing as Windows-only bugs accumulate; no Windows-skip macro pattern documented.
docs/audit/inventory/code-tests-scripts.md:260:- CI matrix at `.github/workflows/ci.yml:28-56` runs `cargo test --workspace` on Windows, which depends on `audit-6e` being ignored. If a contributor removes the `cfg_attr(target_os = "windows", ignore = ...)` in `remote_move.rs:100` without fixing the underlying hang, CI will silently begin hanging on Windows because there is no per-job timeout.

docs/audit/findings/drift-cli.md:137:**Code does**: `Cargo.toml` workspace-level (`/Users/michael/Dev/Blit/Cargo.toml:1-20`) and per-crate (`crates/blit-cli/Cargo.toml`) do not pin `rust-version`. The build will succeed only on rustc versions that support tonic / clap-complete / let-else / async-trait / 2021-edition combinations that, in practice, require much newer Rust than 1.56. README is making a claim the build does not enforce.

   256	
   257	/// Per-role capability check of the operation a `SessionOpen`
   258	/// describes. otp-3 refuses what later slices implement rather than
   259	/// silently ignoring it (fail-fast; contract §Errors).
   260	type OpenValidator = dyn Fn(&SessionOpen) -> std::result::Result<(), SessionFault> + Send + Sync;
   261	
   262	/// The local endpoint a Responder resolves a received `SessionOpen`
   263	/// to. The daemon maps the wire module name + path here; a test can
   264	/// hand a fixed root with no module semantics via
   265	/// [`DestinationTarget::Fixed`] instead.
   266	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   267	pub struct ResolvedEndpoint {
   268	    /// Absolute local root this end targets.
   269	    pub root: PathBuf,
   270	    /// Whether the resolved module forbids writes. A DESTINATION
   271	    /// responder refuses `READ_ONLY`; a SOURCE responder (otp-5,
   272	    /// daemon-send) does not care — reading a read-only module is fine.
   273	    pub read_only: bool,
   274	}
   275	
   276	/// Async callback a Responder uses to turn a received (and
   277	/// capability-validated) `SessionOpen` into its local endpoint. It
   278	/// lives caller-side — the daemon resolves modules and maps its own
   279	/// `tonic::Status` errors to [`SessionFault`], so blit-core stays free
   280	/// of module/Status types. A returned fault (unknown module,
   281	/// containment failure) becomes a `SessionError` at OPEN, never a
   282	/// silent close (contract §Phase state machine).
   283	pub type OpenResolver = dyn Fn(
   284	        &SessionOpen,
   285	    )
   286	        -> Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = std::result::Result<ResolvedEndpoint, SessionFault>> + Send>>
   287	    + Send
   288	    + Sync;
   289	
   290	/// Where a DESTINATION driver writes. `Fixed` is a root known up front
   291	/// (an initiator's own local root, or a test's temp dir). `Resolve`
   292	/// defers to a caller callback that maps the received `SessionOpen` to
   293	/// a local root — the daemon path, where the root depends on the wire
   294	/// module name and so can only be resolved mid-handshake (after HELLO,
   295	/// before SessionAccept). A `Resolve` target is meaningful only on a
   296	/// Responder; an Initiator always knows its own root.
   297	pub enum DestinationTarget {
   298	    Fixed(PathBuf),
   299	    Resolve(Box<OpenResolver>),
   300	}
   301	
   302	fn source_open_validator(open: &SessionOpen) -> std::result::Result<(), SessionFault> {
   303	    if open.resume.as_ref().is_some_and(|r| r.enabled) {
   304	        return Err(SessionFault::internal(
   305	            "resume is not implemented on the unified session yet (otp-7)",
   306	        ));
   307	    }
   308	    if open
   309	        .filter
   310	        .as_ref()
   311	        .is_some_and(|f| *f != FilterSpec::default())
   312	    {
   313	        return Err(SessionFault::internal(
   314	            "filters are not implemented on the unified session yet (otp-6)",
   315	        ));
   316	    }
   317	    Ok(())
   318	}
   319	
   320	fn destination_open_validator(open: &SessionOpen) -> std::result::Result<(), SessionFault> {
   321	    if open.mirror_enabled {
   322	        return Err(SessionFault::internal(
   323	            "mirror is not implemented on the unified session yet (otp-6)",
   324	        ));
   325	    }
   326	    if open.resume.as_ref().is_some_and(|r| r.enabled) {
   327	        return Err(SessionFault::internal(
   328	            "resume is not implemented on the unified session yet (otp-7)",
   329	        ));
   330	    }
   331	    Ok(())
   332	}
   333	
   334	/// Outcome of the HELLO + OPEN phases.
   335	struct Negotiated {
   336	    open: SessionOpen,
   337	    #[allow(dead_code)] // capacity/grant consumed from otp-4b (data plane) on
   338	    accept: SessionAccept,
   339	    /// The write root a Responder's [`OpenResolver`] produced from the
   340	    /// received open, if one was supplied; `None` for an Initiator or a
   341	    /// fixed-root Responder (the caller supplies the root then).
   342	    resolved_root: Option<PathBuf>,
   343	}
   344	
   345	/// HELLO + OPEN/ACCEPT, one implementation both roles call (otp-3
   346	/// scoping requirement). Sends the refusal `SessionError` itself when
   347	/// it detects the fault locally; returned faults are `peer_notified`.
   348	async fn establish(
   349	    transport: &mut FrameTransport,
   350	    hello: &HelloConfig,
   351	    endpoint: &SessionEndpoint,
   352	    local_role: TransferRole,
   353	    validate_open: &OpenValidator,
   354	    // Consulted only on the Responder branch, after the received open
   355	    // passes `validate_open` and before SessionAccept. `None` = the
   356	    // caller supplies the root itself (Initiator, or fixed-root test).
   357	    resolve_open: Option<&OpenResolver>,
   358	) -> Result<Negotiated> {
   359	    // HELLO both ways, exact match (D-2026-07-05-2). First frame each
   360	    // direction; no ordering between the two directions.
   361	    transport
   362	        .send(frame(Frame::Hello(SessionHello {
   363	            build_id: hello.build_id.clone(),
   364	            contract_version: hello.contract_version,
   365	        })))
   366	        .await?;
   367	
   368	    let peer_hello = match expect_frame(transport).await? {
   369	        Frame::Hello(h) => h,
   370	        other => {
   371	            return Err(notify_and_wrap(
   372	                transport,
   373	                SessionFault::protocol_violation(format!(
   374	                    "expected SessionHello, got {}",
   375	                    frame_name(&Some(other))
   376	                )),
   377	            )
   378	            .await)
   379	        }
   380	    };
   381	
   382	    if peer_hello.build_id != hello.build_id
   383	        || peer_hello.contract_version != hello.contract_version
   384	    {
   385	        let fault = SessionFault {
   386	            code: session_error::Code::BuildMismatch,
   387	            message: format!(
   388	                "same-build peers required (D-2026-07-05-2): local {} (contract v{}) vs peer {} (contract v{})",
   389	                hello.build_id, hello.contract_version,
   390	                peer_hello.build_id, peer_hello.contract_version,
   391	            ),
   392	            local_build_id: hello.build_id.clone(),
   393	            peer_build_id: peer_hello.build_id.clone(),
   394	            peer_notified: false,
   395	        };
   396	        return Err(notify_and_wrap(transport, fault).await);
   397	    }
   398	
   399	    match endpoint {
   400	        SessionEndpoint::Initiator { open } => {
   401	            let open = open.as_ref().clone();
   402	            transport.send(frame(Frame::Open(open.clone()))).await?;
   403	            let accept = match expect_frame(transport).await? {
   404	                Frame::Accept(a) => a,
   405	                other => {
   406	                    return Err(notify_and_wrap(
   407	                        transport,
   408	                        SessionFault::protocol_violation(format!(
   409	                            "expected SessionAccept, got {}",
   410	                            frame_name(&Some(other))
   411	                        )),
   412	                    )
   413	                    .await)
   414	                }
   415	            };
   416	            Ok(Negotiated {
   417	                open,
   418	                accept,
   419	                resolved_root: None,
   420	            })
   421	        }
   422	        SessionEndpoint::Responder => {
   423	            let open = match expect_frame(transport).await? {
   424	                Frame::Open(o) => o,
   425	                other => {
   426	                    return Err(notify_and_wrap(
   427	                        transport,
   428	                        SessionFault::protocol_violation(format!(
   429	                            "expected SessionOpen, got {}",
   430	                            frame_name(&Some(other))
   431	                        )),
   432	                    )
   433	                    .await)
   434	                }
   435	            };
   436	            // The initiator declares ITS role; this responder end must
   437	            // hold the complement.
   438	            let declared =
   439	                TransferRole::try_from(open.initiator_role).unwrap_or(TransferRole::Unspecified);
   440	            if declared != complement(local_role) {
   441	                return Err(notify_and_wrap(
   442	                    transport,
   443	                    SessionFault::protocol_violation(format!(
   444	                        "initiator declared role {} but this responder is {}",
   445	                        declared.as_str_name(),
   446	                        local_role.as_str_name()
   447	                    )),
   448	                )
   449	                .await);
   450	            }
   451	            if let Err(fault) = validate_open(&open) {
   452	                // Refusal is a SessionError instead of SessionAccept,
   453	                // never a silent close (contract §Phase state machine).
   454	                return Err(notify_and_wrap(transport, fault).await);
   455	            }
   456	            // Responder endpoint resolution (otp-4): map the wire
   457	            // module/path to a local root and enforce read-only, both
   458	            // BEFORE SessionAccept so a refusal replaces the accept
   459	            // (never follows it). The resolver is caller-supplied
   460	            // (daemon module lookup); a fixed-root responder passes

   903	
   904	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   905	// DESTINATION driver
   906	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   907	
   908	/// What the destination end can report after a completed session.
   909	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   910	pub struct DestinationOutcome {
   911	    /// The summary this end computed and sent (contract: DESTINATION
   912	    /// is the scorer).
   913	    pub summary: TransferSummary,
   914	    /// Paths this end put on the need list, in emission order. The
   915	    /// role suite pins these identical across role assignments — the
   916	    /// executable form of the owner's invariance requirement.
   917	    pub needed_paths: Vec<String>,
   918	}
   919	
   920	/// Run the DESTINATION role of one transfer session over `transport`,
   921	/// writing under the root named by `target`. Diffs the streamed
   922	/// manifest against its own filesystem (the destination is the one
   923	/// diff owner — plan §Design 3), returns the summary it computed and
   924	/// sent.
   925	///
   926	/// `target` is [`DestinationTarget::Fixed`] when the root is known up
   927	/// front (an Initiator's own local root, or a test), or
   928	/// [`DestinationTarget::Resolve`] when the root must be resolved from
   929	/// the received `SessionOpen` mid-handshake (the daemon Responder,
   930	/// where the wire module name selects the root).
   931	pub async fn run_destination(
   932	    cfg: DestinationSessionConfig,
   933	    transport: FrameTransport,
   934	    target: DestinationTarget,
   935	) -> Result<DestinationOutcome> {
   936	    let mut transport = transport;
   937	    let endpoint = match cfg.endpoint {
   938	        SessionEndpoint::Initiator { mut open } => {
   939	            let declared = TransferRole::try_from(open.initiator_role);
   940	            if declared != Ok(TransferRole::Destination) {
   941	                eyre::bail!(
   942	                    "run_destination initiator must declare TRANSFER_ROLE_DESTINATION in SessionOpen"
   943	                );
   944	            }
   945	            if let Err(fault) = destination_open_validator(&open) {
   946	                eyre::bail!("run_destination initiator config unsupported: {fault}");
   947	            }
   948	            // Dial contract: the byte receiver advertises capacity in
   949	            // its open when it is the initiator (contract §Invariants 5).
   950	            if open.receiver_capacity.is_none() {
   951	                open.receiver_capacity = Some(crate::engine::local_receiver_capacity());
   952	            }
   953	            SessionEndpoint::Initiator { open }
   954	        }
   955	        SessionEndpoint::Responder => SessionEndpoint::Responder,
   956	    };
   957	
   958	    let resolve_open: Option<&OpenResolver> = match &target {
   959	        DestinationTarget::Resolve(resolver) => Some(resolver.as_ref()),
   960	        DestinationTarget::Fixed(_) => None,
   961	    };
   962	
   963	    let negotiated = establish(
   964	        &mut transport,
   965	        &cfg.hello,
   966	        &endpoint,
   967	        TransferRole::Destination,
   968	        &destination_open_validator,
   969	        resolve_open,
   970	    )
   971	    .await?;
   972	
   973	    // The resolver's root (Responder + Resolve) wins; otherwise the
   974	    // caller-supplied Fixed root.
   975	    let dst_root = match negotiated.resolved_root.clone() {
   976	        Some(root) => root,
   977	        None => match &target {
   978	            DestinationTarget::Fixed(root) => root.clone(),
   979	            // Unreachable: a Resolve target always yields a root on the
   980	            // Responder branch, and establish only skips resolution on
   981	            // the Initiator branch (which pairs with a Fixed root).
   982	            DestinationTarget::Resolve(_) => {
   983	                return Err(eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::internal(
   984	                    "resolver target produced no destination root",
   985	                )));
   986	            }
   987	        },
   988	    };
   989	
   990	    match destination_session(&mut transport, &negotiated, &dst_root).await {
   991	        Ok(outcome) => Ok(outcome),
   992	        Err(report) => {
   993	            let mut fault = fault_from_report(report);
   994	            if !fault.peer_notified {
   995	                let _ = transport.send(error_frame(&fault)).await;
   996	                fault.peer_notified = true;
   997	            }
   998	            Err(eyre::Report::new(fault))
   999	        }
  1000	    }
  1001	}
  1002	
  1003	fn violation(message: String) -> eyre::Report {
  1004	    eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::protocol_violation(message))
  1005	}
  1006	
  1007	async fn destination_session(
  1008	    transport: &mut FrameTransport,
  1009	    negotiated: &Negotiated,
  1010	    dst_root: &Path,
  1011	) -> Result<DestinationOutcome> {
  1012	    let compare_mode = ComparisonMode::try_from(negotiated.open.compare_mode)
  1013	        .unwrap_or(ComparisonMode::Unspecified);
  1014	    let compare_opts = CompareOptions {
  1015	        mode: compare_mode.into(),
  1016	        ignore_existing: negotiated.open.ignore_existing,
  1017	        include_deletions: false, // mirror lands at otp-6
  1018	    };
  1019	    // src_root is only consumed by local File payloads, which never
  1020	    // occur on a session destination (payload bytes arrive as records
  1021	    // and go through the stream/tar write paths).
  1022	    let sink = FsTransferSink::new(
  1023	        PathBuf::new(),
  1024	        dst_root.to_path_buf(),
  1025	        FsSinkConfig {
  1026	            preserve_times: true,
  1027	            dry_run: false,
  1028	            checksum: None,
  1029	            resume: false,
  1030	            compare_mode,

 succeeded in 0ms:

   702	                )));

   708	
   709	async fn source_send_half(
   710	    cfg: &SourceSessionConfig,
   711	    negotiated: &Negotiated,
   712	    tx: &mut Box<dyn FrameTx>,
   713	    source: Arc<dyn TransferSource>,
   714	    sent: Arc<StdMutex<HashMap<String, FileHeader>>>,
   715	    manifest_sent: &AtomicBool,
   716	    mut events: mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<SourceEvent>,
   717	) -> Result<TransferSummary> {
   718	    let mut pending: Vec<FileHeader> = Vec::new();
   719	    let mut need_complete = false;
   720	
   721	    // Streaming manifest: entries go out as enumeration produces them
   722	    // (immediate start in every direction — plan §Design 2). The open
   723	    // carries no source path: the source end owns its local endpoint.
   724	    let _ = &negotiated.open;
   725	    let unreadable: Arc<StdMutex<Vec<String>>> = Arc::default();
   726	    let (mut header_rx, scan_handle) = source.scan(None, Arc::clone(&unreadable));
   727	    while let Some(header) = header_rx.recv().await {
   728	        sent.lock()
   729	            .expect("sent-manifest lock poisoned")
   730	            .insert(header.relative_path.clone(), header.clone());
   731	        tx.send(frame(Frame::ManifestEntry(header))).await?;
   732	        // Faults detected by the receive half abort the stream now,
   733	        // not after the full scan; needs just accumulate.
   734	        drain_source_events(&mut events, &mut pending, &mut need_complete)?;
   735	    }
   736	    let scanned = scan_handle
   737	        .await
   738	        .map_err(|err| eyre::eyre!("manifest scan task panicked: {err}"))??;
   739	    let scan_complete = unreadable
   740	        .lock()
   741	        .expect("unreadable list lock poisoned")
   742	        .is_empty();
   743	    log::debug!("session source manifest complete: {scanned} entries, complete={scan_complete}");
   744	    tx.send(frame(Frame::ManifestComplete(ManifestComplete {
   745	        scan_complete,
   746	    })))
   747	    .await?;
   748	    manifest_sent.store(true, Ordering::Release);
   749	
   750	    // Payload phase. In-stream record grammar: payload records only
   751	    // after ManifestComplete, strictly serialized per record
   752	    // (contract §Transport selection). Needs accumulated while a
   753	    // record batch was being sent become the next planner batch.
   754	    let mut read_buf = vec![0u8; IN_STREAM_CHUNK];
   755	    loop {
   756	        drain_source_events(&mut events, &mut pending, &mut need_complete)?;
   757	        if !pending.is_empty() {
   758	            let batch = std::mem::take(&mut pending);
   759	            send_payload_records(tx, &source, cfg.plan_options, batch, &mut read_buf).await?;
   760	            continue;
   761	        }
   762	        if need_complete {
   763	            break;
   764	        }
   765	        match events.recv().await {
   766	            Some(event) => {
   767	                handle_source_event(event, &mut pending, &mut need_complete)?;
   768	            }
   769	            None => {
   770	                return Err(eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::internal(
   771	                    "source receive half ended before NeedComplete",
   772	                )))
   773	            }
   774	        }
   775	    }
   776	
   777	    tx.send(frame(Frame::SourceDone(SourceDone {}))).await?;
   778	
   779	    // CLOSING: the destination is the scorer; the next event must be
   780	    // its summary (the receive half ends after forwarding it).
   781	    match events.recv().await {
   782	        Some(SourceEvent::Summary(summary)) => Ok(summary),
   783	        Some(SourceEvent::Fault(fault)) => Err(eyre::Report::new(fault)),
   784	        Some(SourceEvent::Need(h)) => Err(eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::protocol_violation(
   785	            format!("need for '{}' after NeedComplete", h.relative_path),
   786	        ))),
   787	        Some(SourceEvent::NeedComplete) => Err(eyre::Report::new(
   788	            SessionFault::protocol_violation("duplicate NeedComplete"),
   789	        )),
   790	        None => Err(eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::internal(
   791	            "source receive half ended before TransferSummary",
   792	        ))),
   793	    }
   794	}
   795	
   796	fn drain_source_events(
   797	    events: &mut mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<SourceEvent>,
   798	    pending: &mut Vec<FileHeader>,
   799	    need_complete: &mut bool,
   800	) -> Result<()> {
   801	    while let Ok(event) = events.try_recv() {
   802	        handle_source_event(event, pending, need_complete)?;
   803	    }
   804	    Ok(())
   805	}
   806	
   807	fn handle_source_event(
   808	    event: SourceEvent,
   809	    pending: &mut Vec<FileHeader>,
   810	    need_complete: &mut bool,
   811	) -> Result<()> {
   812	    match event {
   813	        SourceEvent::Need(header) => {
   814	            if *need_complete {
   815	                return Err(eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::protocol_violation(
   816	                    format!("need for '{}' after NeedComplete", header.relative_path),
   817	                )));
   818	            }
   819	            pending.push(header);
   820	            Ok(())
   821	        }
   822	        SourceEvent::NeedComplete => {
   823	            if *need_complete {
   824	                return Err(eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::protocol_violation(
   825	                    "duplicate NeedComplete",
   826	                )));
   827	            }
   828	            *need_complete = true;
   829	            Ok(())
   830	        }
   831	        SourceEvent::Summary(_) => Err(eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::protocol_violation(
   832	            "TransferSummary before SourceDone",
   833	        ))),
   834	        SourceEvent::Fault(fault) => Err(eyre::Report::new(fault)),
   835	    }
   836	}
   837	
   838	/// Plan one batch of needed headers with the engine planner and emit
   839	/// the resulting payload records per the in-stream grammar.
   840	async fn send_payload_records(
   841	    tx: &mut Box<dyn FrameTx>,
   842	    source: &Arc<dyn TransferSource>,
   843	    plan_options: PlanOptions,
   844	    batch: Vec<FileHeader>,
   845	    read_buf: &mut [u8],
   846	) -> Result<()> {
   847	    let payloads = diff_planner::plan_push_payloads(batch, source.root(), plan_options)?;
   848	    for payload in payloads {
   849	        match source.prepare_payload(payload).await? {
   850	            PreparedPayload::File(header) => {
   851	                tx.send(frame(Frame::FileBegin(header.clone()))).await?;
   852	                if header.size == 0 {
   853	                    continue; // record complete at 0 cumulative bytes
   854	                }
   855	                let mut reader = source.open_file(&header).await?;
   856	                let mut remaining = header.size;
   857	                while remaining > 0 {
   858	                    let want = read_buf.len().min(remaining as usize);
   859	                    let got = reader.read(&mut read_buf[..want]).await?;
   860	                    if got == 0 {
   861	                        // Shorter on disk than the manifest promised —
   862	                        // the record can no longer complete at
   863	                        // header.size; abort rather than pad.
   864	                        eyre::bail!(
   865	                            "'{}' hit EOF with {} bytes still promised",
   866	                            header.relative_path,
   867	                            remaining
   868	                        );
   869	                    }
   870	                    tx.send(frame(Frame::FileData(FileData {
   871	                        content: read_buf[..got].to_vec(),
   872	                    })))
   873	                    .await?;
   874	                    remaining -= got as u64;
   875	                }
   876	            }
   877	            PreparedPayload::TarShard { headers, data } => {
   878	                tx.send(frame(Frame::TarShardHeader(TarShardHeader {
   879	                    files: headers,
   880	                    archive_size: data.len() as u64,
   881	                })))
   882	                .await?;
   883	                for chunk in data.chunks(IN_STREAM_CHUNK) {
   884	                    tx.send(frame(Frame::TarShardChunk(
   885	                        crate::generated::TarShardChunk {
   886	                            content: chunk.to_vec(),
   887	                        },
   888	                    )))
   889	                    .await?;
   890	                }
   891	                tx.send(frame(Frame::TarShardComplete(TarShardComplete {})))
   892	                    .await?;
   893	            }
   894	            PreparedPayload::FileBlock { .. } | PreparedPayload::FileBlockComplete { .. } => {
   895	                // The outbound planner never emits these (resume is
   896	                // receive-originated and lands at otp-7).
   897	                eyre::bail!("resume payload planned in a non-resume session");
   898	            }
   899	        }
   900	    }

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! Frame transports for the unified transfer session.
     2	//!
     3	//! The session drivers in this module's parent speak
     4	//! [`TransferFrame`]s through the `FrameTx`/`FrameRx` halves and never
     5	//! know what carries them. otp-3 ships the in-process pair below;
     6	//! otp-4 adds a gRPC-backed implementation over the `Transfer` RPC
     7	//! (transport substitution, not new choreography —
     8	//! docs/TRANSFER_SESSION.md); otp-11 reuses the in-process pair for
     9	//! local transfers.
    10	
    11	use async_trait::async_trait;
    12	use eyre::{eyre, Result};
    13	use tokio::sync::mpsc;
    14	use tonic::{Status, Streaming};
    15	
    16	use crate::generated::TransferFrame;
    17	
    18	/// Sending half of a frame transport. `send` applies the transport's
    19	/// own backpressure (bounded channel here, HTTP/2 flow control on the
    20	/// wire) — the session contract deliberately leans on it instead of
    21	/// buffering (docs/TRANSFER_SESSION.md §Phase state machine).
    22	#[async_trait]
    23	pub trait FrameTx: Send {
    24	    async fn send(&mut self, frame: TransferFrame) -> Result<()>;
    25	}
    26	
    27	/// Receiving half of a frame transport. `Ok(None)` means the peer
    28	/// closed the stream cleanly; transport-level failures are `Err`.
    29	#[async_trait]
    30	pub trait FrameRx: Send {
    31	    async fn recv(&mut self) -> Result<Option<TransferFrame>>;
    32	}
    33	
    34	/// One endpoint's bidirectional frame stream, splittable so a driver
    35	/// can run its send and receive halves concurrently (the source
    36	/// driver must keep draining need batches while it streams manifest
    37	/// entries, or a full channel in each direction deadlocks the pair).
    38	pub struct FrameTransport {
    39	    tx: Box<dyn FrameTx>,
    40	    rx: Box<dyn FrameRx>,
    41	}
    42	
    43	impl FrameTransport {
    44	    pub fn new(tx: Box<dyn FrameTx>, rx: Box<dyn FrameRx>) -> Self {
    45	        Self { tx, rx }
    46	    }
    47	
    48	    pub async fn send(&mut self, frame: TransferFrame) -> Result<()> {
    49	        self.tx.send(frame).await
    50	    }
    51	
    52	    pub async fn recv(&mut self) -> Result<Option<TransferFrame>> {
    53	        self.rx.recv().await
    54	    }
    55	
    56	    pub fn split(self) -> (Box<dyn FrameTx>, Box<dyn FrameRx>) {
    57	        (self.tx, self.rx)
    58	    }
    59	}
    60	
    61	/// Bounded per-direction capacity of the in-process pair. Small on
    62	/// purpose: the session must stay live under transport backpressure
    63	/// (both drivers are exercised against it in the role suite), and a
    64	/// deep channel would only hide ordering bugs the wire will expose.
    65	pub const IN_PROCESS_CHANNEL_FRAMES: usize = 64;
    66	
    67	struct MpscFrameTx {
    68	    tx: mpsc::Sender<TransferFrame>,
    69	}
    70	
    71	#[async_trait]
    72	impl FrameTx for MpscFrameTx {
    73	    async fn send(&mut self, frame: TransferFrame) -> Result<()> {
    74	        self.tx
    75	            .send(frame)
    76	            .await
    77	            .map_err(|_| eyre!("in-process transport peer closed"))
    78	    }
    79	}
    80	
    81	struct MpscFrameRx {
    82	    rx: mpsc::Receiver<TransferFrame>,
    83	}
    84	
    85	#[async_trait]
    86	impl FrameRx for MpscFrameRx {
    87	    async fn recv(&mut self) -> Result<Option<TransferFrame>> {
    88	        Ok(self.rx.recv().await)
    89	    }
    90	}
    91	
    92	/// Two connected in-process endpoints: what one sends, the other
    93	/// receives. Both roles of a local transfer (otp-11) — and every
    94	/// otp-3 test — run over this pair.
    95	pub fn in_process_pair() -> (FrameTransport, FrameTransport) {
    96	    let (a_tx, b_rx) = mpsc::channel(IN_PROCESS_CHANNEL_FRAMES);
    97	    let (b_tx, a_rx) = mpsc::channel(IN_PROCESS_CHANNEL_FRAMES);
    98	    (
    99	        FrameTransport::new(
   100	            Box::new(MpscFrameTx { tx: a_tx }),
   101	            Box::new(MpscFrameRx { rx: a_rx }),
   102	        ),
   103	        FrameTransport::new(
   104	            Box::new(MpscFrameTx { tx: b_tx }),
   105	            Box::new(MpscFrameRx { rx: b_rx }),
   106	        ),
   107	    )
   108	}
   109	
   110	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   111	// gRPC-backed transport (otp-4)
   112	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   113	//
   114	// The unified `Transfer` RPC is bidi-streaming and carries `TransferFrame`
   115	// both directions, so a session end reads its inbound `tonic::Streaming`
   116	// as a `FrameRx` and writes its outbound frames through an mpsc whose
   117	// receiver becomes the peer's stream. Two `FrameTx` impls exist only
   118	// because the client's outbound stream item is a bare `TransferFrame`
   119	// (what `BlitClient::transfer` wants) while the daemon's outbound item is
   120	// `Result<TransferFrame, Status>` (what its `ReceiverStream` response
   121	// yields). Session-level refusals travel as `SessionError` *frames*
   122	// (`Ok`); the gRPC `Status` channel is reserved for terminal transport
   123	// faults.
   124	
   125	/// Inbound half over a tonic server/response stream — shared by both
   126	/// ends (client reads the response stream, daemon reads the request
   127	/// stream). `message()`'s `Ok(None)`/`Err(Status)` map directly onto the
   128	/// `FrameRx` clean-close / transport-error contract.
   129	struct GrpcFrameRx {
   130	    stream: Streaming<TransferFrame>,
   131	}
   132	
   133	#[async_trait]
   134	impl FrameRx for GrpcFrameRx {
   135	    async fn recv(&mut self) -> Result<Option<TransferFrame>> {
   136	        self.stream
   137	            .message()
   138	            .await
   139	            // Preserve the gRPC code in the message — the FrameRx trait
   140	            // carries only eyre, and the session's own SessionError
   141	            // frames are the semantic error channel; this Err is a raw
   142	            // transport failure the drivers surface as INTERNAL.
   143	            .map_err(|status| {
   144	                eyre!(
   145	                    "gRPC transport error ({:?}): {}",
   146	                    status.code(),
   147	                    status.message()
   148	                )
   149	            })
   150	    }
   151	}
   152	
   153	/// Client outbound half: frames go into the mpsc whose `ReceiverStream`
   154	/// was handed to `BlitClient::transfer` as the request stream.
   155	struct GrpcClientFrameTx {
   156	    tx: mpsc::Sender<TransferFrame>,
   157	}
   158	
   159	#[async_trait]
   160	impl FrameTx for GrpcClientFrameTx {
   161	    async fn send(&mut self, frame: TransferFrame) -> Result<()> {
   162	        self.tx
   163	            .send(frame)
   164	            .await
   165	            .map_err(|_| eyre!("transfer transport peer closed"))
   166	    }
   167	}
   168	
   169	/// Daemon outbound half: the session's frames ride the RPC response
   170	/// stream, whose item type is `Result<TransferFrame, Status>`. Frames
   171	/// are always `Ok` — the `Status` variant is used by the handler for a
   172	/// terminal transport fault, not by the session.
   173	struct GrpcDaemonFrameTx {
   174	    tx: mpsc::Sender<Result<TransferFrame, Status>>,
   175	}
   176	
   177	#[async_trait]
   178	impl FrameTx for GrpcDaemonFrameTx {
   179	    async fn send(&mut self, frame: TransferFrame) -> Result<()> {
   180	        self.tx
   181	            .send(Ok(frame))
   182	            .await
   183	            .map_err(|_| eyre!("transfer transport peer closed"))
   184	    }
   185	}
   186	
   187	/// Bounded capacity of the mpsc feeding a gRPC stream direction — the
   188	/// session leans on transport backpressure (this channel plus HTTP/2
   189	/// flow control), matching the push handler's `mpsc::channel(32)`.
   190	pub const GRPC_CHANNEL_FRAMES: usize = 32;
   191	
   192	/// Assemble the CLIENT end's transport: `out_tx` feeds the request
   193	/// stream (build it with `ReceiverStream::new(out_rx)` and hand that to
   194	/// `BlitClient::transfer`), `inbound` is the response stream.
   195	pub fn grpc_client_transport(
   196	    out_tx: mpsc::Sender<TransferFrame>,
   197	    inbound: Streaming<TransferFrame>,
   198	) -> FrameTransport {
   199	    FrameTransport::new(
   200	        Box::new(GrpcClientFrameTx { tx: out_tx }),
   201	        Box::new(GrpcFrameRx { stream: inbound }),
   202	    )
   203	}
   204	
   205	/// Assemble the DAEMON end's transport: `out_tx` feeds the RPC response
   206	/// `ReceiverStream`, `inbound` is the request stream
   207	/// (`request.into_inner()`).
   208	pub fn grpc_daemon_transport(
   209	    out_tx: mpsc::Sender<Result<TransferFrame, Status>>,
   210	    inbound: Streaming<TransferFrame>,
   211	) -> FrameTransport {
   212	    FrameTransport::new(
   213	        Box::new(GrpcDaemonFrameTx { tx: out_tx }),
   214	        Box::new(GrpcFrameRx { stream: inbound }),
   215	    )
   216	}
   217	
   218	#[cfg(test)]
   219	mod tests {
   220	    use super::*;
   221	    use crate::generated::{transfer_frame, SourceDone};
   222	
   223	    fn source_done_frame() -> TransferFrame {
   224	        TransferFrame {
   225	            frame: Some(transfer_frame::Frame::SourceDone(SourceDone {})),
   226	        }
   227	    }
   228	
   229	    #[tokio::test]
   230	    async fn pair_delivers_frames_both_directions() {
   231	        let (mut a, mut b) = in_process_pair();
   232	        a.send(source_done_frame()).await.unwrap();
   233	        b.send(source_done_frame()).await.unwrap();
   234	        assert!(matches!(
   235	            b.recv().await.unwrap().unwrap().frame,
   236	            Some(transfer_frame::Frame::SourceDone(_))
   237	        ));
   238	        assert!(matches!(
   239	            a.recv().await.unwrap().unwrap().frame,
   240	            Some(transfer_frame::Frame::SourceDone(_))
   241	        ));
   242	    }
   243	
   244	    #[tokio::test]
   245	    async fn dropped_peer_reads_as_clean_close_and_send_error() {
   246	        let (mut a, b) = in_process_pair();
   247	        drop(b);
   248	        assert!(a.recv().await.unwrap().is_none(), "closed peer = Ok(None)");
   249	        assert!(a.send(source_done_frame()).await.is_err());
   250	    }
   251	}

     1	//! Client-side entry for initiating a unified transfer session as the
     2	//! SOURCE role (otp-4a).
     3	//!
     4	//! Builds a gRPC-backed [`FrameTransport`] over `BlitClient::transfer`
     5	//! and runs [`run_source`], so a CLI push becomes "open the Transfer
     6	//! RPC, declare SOURCE, stream the manifest + payloads." This is the
     7	//! push-equivalent on the unified path; the daemon answers by running
     8	//! `run_destination` as the Responder.
     9	//!
    10	//! Not yet wired to CLI verbs — the verbs keep riding the old push
    11	//! path until the otp-10 cutover; today the parity tests drive this.
    12	//! otp-4a uses the in-stream byte carrier only (`in_stream_bytes`);
    13	//! the TCP data plane lands at otp-4b.
    14	
    15	use std::sync::Arc;
    16	use std::time::Duration;
    17	
    18	use eyre::{eyre, Result};
    19	use tokio::sync::mpsc;
    20	use tokio_stream::wrappers::ReceiverStream;
    21	use tonic::transport::{Channel, Endpoint};
    22	
    23	use crate::generated::blit_client::BlitClient;
    24	use crate::generated::{ComparisonMode, SessionOpen, TransferRole, TransferSummary};
    25	use crate::remote::endpoint::{RemoteEndpoint, RemotePath};
    26	use crate::remote::transfer::source::TransferSource;
    27	use crate::transfer_plan::PlanOptions;
    28	use crate::transfer_session::transport::{grpc_client_transport, GRPC_CHANNEL_FRAMES};
    29	use crate::transfer_session::{run_source, HelloConfig, SessionEndpoint, SourceSessionConfig};
    30	
    31	/// The push-shaped subset of session options otp-4a supports. Mirror,
    32	/// filters, and resume are refused at OPEN until their slices land
    33	/// (otp-6/otp-7), so they are intentionally absent here.
    34	pub struct PushSessionOptions {
    35	    pub compare_mode: ComparisonMode,
    36	    pub ignore_existing: bool,
    37	    pub require_complete_scan: bool,
    38	    pub plan_options: PlanOptions,
    39	}
    40	
    41	impl Default for PushSessionOptions {
    42	    fn default() -> Self {
    43	        Self {
    44	            compare_mode: ComparisonMode::SizeMtime,
    45	            ignore_existing: false,
    46	            require_complete_scan: false,
    47	            plan_options: PlanOptions::default(),
    48	        }
    49	    }
    50	}
    51	
    52	/// Connect to `endpoint`'s daemon and run one SOURCE-role transfer
    53	/// session pushing `source`'s tree into the endpoint's module/path.
    54	/// Returns the destination-computed [`TransferSummary`] (contract:
    55	/// DESTINATION is the scorer).
    56	pub async fn run_push_session(
    57	    endpoint: &RemoteEndpoint,
    58	    source: Arc<dyn TransferSource>,
    59	    options: PushSessionOptions,
    60	) -> Result<TransferSummary> {
    61	    // The responder resolves module→root; the initiator's own local
    62	    // path never crosses the wire (contract §SessionOpen). Empty module
    63	    // targets the daemon's default root export.
    64	    let (module, path) = match &endpoint.path {
    65	        RemotePath::Module { module, rel_path } => {
    66	            (module.clone(), rel_path.to_string_lossy().into_owned())
    67	        }
    68	        RemotePath::Root { rel_path } => (String::new(), rel_path.to_string_lossy().into_owned()),
    69	        RemotePath::Discovery => {
    70	            return Err(eyre!(
    71	                "a transfer session needs a resolved module or root endpoint, not a discovery form"
    72	            ));
    73	        }
    74	    };
    75	
    76	    let mut client = connect_transfer_client(endpoint).await?;
    77	
    78	    let open = SessionOpen {
    79	        initiator_role: TransferRole::Source as i32,
    80	        module,
    81	        path,
    82	        compare_mode: options.compare_mode as i32,
    83	        ignore_existing: options.ignore_existing,
    84	        require_complete_scan: options.require_complete_scan,
    85	        // otp-4a: in-stream byte carrier only; the TCP data plane
    86	        // grant lands at otp-4b.
    87	        in_stream_bytes: true,
    88	        ..Default::default()
    89	    };
    90	
    91	    // Open the bidi RPC: the request stream is fed by `out_tx`, the
    92	    // response stream is the inbound half. The handler returns its
    93	    // response stream immediately (it spawns the session), so this
    94	    // await resolves before any frame flows — no deadlock.
    95	    let (out_tx, out_rx) = mpsc::channel(GRPC_CHANNEL_FRAMES);
    96	    let inbound = client
    97	        .transfer(ReceiverStream::new(out_rx))
    98	        .await
    99	        .map_err(|status| eyre!("opening Transfer RPC: {}", status.message()))?
   100	        .into_inner();
   101	    let transport = grpc_client_transport(out_tx, inbound);
   102	
   103	    let cfg = SourceSessionConfig {
   104	        hello: HelloConfig::default(),
   105	        endpoint: SessionEndpoint::initiator(open),
   106	        plan_options: options.plan_options,
   107	    };
   108	    run_source(cfg, transport, source).await
   109	}
   110	
   111	/// Build a `BlitClient` over `endpoint`'s control-plane URI with the
   112	/// same bounded-connect policy `RemotePushClient::connect` uses.
   113	async fn connect_transfer_client(endpoint: &RemoteEndpoint) -> Result<BlitClient<Channel>> {
   114	    let uri = endpoint.control_plane_uri();
   115	    let conn = Endpoint::from_shared(uri.clone())
   116	        .map_err(|e| eyre!("invalid endpoint uri {uri}: {e}"))?
   117	        .connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(30));
   118	    let channel = tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(30), conn.connect())
   119	        .await
   120	        .map_err(|_| eyre!("timed out connecting to {uri}"))?
   121	        .map_err(|e| eyre!("connecting to {uri}: {e}"))?;
   122	    Ok(BlitClient::new(channel))
   123	}

   456	            // Responder endpoint resolution (otp-4): map the wire
   457	            // module/path to a local root and enforce read-only, both
   458	            // BEFORE SessionAccept so a refusal replaces the accept
   459	            // (never follows it). The resolver is caller-supplied
   460	            // (daemon module lookup); a fixed-root responder passes
   461	            // None and resolves nothing here.
   462	            let resolved_root = match resolve_open {
   463	                Some(resolve) => match resolve(&open).await {
   464	                    Ok(resolved) => {
   465	                        // A read-only module is fatal only for a
   466	                        // DESTINATION (it would write); a SOURCE
   467	                        // responder (otp-5, daemon-send) reads happily.
   468	                        if local_role == TransferRole::Destination && resolved.read_only {
   469	                            return Err(notify_and_wrap(
   470	                                transport,
   471	                                SessionFault::read_only(
   472	                                    "destination module is read-only".to_string(),
   473	                                ),
   474	                            )
   475	                            .await);
   476	                        }
   477	                        Some(resolved.root)
   478	                    }
   479	                    Err(fault) => return Err(notify_and_wrap(transport, fault).await),
   480	                },
   481	                None => None,
   482	            };
   483	            let accept = SessionAccept {
   484	                // The byte RECEIVER advertises capacity at session
   485	                // open (D-2026-06-20-1/-2); consumed by the dial when
   486	                // the data plane lands (otp-4b).
   487	                receiver_capacity: if local_role == TransferRole::Destination {
   488	                    Some(crate::engine::local_receiver_capacity())
   489	                } else {
   490	                    None
   491	                },
   492	                // No grant = in-stream byte carrier, otp-4a's only one.
   493	                data_plane: None,
   494	            };
   495	            transport.send(frame(Frame::Accept(accept.clone()))).await?;
   496	            Ok(Negotiated {
   497	                open,
   498	                accept,
   499	                resolved_root,
   500	            })
   501	        }
   502	    }
   503	}
   504	
   505	/// Receive one frame during establish; peer errors and closes become
   506	/// terminal faults.
   507	async fn expect_frame(transport: &mut FrameTransport) -> Result<Frame> {
   508	    match transport.recv().await? {
   509	        Some(TransferFrame {
   510	            frame: Some(Frame::Error(err)),
   511	        }) => Err(eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::from_wire(err))),
   512	        Some(TransferFrame { frame: Some(f) }) => Ok(f),
   513	        Some(TransferFrame { frame: None }) => Err(eyre::Report::new(
   514	            SessionFault::protocol_violation("frame with empty oneof"),
   515	        )),
   516	        None => Err(eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::internal(
   517	            "peer closed during session establish",
   518	        ))),
   519	    }
   520	}
   521	
   522	/// Send the fault to the peer (best effort), mark it notified, and
   523	/// wrap it for return.
   524	async fn notify_and_wrap(transport: &mut FrameTransport, mut fault: SessionFault) -> eyre::Report {
   525	    let _ = transport.send(error_frame(&fault)).await;
   526	    fault.peer_notified = true;
   527	    eyre::Report::new(fault)
   528	}
   529	
   530	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   531	// SOURCE driver
   532	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   533	
   534	/// Events the source's receive half forwards to its send half. The
   535	/// channel is unbounded but bounded by construction: every `Need`
   536	/// consumes a distinct sent-manifest entry (unknown or repeated paths
   537	/// fault the session), so the queue never exceeds the source's own
   538	/// manifest size — the contract's bounded-buffering rule holds.
   539	enum SourceEvent {
   540	    Need(FileHeader),
   541	    NeedComplete,
   542	    Summary(TransferSummary),
   543	    Fault(SessionFault),
   544	}
   545	
   546	/// Run the SOURCE role of one transfer session over `transport`.
   547	/// Returns the destination-computed `TransferSummary` (contract: the
   548	/// end that wrote the bytes is the end that attests to them).
   549	pub async fn run_source(
   550	    cfg: SourceSessionConfig,
   551	    transport: FrameTransport,
   552	    source: Arc<dyn TransferSource>,
   553	) -> Result<TransferSummary> {
   554	    let mut transport = transport;
   555	    if let SessionEndpoint::Initiator { open } = &cfg.endpoint {
   556	        // Own-config coherence: a source initiator declares SOURCE.
   557	        let declared = TransferRole::try_from(open.initiator_role);
   558	        if declared != Ok(TransferRole::Source) {
   559	            eyre::bail!("run_source initiator must declare TRANSFER_ROLE_SOURCE in SessionOpen");
   560	        }
   561	        if let Err(fault) = source_open_validator(open) {
   562	            eyre::bail!("run_source initiator config unsupported: {fault}");
   563	        }
   564	    }
   565	
   566	    let negotiated = establish(
   567	        &mut transport,
   568	        &cfg.hello,
   569	        &cfg.endpoint,
   570	        TransferRole::Source,
   571	        &source_open_validator,
   572	        // A SOURCE responder's endpoint resolution (module→root for a
   573	        // daemon-send) lands with otp-5; otp-4a's daemon is always the
   574	        // DESTINATION responder, so the source never resolves here.
   575	        None,
   576	    )
   577	    .await?;
   578	
   579	    let (mut tx, rx) = transport.split();
   580	    let sent: Arc<StdMutex<HashMap<String, FileHeader>>> = Arc::default();
   581	    // Set by the send half the moment ManifestComplete goes out. On
   582	    // an ordered transport, a NeedComplete arriving while this is
   583	    // still false is provably premature — the peer cannot have
   584	    // received what we have not sent (contract: NeedComplete only
   585	    // after ManifestComplete received + all entries diffed).
   586	    let manifest_sent = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));
   587	    let (event_tx, event_rx) = mpsc::unbounded_channel();
   588	    // AbortOnDrop: an early error return below must abort the receive
   589	    // half instead of leaking it (same rationale as design-2 / w4-1).
   590	    let _recv_guard = AbortOnDrop::new(tokio::spawn(source_recv_half(
   591	        rx,
   592	        Arc::clone(&sent),
   593	        Arc::clone(&manifest_sent),
   594	        event_tx,
   595	    )));
   596	
   597	    match source_send_half(
   598	        &cfg,
   599	        &negotiated,
   600	        &mut tx,
   601	        source,
   602	        sent,
   603	        &manifest_sent,
   604	        event_rx,
   605	    )
   606	    .await
   607	    {
   608	        Ok(summary) => Ok(summary),
   609	        Err(report) => {
   610	            let mut fault = fault_from_report(report);
   611	            if !fault.peer_notified {
   612	                let _ = tx.send(error_frame(&fault)).await;
   613	                fault.peer_notified = true;
   614	            }
   615	            Err(eyre::Report::new(fault))
   616	        }
   617	    }
   618	}

   624	async fn source_recv_half(
   625	    mut rx: Box<dyn FrameRx>,
   626	    sent: Arc<StdMutex<HashMap<String, FileHeader>>>,
   627	    manifest_sent: Arc<AtomicBool>,
   628	    events: mpsc::UnboundedSender<SourceEvent>,

   343	impl Blit for BlitService {
   344	    type PushStream = ReceiverStream<Result<ServerPushResponse, Status>>;
   345	    type PullSyncStream = ReceiverStream<Result<ServerPullMessage, Status>>;
   346	    type FindStream = ReceiverStream<Result<FindEntry, Status>>;
   347	    type DiskUsageStream = ReceiverStream<Result<DiskUsageEntry, Status>>;
   348	    type DelegatedPullStream = ReceiverStream<Result<DelegatedPullProgress, Status>>;
   349	    type SubscribeStream =
   350	        std::pin::Pin<Box<dyn tokio_stream::Stream<Item = Result<DaemonEvent, Status>> + Send>>;
   351	    type TransferStream = ReceiverStream<Result<blit_core::generated::TransferFrame, Status>>;
   352	
   353	    /// ONE_TRANSFER_PATH otp-4a: the daemon serves the unified session
   354	    /// by running `run_destination` as the Responder — the byte
   355	    /// RECEIVER of a client-initiated SOURCE push. Mirrors `push`:
   356	    /// register a jobs row, race the session against cancel/hangup, and
   357	    /// return the response stream immediately (the session runs in the
   358	    /// spawned task, feeding the `ReceiverStream`). Session refusals
   359	    /// travel to the peer as `SessionError` frames; the daemon-specific
   360	    /// module resolution + transport assembly live in `super::transfer`.
   361	    /// Contract: docs/TRANSFER_SESSION.md.
   362	    async fn transfer(
   363	        &self,
   364	        request: Request<tonic::Streaming<blit_core::generated::TransferFrame>>,
   365	    ) -> Result<Response<Self::TransferStream>, Status> {
   366	        let peer = peer_addr_string(&request);
   367	        let modules = Arc::clone(&self.modules);
   368	        let default_root = self.default_root.clone();
   369	        let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel(32);
   370	        let inbound = request.into_inner();
   371	        let metrics = Arc::clone(&self.metrics);
   372	        metrics.inc_push();
   373	        let guard = Arc::clone(&metrics).enter_transfer();
   374	        // Jobs row: registered with an empty endpoint (the module/path
   375	        // arrive in the SessionOpen, mid-handshake inside the session).
   376	        // Populating the row's endpoint from the open is a follow-up —
   377	        // the row still supports CancelJob and appears in GetState, and
   378	        // reuses ActiveJobKind::Push (daemon-receive = push-equivalent)
   379	        // until the kind taxonomy is revisited at cutover.
   380	        let job = self.active_jobs.register(
   381	            ActiveJobKind::Push,
   382	            peer.clone(),
   383	            String::new(),
   384	            String::new(),
   385	        );
   386	        self.emit_transfer_started(&job, ActiveJobKind::Push, &peer, "", "");
   387	        let started = std::time::Instant::now();
   388	        let events_tx = self.events_tx();
   389	
   390	        tokio::spawn(async move {
   391	            let guard = guard;
   392	            let job = job;
   393	            let cancel_token = job.cancellation_token().clone();
   394	            let (ok, err_msg) = resolve_streaming_outcome(
   395	                super::transfer::run_transfer_session(modules, default_root, inbound, tx.clone()),
   396	                &tx,
   397	                &cancel_token,
   398	                &metrics,
   399	            )
   400	            .await;
   401	            job.record_outcome(ok, err_msg.clone());
   402	            let finished_event = build_transfer_finished_event(&job, ok, err_msg.as_deref());
   403	            drop(job);
   404	            drop(guard);
   405	            let _ = events_tx.send(finished_event);
   406	            metrics.log_completion("transfer", started.elapsed(), ok);
   407	        });
   408	
   409	        Ok(Response::new(ReceiverStream::new(rx)))
   410	    }

  1176	/// Stat-and-compare one chunk of manifest entries on the blocking
  1177	/// pool (2+ syscalls per entry — same rationale as the daemon's
  1178	/// w4-4 chunked checks), then stream the resulting need batch.
  1179	async fn diff_chunk_and_send_needs(
  1180	    transport: &mut FrameTransport,
  1181	    chunk: Vec<FileHeader>,
  1182	    dst_root: &Path,
  1183	    canonical_dst_root: Option<&Path>,
  1184	    compare_opts: &CompareOptions,
  1185	    outstanding: &mut HashSet<String>,
  1186	    needed_paths: &mut Vec<String>,
  1187	) -> Result<()> {
  1188	    if chunk.is_empty() {
  1189	        return Ok(());
  1190	    }
  1191	    let dst_root = dst_root.to_path_buf();
  1192	    let canonical = canonical_dst_root.map(Path::to_path_buf);
  1193	    let opts = compare_opts.clone();
  1194	    let needed: Vec<String> = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || -> Result<Vec<String>> {
  1195	        let mut needed = Vec::new();
  1196	        for header in &chunk {
  1197	            if destination_needs(header, &dst_root, canonical.as_deref(), &opts)? {
  1198	                needed.push(header.relative_path.clone());
  1199	            }
  1200	        }
  1201	        Ok(needed)
  1202	    })
  1203	    .await
  1204	    .map_err(|err| eyre::eyre!("destination diff task panicked: {err}"))??;
  1205	
  1206	    let entries: Vec<NeedEntry> = needed
  1207	        .into_iter()
  1208	        // A path the source manifests twice is diffed twice but
  1209	        // needed at most once.
  1210	        .filter(|path| outstanding.insert(path.clone()))
  1211	        .map(|relative_path| {
  1212	            needed_paths.push(relative_path.clone());
  1213	            NeedEntry {
  1214	                relative_path,
  1215	                resume: false, // resume lands at otp-7
  1216	            }
  1217	        })
  1218	        .collect();
  1219	    if entries.is_empty() {
  1220	        return Ok(());
  1221	    }
  1222	    transport
  1223	        .send(frame(Frame::NeedBatch(NeedBatch { entries })))
  1224	        .await?;
  1225	    Ok(())
  1226	}

  1228	/// Does the destination need this manifest entry? Stats its own file
  1229	/// and delegates the verdict to `manifest::header_transfer_status` —
  1230	/// the same mode-aware owner `compare_manifests` uses, fed from a
  1231	/// live stat instead of a materialized target manifest.
  1232	fn destination_needs(
  1233	    header: &FileHeader,
  1234	    dst_root: &Path,
  1235	    canonical_dst_root: Option<&Path>,
  1236	    opts: &CompareOptions,
  1237	) -> Result<bool> {

  1240	            crate::path_safety::safe_join_contained(canonical, dst_root, &header.relative_path)
  1241	        }
  1242	        None => crate::path_safety::safe_join(dst_root, &header.relative_path),
  1243	    }
  1244	    .map_err(|err| {
  1245	        SessionFault::protocol_violation(format!(
  1246	            "manifest path '{}' escapes the destination root: {err:#}",
  1247	            header.relative_path
  1248	        ))
  1249	    })?;

     1	//! ONE_TRANSFER_PATH unified `Transfer` session — daemon side.
     2	//!
     3	//! otp-1 (D-2026-07-05-4) landed the wire surface (the RPC, the frame
     4	//! set, `docs/TRANSFER_SESSION.md`) with the handler refusing
     5	//! UNIMPLEMENTED. otp-4a lands the behavior: the daemon serves the RPC
     6	//! by running `blit_core::transfer_session::run_destination` as the
     7	//! Responder — the byte RECEIVER of a client-initiated SOURCE push.
     8	//! The dispatcher in `core.rs::transfer` mirrors `push`: register a
     9	//! jobs row, race the session against cancel/hangup via
    10	//! `resolve_streaming_outcome`, return the response `ReceiverStream`.
    11	//!
    12	//! This module owns the two daemon-specific pieces the session driver
    13	//! in blit-core cannot: (1) the [`OpenResolver`] that maps a wire
    14	//! module/path to a local root and read-only decision (blit-core stays
    15	//! free of module config and `tonic::Status`), and (2) the transport
    16	//! assembly + outcome mapping.
    17	//!
    18	//! otp-4a uses the in-stream byte carrier only; the TCP data plane
    19	//! grant + resize land at otp-4b. Progress-byte wiring
    20	//! (`with_byte_progress`) is not threaded yet — session rows report
    21	//! `bytes_completed=0`, matching today's push rows.
    22	
    23	use std::collections::HashMap;
    24	use std::sync::Arc;
    25	
    26	use tokio::sync::mpsc;
    27	use tokio::sync::Mutex;
    28	use tonic::{Status, Streaming};
    29	
    30	use blit_core::generated::session_error::Code;
    31	use blit_core::generated::{SessionOpen, TransferFrame};
    32	use blit_core::transfer_session::transport::grpc_daemon_transport;
    33	use blit_core::transfer_session::{
    34	    run_destination, DestinationSessionConfig, DestinationTarget, HelloConfig, OpenResolver,
    35	    ResolvedEndpoint, SessionEndpoint, SessionFault,
    36	};
    37	
    38	use super::util::{resolve_contained_path, resolve_module, resolve_relative_path};
    39	use crate::runtime::{ModuleConfig, RootExport};
    40	
    41	/// Map a resolver `tonic::Status` onto a `SessionError` code. blit-core
    42	/// is deliberately `Status`-free, so the daemon picks the wire code:
    43	/// an unknown module is `MODULE_UNKNOWN`, a bad or escaping path is a
    44	/// `PROTOCOL_VIOLATION` (the initiator sent an unusable request),
    45	/// anything else is `INTERNAL`.
    46	fn status_to_fault(status: Status) -> SessionFault {
    47	    let code = match status.code() {
    48	        tonic::Code::NotFound => Code::ModuleUnknown,
    49	        tonic::Code::InvalidArgument | tonic::Code::PermissionDenied => Code::ProtocolViolation,
    50	        _ => Code::Internal,
    51	    };
    52	    SessionFault::refusal(code, status.message().to_string())
    53	}
    54	
    55	/// Build the daemon's [`OpenResolver`]: given a received `SessionOpen`,
    56	/// resolve its module + path to an absolute local root and report the
    57	/// module's read-only flag. Mirrors the push Header sequence
    58	/// (`resolve_module` → path validation → F2 canonical containment via
    59	/// `resolve_contained_path`), refusing with a `SessionError` instead of
    60	/// a `tonic::Status`. The closure is `Fn` (callable once per session)
    61	/// and clones its captured handles per call so it stays `Send + Sync`.
    62	pub(crate) fn make_open_resolver(
    63	    modules: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, ModuleConfig>>>,
    64	    default_root: Option<RootExport>,
    65	) -> Box<OpenResolver> {
    66	    Box::new(move |open: &SessionOpen| {
    67	        let modules = Arc::clone(&modules);
    68	        let default_root = default_root.clone();
    69	        let module_name = open.module.clone();
    70	        let wire_path = open.path.clone();
    71	        Box::pin(async move {
    72	            let config = resolve_module(&modules, default_root.as_ref(), &module_name)
    73	                .await
    74	                .map_err(status_to_fault)?;
    75	            // Empty path targets the module root; a non-empty path is
    76	            // validated and contained against the module's canonical
    77	            // root (F2 symlink-escape protection — the same chokepoint
    78	            // the per-file write path uses).
    79	            let root = if wire_path.is_empty() {
    80	                config.path.clone()
    81	            } else {
    82	                let rel = resolve_relative_path(&wire_path).map_err(status_to_fault)?;
    83	                resolve_contained_path(&config, &rel).map_err(status_to_fault)?
    84	            };
    85	            Ok(ResolvedEndpoint {
    86	                root,
    87	                read_only: config.read_only,
    88	            })
    89	        })
    90	    })
    91	}
    92	
    93	/// Run one daemon-side transfer session to completion as the DESTINATION
    94	/// Responder, returning `Ok(())` on a clean transfer or `Err(Status)`
    95	/// carrying the session fault's message for the jobs record. The
    96	/// session communicates its own refusals to the peer as `SessionError`
    97	/// *frames* (via the response stream); this `Status` is for the
    98	/// daemon's outcome record and `resolve_streaming_outcome`'s terminal
    99	/// handling, not the primary error channel.
   100	pub(crate) async fn run_transfer_session(
   101	    modules: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, ModuleConfig>>>,
   102	    default_root: Option<RootExport>,
   103	    inbound: Streaming<TransferFrame>,
   104	    tx: mpsc::Sender<Result<TransferFrame, Status>>,
   105	) -> Result<(), Status> {
   106	    let transport = grpc_daemon_transport(tx, inbound);
   107	    let resolver = make_open_resolver(modules, default_root);
   108	    let cfg = DestinationSessionConfig {
   109	        hello: HelloConfig::default(),
   110	        endpoint: SessionEndpoint::Responder,
   111	    };
   112	    match run_destination(cfg, transport, DestinationTarget::Resolve(resolver)).await {
   113	        Ok(_outcome) => Ok(()),
   114	        Err(report) => {
   115	            // run_destination already emitted a SessionError frame to
   116	            // the peer; surface the reason for the record.
   117	            let msg = report
   118	                .downcast_ref::<SessionFault>()
   119	                .map(|f| f.message.clone())
   120	                .unwrap_or_else(|| format!("{report:#}"));
   121	            Err(Status::internal(msg))
   122	        }
   123	    }
   124	}
   125	
   126	#[cfg(test)]
   127	mod tests {
   128	    use super::*;
   129	
   130	    #[test]
   131	    fn status_to_fault_maps_not_found_to_module_unknown() {
   132	        let fault = status_to_fault(Status::not_found("module 'x' not found"));
   133	        assert_eq!(fault.code, Code::ModuleUnknown);
   134	        assert!(fault.message.contains("not found"));
   135	    }
   136	
   137	    #[test]
   138	    fn status_to_fault_maps_permission_denied_to_protocol_violation() {
   139	        let fault = status_to_fault(Status::permission_denied("path containment: escape"));
   140	        assert_eq!(fault.code, Code::ProtocolViolation);
   141	    }
   142	
   143	    #[test]
   144	    fn status_to_fault_maps_invalid_argument_to_protocol_violation() {
   145	        let fault = status_to_fault(Status::invalid_argument("path not allowed"));
   146	        assert_eq!(fault.code, Code::ProtocolViolation);
   147	    }
   148	}

codex
The core handshake shape matches the intended order so far: resolver after open validation and before accept, with refusal replacing accept. I’m checking whether any `tonic::Status` dependency or cancellation path violates the narrower invariants rather than the happy-path tests.

  1482	            pending::<()>(),   // ...but no cancel
  1483	            true,              // detached

  1495	    /// w4-3: a client hangup (dropped response `Receiver`) must resolve

  1500	    /// resolve — the test would hang, not merely assert-fail.
  1501	    /// Deterministic: the receiver is dropped before the race starts,

  1516	    /// w4-3: a fired row token must resolve a still-running streaming

  1537	    /// w4-3 extends audit-10's guarantee to the streaming dispatchers:
