 succeeded in 0ms:
  1310	/// RPC races through — `delegated_pull` calls it directly (handler
  1311	/// output `bool`), while `push` / `pull_sync` go through
  1312	/// [`resolve_streaming_outcome`] (handler output `Result<(), Status>`).
  1313	///
  1314	/// The select is `biased` with the **handler branch first**: when the
  1315	/// handler future is `Ready`, its result wins even if the cancel token
  1316	/// has also just fired or the client just hung up. A handler that is
  1317	/// still `Pending` yields to a client hangup (only when `!detach`) or a
  1318	/// `CancelJob` cancel, both of which resolve to `None` so the caller
  1319	/// records the cancellation.
  1320	///
  1321	/// Pre-audit-10 the cancel branch was evaluated before the handler, so a
  1322	/// transfer that completed at the same instant `CancelJob` fired its
  1323	/// token was mis-recorded as "cancelled via CancelJob" despite having
  1324	/// actually succeeded. Ordering completion first makes a real result
  1325	/// (success *or* failure) authoritative over a simultaneous cancel.
  1326	///
  1327	/// Returns `Some(output)` when the handler completed, or `None` for a
  1328	/// client hangup or cancel.
  1329	async fn resolve_transfer_outcome<T, H, C, K>(
  1330	    handler: H,
  1331	    tx_closed: C,
  1332	    cancelled: K,
  1333	    detach: bool,
  1334	) -> Option<T>
  1335	where
  1336	    H: std::future::Future<Output = T>,
  1337	    C: std::future::Future<Output = ()>,
  1338	    K: std::future::Future<Output = ()>,
  1339	{
  1340	    tokio::select! {
  1341	        biased;
  1342	        output = handler => Some(output),
  1343	        _ = tx_closed, if !detach => None,
  1344	        _ = cancelled => None,
  1345	    }
  1346	}
  1347	
  1348	/// w4-3: resolve a streaming transfer RPC's (`push` / `pull_sync`)
  1349	/// terminal outcome, racing the handler against client hangup and the
  1350	/// row's `CancelJob` token via [`resolve_transfer_outcome`].
  1351	///
  1352	/// Pre-w4-3 these dispatchers bare-awaited their handlers, so a client
  1353	/// that disconnected during a send-free compute phase (pull_sync's
  1354	/// enumerate+checksum collection, push's mirror purge) left the daemon
  1355	/// running the whole remaining handler for a dead peer — unbounded,
  1356	/// unobservable work that `CancelJob` also refused to touch
  1357	/// (async-daemon-handlers-blind-to-disconnect-in-compute-phases).
  1358	/// Dropping the handler future propagates through the existing
  1359	/// cancellation paths: the push data-plane accept task is
  1360	/// `AbortOnDrop`-wrapped and its workers live in a `JoinSet` (w4-1),
  1361	/// and pull_sync's payload feeder exits when its channel closes. An
  1362	/// in-flight `spawn_blocking` enumeration/checksum batch still runs to
  1363	/// its natural end with the result discarded — making that window
  1364	/// abortable is the finding's stated follow-up slice.
  1365	///
  1366	/// The streaming RPCs have no `detach` mode (the client is inherently
  1367	/// attached to the byte path), so the hangup arm is always armed —
  1368	/// hence the hardcoded `detach: false`.
  1369	///
  1370	/// Returns the `(ok, error_message)` pair the ActiveJobs ring records:
  1371	/// - handler completed → its result via [`outcome_from_status`]; an
  1372	///   `Err` is counted (`inc_error`) and forwarded to the
  1373	///   still-connected client, exactly as the pre-w4-3 dispatchers did.
  1374	/// - client hung up → `(false, "client cancelled")`; nothing is sent —
  1375	///   the receiver is gone, that's what ended the race.
  1376	/// - cancel token fired → `(false, "cancelled via CancelJob")`, and the
  1377	///   still-connected client gets a terminal `Status::cancelled`. This
  1378	///   arm is live in production: D-2026-07-04-3 flipped
  1379	///   `ActiveJobKind::supports_cancellation` on for push/pull_sync, so
  1380	///   `blit jobs cancel` (and the TUI `K`/`Shift+X`) reaches it for
  1381	///   attached transfers.
  1382	async fn resolve_streaming_outcome<T, H>(
  1383	    handler: H,
  1384	    tx: &mpsc::Sender<Result<T, Status>>,
  1385	    cancel_token: &CancellationToken,
  1386	    metrics: &TransferMetrics,
  1387	) -> (bool, Option<String>)
  1388	where
  1389	    H: std::future::Future<Output = Result<(), Status>>,
  1390	{
  1391	    let outcome =
  1392	        resolve_transfer_outcome(handler, tx.closed(), cancel_token.cancelled(), false).await;
  1393	    match outcome {
  1394	        Some(result) => {
  1395	            let (ok, err_msg) = outcome_from_status(&result);
  1396	            if let Err(status) = result {
  1397	                metrics.inc_error();
  1398	                let _ = tx.send(Err(status)).await;
  1399	            }
  1400	            (ok, err_msg)
  1401	        }
  1402	        // Same disambiguation the delegated_pull closure uses: a fired
  1403	        // token means the cause was CancelJob; otherwise the client
  1404	        // hung up.
  1405	        None if cancel_token.is_cancelled() => {
  1406	            let _ = tx
  1407	                .send(Err(Status::cancelled("transfer cancelled via CancelJob")))
  1408	                .await;
  1409	            (false, Some("cancelled via CancelJob".to_string()))
  1410	        }
  1411	        None => (false, Some("client cancelled".to_string())),
  1412	    }
  1413	}
  1414	
  1415	/// Translate a handler's `Result<_, Status>` into the
  1416	/// `(ok, error_message)` pair the ActiveJobs guard expects.
  1417	/// Used inside [`resolve_streaming_outcome`] for the `push` /
  1418	/// `pull_sync` dispatchers. `delegated_pull` has its own shape
  1419	/// (handler returns `bool` inside a select) and inlines the
  1420	/// equivalent mapping there.
  1421	fn outcome_from_status<T>(result: &Result<T, Status>) -> (bool, Option<String>) {
  1422	    match result {
  1423	        Ok(_) => (true, None),
  1424	        Err(status) => (false, Some(status.message().to_string())),
  1425	    }
  1426	}
  1427	
  1428	#[cfg(test)]
  1429	mod tests {
  1430	    use super::*;
  1431	    use crate::active_jobs::ActiveJobKind;
  1432	    use blit_core::generated::TransferKind as WireKind;
  1433	
  1434	    fn empty_service() -> BlitService {
  1435	        BlitService::with_modules(HashMap::new(), false)

(b) **W8.1** — `zero_copy.rs` is **excluded** from the dead-code deletion sweep; owner judges it has FAST potential; disposition is an evaluation slice (`w8-1b`) that either produces a plan doc to wire splice into the receive pipeline or concludes deletion. docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:3:**Status**: Active (Phase A deliverable of `docs/plan/DESIGN_COHERENCE_REVIEW.md`, D-2026-06-11-1)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:44:   4, 5): every tonic::Status→String conversion destroys the chain
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:434:The retry concept has a nominal owner — blit_app::transfers::retry (is_retryable + run_with_retries), wired to --retry/--wait for exactly three CLI verbs in blit-cli/main.rs:55-69 — but its effectiveness is smeared across strata that don't cooperate. The classifier sees only std::io::Error in the eyre chain, while the gRPC layers (pull.rs, push client helpers, the whole delegated remote-to-remote path, daemon delegated_pull) flatten every tonic::Status into a bare string through at least four independently-written conversion idioms, so on those paths --retry is documented-by-TODO to silently do nothing (pull.rs:322-329, 780-788). A second, dead classifier in blit-core/src/errors.rs contradicts the live one on ConnectionRefused/UnexpectedEof/NotConnected and remains publicly exported. The three seed channel builders diverge on the one axis that matters here: blit-app/client.rs preserves the error chain (connect failures retryable), the core pull/push builders strip it (same failure fatal). Sharpest problem: the combination of unshipped audit-h3c slice 2, no client keepalive, and chain-stripping means the gRPC fallback path — used precisely when networks misbehave — can hang forever, and when it does fail, the user's --retry flag won't fire and nothing says why.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:438:- --retry/--wait is silently inert on the primary gRPC paths. Every tonic::Status->String conversion (pull.rs:307/332/493/507/541, format_status at pull.rs:116 feeding PullSyncError at 792-798, push helpers.rs:48, the entire delegated path in blit-app/remote.rs and daemon delegated_pull.rs:216/245) destroys the io::Error chain that is_retryable (retry.rs:27-33) requires. The code knows: TODOs at pull.rs:322-329 and 780-788 literally say '--retry/--wait will silently stop working on this code path' — a RELIABLE failure mode where the advertised flag does nothing and tells no one. audit-h3c slice 2, which would fix it, is unshipped (stall_guard.rs:35-41).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:442:- Code::Unavailable — gRPC's canonical transient error — is examined in blit-app/transfers/remote.rs (714, 738, 849) solely to pick friendlier wording, never to classify as retryable. A classifier extension accepting tonic::Status codes (Unavailable, DeadlineExceeded, ResourceExhausted) plus chain preservation would make --retry actually work on every gRPC path; today the classifier's only vocabulary is io::ErrorKind.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:453:- [DIVERGE] tonic::Status -> eyre string conversion (the chain-stripping that blinds the classifier)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:486:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs:841 — Wiring: daemon push-receive socket wrapped in StallGuard (audit-h3a) — Daemon-side TimedOut; surfaced to the pushing CLI only as a tonic Status string via control plane, so the client classifier never sees it directly (client usually sees its own BrokenPipe/Reset instead).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:490:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:307 — CLASSIFIER-BLIND: pull() control/fallback receive maps tonic::Status -> eyre!(status.message().to_string()) at 307 and 332 — Explicit TODO(audit-h3c-2) at 322-329: this strip means is_retryable will not classify a stall/drop here and --retry/--wait will not fire.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:491:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:493 — CLASSIFIER-BLIND: scan_remote_files (493, 507 with TODO at 502-504) and open_remote_file (541) strip Status to String the same way
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:492:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:792 — CLASSIFIER-BLIND: pull_sync_with_spec maps Status into PullSyncError::transfer/negotiation, whose format_status (116-122) flattens to String — TODO(audit-h3c-2) at 780-788 says exactly this: '--retry/--wait will silently stop working on this code path'. This is the PRIMARY pull path.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:493:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/helpers.rs:48 — CLASSIFIER-BLIND: map_status(Status) -> eyre!(status.message().to_string()) used across the push client — All push control-plane failures (daemon died, daemon-side StallGuard fired) become plain strings.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:497:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:709 — CLASSIFIER-BLIND with code-aware wording: delegated pull maps Status by Code (Unimplemented 709, Unavailable 714, mid-stream Unavailable 738-751, detach variants 843-859, pre-Started 906-909) into plain eyre strings — Code::Unavailable — the canonical transient gRPC condition — is inspected only to improve the message text, never to mark the error retryable.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:499:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/delegated_pull.rs:216 — Daemon-side strip: upstream tonic Status flattened to status.message().to_string() into proto error/progress events (216, 245) — Error structure is destroyed before it even crosses the wire back to the CLI.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:514:No module owns error handling; ownership is smeared across all six crates plus the proto. The nominal owner, blit_core::errors (TransferError/ErrorCategory/categorize_io_error, errors.rs:12-121, exported at lib.rs:9), is dead code with zero consumers, while the live retry policy lives in blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs with an io::ErrorKind table that directly contradicts the dead one on three kinds (ConnectionRefused, UnexpectedEof, NotConnected). The workspace is uniformly eyre (thiserror and anyhow appear nowhere), but at least seven distinct styles coexist: chain-preserving context, chain-destroying eyre! stringification, three hand-rolled typed errors, tonic::Status-as-error with 116/189 constructors collapsed to Status::internal and line-dependent {err} vs {err:#} formatting, sentinel io::Errors for retry signaling, proto-carried string errors, and five different stderr prefixes. The sharpest problem is a self-documented broken contract: stall_guard.rs manufactures downcastable TimedOut errors precisely so --retry/--wait can classify stalls as retryable, but PullSyncError/format_status (pull.rs:82/116) and the dominant eyre!(status.message().to_string()) pattern (15+ sites) flatten errors to strings before the classifier sees them — the TODO at pull.rs:780-788 admits retry silently no-ops on the pull-sync path. Secondary: the same stringification drops the gRPC code, so a code-only Status renders as an empty user-facing error, violating RELIABLE's plain-failure principle.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:518:- --retry/--wait is silently broken on the pull-sync path: stall_guard manufactures io::ErrorKind::TimedOut precisely so retry.rs:27 can downcast it, but PullSyncError (pull.rs:82) and format_status (pull.rs:116) flatten the Status to a String before it reaches the eyre chain. The repo knows: TODO(audit-h3c-2) at pull.rs:780-788 documents the break, unfixed. RELIABLE violation — the feature degrades with no error at all.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:521:- The dominant Status→eyre pattern eyre!(status.message().to_string()) (15+ sites across blit-core, blit-app admin, blit-cli) drops the gRPC code; a code-only Status with empty message renders as a completely empty error line to the user — the empty-message guard exists only in pull.rs:116 and is private to PullSyncError.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:522:- The daemon collapses 116/189 Status constructors to Status::internal, including plain file NotFound/PermissionDenied (pull.rs:516,546,579,601). Client-side code that branches on status.code() (remote.rs:709-751, jobs.rs:83-118) can therefore only ever distinguish transport-level codes; daemon-originated causes are undifferentiable strings.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:523:- Chain survival across the wire is line-dependent: ~48 daemon eyre→Status sites format with {err}/{} (outermost message only) vs ~9 with {err:#} (full chain), in the same files (pull_sync.rs:507 vs :653; util.rs:61 vs :110). Root causes of mid-transfer failures are intermittently amputated before the user sees them.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:524:- No thiserror and no anyhow anywhere in the workspace — all six crates are eyre-only with three hand-rolled std::error::Error impls (errors.rs:87, pull.rs:114, fast_path.rs:74). At least 7 distinct error-handling styles coexist: (1) chain-preserving eyre context (blit-app/client.rs, transfer/data_plane.rs), (2) chain-dropping eyre! stringify (core pull/push clients, admin verbs), (3) hand-rolled typed enums (blit-core), (4) tonic::Status-as-error with mixed formatting (blit-daemon services), (5) sentinel io::ErrorKind for retry signaling (stall_guard, tar_stream:84), (6) proto-carried string errors with one Phase enum (blit.proto:987/:710/:811), (7) prefixed-eprintln side channels with five different prefixes ('blit:' retry.rs:70, '[push]' helpers.rs:184, '[warn]/[info]' daemon main.rs:35, '[failed]' cli jobs.rs:336, 'blit-prometheus-bridge:' server.rs:224).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:529:- [DIVERGE] tonic::Status → human-readable text converters (4 independent styles)
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:531:  - divergence: Only format_status preserves the gRPC code and guards against an empty Status message; the dominant pattern (15+ sites) discards the code entirely, so a transport-level Unavailable with empty message renders as an empty error string. Remediation hints exist only in the delegation path.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:538:- [DIVERGE] eyre::Report → tonic::Status formatting at the daemon service boundary
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:559:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:82 — Hand-rolled typed error PullSyncError {Negotiation, Transfer} (no thiserror — manual Display + Error impl). Built FROM tonic::Status via format_status; carries only a String, so the Status code and any io::Error source are flattened.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:560:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:116 — format_status(): the ONLY Status→text converter that preserves the gRPC code and handles an empty message (`msg (code)` / bare code). Used only by PullSyncError constructors.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:562:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:780 — In-code TODO(audit-h3c-2) admitting the defect: PullSyncError's stringification of Status destroys the io::Error so blit_app::transfers::retry::is_retryable cannot classify stall TimedOut as retryable — `--retry/--wait` silently stops working on the pull-sync path. Acknowledged, not fixed.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:566:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/helpers.rs:48 — map_status(status) -> eyre!(status.message().to_string()): the push client's canonical Status→eyre converter; drops code and chain; empty-message Status becomes an empty error string.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:567:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs:27 — is_retryable(): retry classifier that walks err.chain() and downcasts to io::Error, then checks is_retryable_io_kind (:35). Entire --retry/--wait feature depends on upstream conversions preserving io::Error in the eyre chain — which the Status-stringify sites do not.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:573:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:507 — Daemon style: eyre→tonic::Status at the service boundary. :507 uses {err} (drops eyre context chain) while :653 uses {err:#} (keeps it) in the SAME FILE — chain survival depends on which line fails.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:575:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:516 — io::Error→Status::internal(format!("open {}: {}")) — file-level NotFound/PermissionDenied become code INTERNAL on the wire. Daemon-wide tally: 116 of 189 Status constructors are Status::internal (40 invalid_argument, 13 permission_denied, 8 failed_precondition, 7 not_found, 5 aborted), so the gRPC code dimension carries almost no information for clients.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:587:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/proto/blit.proto:811 — Wire error surface #3: TransferRecord.error_message — recents/audit history stores Status::message() string or "cancelled before outcome recorded" marker.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:588:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-tui/src/main.rs:5734 — TUI site (recorded only, per instructions): tonic::Status flattened to EventOrError::Error(format!("stream: {}", status.message())) — String-channel error style; blit-tui is eyre-based (Cargo.toml:18).
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:637:- `SUBSCRIBE_BROADCAST_CAPACITY` = 256 events (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/core.rs:41) — RELIABLE: at 10 Hz × N transfers, 256 is ~25 s of headroom for 1 transfer but ~2.5 s for 10; a lagging subscriber gets Status::aborted and must re-subscribe — capacity is load-dependent but fixed.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:869:- `WINDOWS_NO_BUFFERING_FLOOR` = 2 GiB (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/windows.rs:21) — FAST: static floor when GlobalMemoryStatusEx fails; arbitrary on 256 GB-RAM servers
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:963:  - divergence: Same JoinError-to-Status mapping written twice with slightly different wording in the two push files; the distinct outcome strings elsewhere are intentional but nothing ties them to the proto-documented vocabulary at blit.proto:808/990
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1106:blit-daemon is four readable geological layers. (1) An original transfer-engine stratum (pull.rs, pull_sync.rs, push/*) with terse phase comments, per-file `eprintln!` hot-path logging under inconsistent bracket tags, and three hand-rolled stream-count ladders that re-derive (and then clamp against) blit-core's tuning. (2) A review-remediation stratum that patched R-/audit-/F-numbered fixes into those files four separate times instead of extracting shared code — the accept+token handshake exists in 4 copies with 3 sets of identical timeout constants (the `StdDuration`/`StdDuration2` aliases in pull_sync.rs are the smoking gun), and only the push copy sets TCP nodelay/keepalive. (3) A jobs/TUI milestone stratum (active_jobs.rs, core.rs Subscribe/GetState machinery, recents_store.rs, metrics.rs) written in slice-IDs (b-1..c-5b, m-jobs, rec) whose `#[allow(dead_code)] // lands in next slice` annotations were never swept after the consumers landed. (4) A newest delegation stratum (delegated_pull.rs, delegation_gate.rs, net_timeout.rs) with plan-doc headers, Step-numbered handlers, and a fourth error idiom (phased-progress-frames and String errors) on top of the crate's existing three (Status, eyre, and the select-outcome Option<bool>). Headline findings: the deprecated Pull RPC carries ~500 lines of unreachable TCP data plane that is ALSO the only multi-stream pull implementation — the production pull_sync path runs a single stream ('multi-stream support lives in pull.rs'), a direct FAST violation born of an unfinished migration; the push path drags a vestigial 262,144-slot manifest channel whose receiver exists only to drain it; cross-crate, the daemon re-implements blit-app's manifest enumerator and delete-list applier (the latter already caused one containment-divergence CVE-class bug, R58-F3), hand-rolls the FilterSpec conversion that blit-core validates properly, and inherits the keepalive-less, decode-limit-less client channel for its own outbound delegated connects while its server side carefully configures keepalive. Searches run: full reads of all 17 service-layer and support files (admin.rs/runtime.rs/active_jobs.rs/delegation_gate.rs skimmed at heads plus targeted ranges); rg by API (max_decoding_message_size, keepalive, tcp_nodelay, read_exact token), by symbol (.pull(, scan_remote_files, enumerate_local_manifest, metadata_mtime_seconds, permissions_mode, filter conversions via min_age_secs), by constant (32*1024*1024*1024 ladder), by annotation (allow(dead_code), cfg(test)), and by string ([data-plane], eprintln counts, 'deprecated').
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1110:- **Terse-comment transfer-engine stratum: magic-number tuning ladders, per-event `eprintln!("[tag] ...")` debug logging (no log/tracing crate anywhere in the crate), `Status::internal(format!(...))` on every error, phase-numbered section comments (`// Phase 1: ...`)** — The original push/pull/pull_sync wire implementation. Hand-rolled heuristics live here that re-derive decisions blit-core's tuning already makes; hot-path stderr logging (one eprintln per queued file on push); duplicated send_* helper families per RPC type. This is the bulk of pull.rs, pull_sync.rs, push/control.rs, push/data_plane.rs, admin.rs, util.rs.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1131:- **Delegation stratum (newest): plan-doc-referenced `//!` headers (`See docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md §4.2/§4.3`), numbered `// Step 1..Step 12` handler body, phased-error values instead of Status, trait-injected resolver for testability, and `// ── R25-F2: ... ───` section-divider test organization** — delegated_pull.rs + delegation_gate.rs + net_timeout.rs use a completely different error idiom from the rest of the crate: `Result<(), DelegatedPullProgress>` (error IS a progress frame), `Result<u64, String>` (apply_delete_list), `Result<_, String>` (validate_spec) — versus tonic Status everywhere else and eyre in runtime.rs/main.rs. Four error-handling regimes coexist in one crate. This stratum also cites agent memory by name in a doc comment.
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1137:  - crates/blit-daemon/src/runtime.rs uses eyre::Result (runtime.rs:2) and main.rs:19 `async fn main() -> Result<()>` (eyre) vs service/* tonic Status — error-idiom seam
docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:1299:blit-tui (light pass, ~17k LOC across 24 src files + 5 screens) shows three clean strata, all post-dating the seed problems and all from a single project lineage rather than independent restarts: (1) the Phase 5 A.1 build-out (2026-05-17..22) — a 10,853-line main.rs that monopolizes async (38/39 tokio::spawn, all 34 #[tokio::test]) surrounded by pure sync state-machine modules sharing an identical 'monotonic generation-guard' idiom and saturated with d-NN review-finding comments; (2) a one-day audit-7d extraction wave (2026-05-23) of nine tiny pure-helper modules, each headed with the verbatim formula 'pure X extracted from main.rs (behavior-preserving — verbatim move, no logic change)'; (3) the Phase 6 dual-pane stratum (2026-06-05) with zero finding-ID comments and freshly re-invented size/mtime formatters. Architecturally the crate is well-layered against the seed problem class: it builds NO gRPC channels (tonic appears only as Status/Streaming types at main.rs:5688/5707; all RPC goes through blit_app::admin/transfers/scan) and shares config-dir resolution via blit_core::config::config_dir. The standout duplication is byte formatting: five private copies of format_bytes/format_size inside the crate (screens/f1.rs:614, f2.rs:562, f3.rs:586, f4.rs:699, dual_pane.rs:208), mutually divergent (only f2 got the TiB tier; dual_pane switched to 1 decimal), all shadowing the existing pub blit_app::display::format_bytes (display.rs:14). The Phase 6 dual-pane modules also re-do mtime formatting worse (raw epoch seconds at screens/dual_pane.rs:223 vs f3's YYYY-MM-DD at screens/f3.rs:598). No dead modules; the F3 single-pane browser stack (browse.rs + screens/f3.rs, ~2.6k lines) is the designated casualty of the queued Phase 6 rework and currently coexists with its replacement behind Screen::F3 vs Screen::Dual (main.rs:120-139).

docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:284:**Proposed fix**: Make filter_from_spec pub in operation_spec.rs and replace the push handler's inline mapping with a call to it, converting the resulting error into Status::invalid_argument as pull_sync already does.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:486:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/docs/plan/WORKFLOW_PHASE_2.md:5 — **Status**: Shipped (was: In progress (streaming planner + fast-path routing in place))
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:605:#### errors-daemon-eyre-to-status-chain-amputation — Daemon eyre→Status formatting is line-dependent: ~12 {err:#} sites preserve the cause chain, ~69 {err} sites amputate it
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:609:**Claim**: Whether the root cause of a daemon-side failure crosses the wire depends on which line failed: most Status::*(format!(...)) sites format the eyre Report with {err}/{e} (outermost message only) while a minority use {err:#} (full chain), with both styles coexisting in the same files.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:611:**Mechanism**: eyre::Report's Display prints only the top message; the alternate {:#} prints 'top: cause1: cause2…'. In pull_sync.rs the two pipeline failure paths diverge: line 507 'planning gRPC payloads: {err}' (chain dropped) vs line 653 'pull sync data plane pipeline: {err:#}' (chain kept). util.rs does the same within one file: line 61 'path not allowed: {}: {e}' vs lines 110/124 'path containment: {e:#}'. delegated_pull.rs:374 sends 'delegated pull: {err}' as the wire upstream_message for the entire Transfer phase — the mid-transfer root cause (e.g. an io error three layers down) is amputated before it reaches the CLI. Counting with rg: 12 Status constructions format with :# vs ~69 with plain {var}. This is daemon-side eyre→Status, not the queued slice-2 client-side Status→eyre work.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:659:**Mechanism**: A tokio mpsc send fails only when the receiver was dropped — i.e. the consuming task (response stream / data-plane writer) already exited, usually because IT hit the real error or the client disconnected. The daemon converts this to Status::internal with 11 distinct strings in pull_sync.rs (378 'failed to send ack', 393, 403, 413, 481, 582, 712, 782, 963, 1021, 1040) and 8 in pull.rs (178, 289, 315/337 'data plane died', 593, 619, 950, 965); blit-core sink.rs uses eyre!("gRPC channel closed") at 985, 1016, 1038, 1048, 1056, 1080 and 'gRPC pull stream closed' at 1156-1227. Because outcome_from_status (core.rs:1330) records exactly this string, `blit jobs` history shows 'failed to send ack' for what was actually a client disconnect or a prior pipeline error — three different vocabularies for the same condition, none stating the cause, and the real error in the exited task races against (and often loses to) the send failure.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:662:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:315 — map_err(|_| Status::internal("data plane died")) — SendError discarded, cause unstated
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:667:**Proposed fix**: One shared helper per side (daemon Status, core eyre) with a single honest text like 'response channel closed (peer disconnected or pipeline failed): <what was being sent>'; where the receiver task handle is available, prefer joining it and surfacing its error over the send failure.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:914:**Proposed fix**: Delete crates/blit-core/src/errors.rs and the lib.rs:9 export outright (zero callers, no wire impact). If a shared classifier is wanted later, it should be the slice-2 tonic-Status-aware extension of blit-app's live table, not a revival of this one.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:1052:#### errors-daemon-status-internal-collapse (reviewer: medium) — Daemon collapses 116 of ~199 Status constructions to Status::internal, erasing gRPC code semantics for io errors
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:1056:**Claim**: More than half of all daemon error returns use Status::internal regardless of cause, so a missing source file or permission failure on pull crosses the wire as code Internal, making daemon-originated causes undifferentiable to any code()-branching client.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:1058:**Mechanism**: Tallying Status:: constructors across crates/blit-daemon/src gives 116 internal vs 40 invalid_argument, 13 permission_denied, 7 not_found, etc. (~199 total). In the pull handler, plain file io errors — open/stat/read on the requested path — are wrapped as Status::internal(format!("open {}: {}", ...)) at pull.rs:516, 519, 528, 546, 579, 601, 608, so io::ErrorKind::NotFound/PermissionDenied become Internal on the wire. Client code that branches on status.code() to choose wording or remediation (blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs:709-751 for Unimplemented/Unavailable; blit-app/src/admin/jobs.rs:83-96 for NotFound/FailedPrecondition) can therefore only ever distinguish transport-level or explicitly-coded conditions; every daemon-side io failure lands in the generic fallback arm, and any future code-based retry classification (queued slice-2) is structurally blind to daemon causes.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:1061:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:516 — Status::internal(format!("open {}: {}", abs_path.display(), err)) — io NotFound/PermissionDenied collapsed to Internal; same at 546, 579, 601
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:1148:- **errors-status-strip-code-and-empty-render** — 14 eyre!(status.message()) sites drop the gRPC code and render a code-only Status as a completely empty error
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:1149:  - kill reason: Mechanism verified (all strip sites exist: e.g. /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/admin/rm.rs:30, blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs:307/332/493/507/541, push/client/helpers.rs:48-49; private format_status at pull.rs:116-122; code-preserving outlier at admin/jobs.rs:91-95), but the finding duplicates two already-tracked items. The admin/CLI half is audit finding R3-H12 (AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04_R2.md:333-348, kept HIGH at _R3.md:168), whose written remediation is exactly the proposed fix — a shared `status_to_eyre(rpc_name, status)` helper with code preservation used everywhere. The blit-core half is the queued slice-2 "tonic Status → eyre" preservation on the DO-NOT-RE-REPORT list (docs/STATE.md:34), with TODO(audit-h3c-2) at pull.rs:322-329 pinning those exact map_err sites for a chain-preserving conversion that inherently restores the code and fixes the empty-message blank render. The novel residue (empty-message fallback detail, completions.rs:84 site) belongs as annotation to R3-H12/slice-2, not a separate medium finding.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:1151:  - kill reason: The headline blank-render impact is unreachable: all four production callers of build_transfer_finished_event pass Some(non-empty) on failure (core.rs:533/591/653 via outcome_from_status at core.rs:1330, which forwards status.message() on every Err; core.rs:801-818 uses hard-coded markers), so the unwrap_or("") at core.rs:336 fires only in tests (core.rs:1790-2037). No Status in blit-daemon is built with an empty message — every construction in util.rs, pull_sync.rs, pull.rs, and push/*.rs uses a literal or format!, and the one raw propagation (push/control.rs:62) yields descriptive tonic transport statuses. The proto's documented empty case (blit.proto:986-993) is the no-outcome drop path, where active_jobs.rs:1065 substitutes the non-empty "cancelled before outcome recorded" marker into recents and the event send at core.rs:541 is never reached, so nothing blank is ever rendered by jobs.rs:336 or blit-tui main.rs:5732-5735 (the latter is the subscribe-stream error path, not the TransferError render). The residual code-drop adds little to self-describing format! messages and overlaps the queued slice-2 tonic-Status error-chain work, which I was instructed to cross-reference rather than re-report.
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:1163:Method: read the design map Parts 1.1-1.5, 2 (all crate sections), then re-derived every reported mechanism from code reads in this session — every finding's mechanism rests on lines I personally read (file:line in evidence). Excluded per instructions and cross-referenced instead of re-reported: design-1 (CLI pull byte double-count — my boundaries-progress-event finding is the structural contract gap, not the bug), design-2, design-3 (my socket-policy finding covers the option/ownership half of the same call sites, not the missing connect timeout), and all queued slice-2 transport work (channel-builder triplication, client keepalive, max_decoding_message_size, adaptive windows, tonic-Status chain preservation, inert sink chunk_bytes — none re-reported; findings 1 and 3 are adjacent but cover the classifier-ownership and dual-heuristic halves the queue does not). Checked and found clean: workspace dependency directions are acyclic and sensible (blit-daemon -> blit-core only; blit-cli/blit-tui -> blit-app -> blit-core; verified all four Cargo.tomls) — the violations are duplications forced by those directions, not actual cycles; blit-cli's transport boundary is clean (no channel construction in src, admin verbs delegate to blit_app::admin — verified jobs.rs/scan.rs reads plus the map's rg results); the path_safety chokepoint is honored on both destructive delete twins today (safe_join_contained verified at delegated_pull.rs:449 and blit-app remote.rs:58/223 — the R58-F3 fix landed on both sides; my finding is about the duplication that caused the original divergence); blit-core helpers.rs:52-57 and daemon util.rs:153-158 both correctly delegate normalize_relative_path to path_posix (the mandate bypass is at other sites); the daemon push accept path and DataPlaneSession::connect agree on socket policy with each other (the asymmetry is pull-side only); blit-tui builds no gRPC channels (daemons.rs feeds RemoteEndpoint into the shared blit-app path — light-pass verification only, per the TUI rule). Not covered: blit-prometheus-bridge (map reports coherent local policy; I did not independently read it), deep TUI internals (Phase 6 rule), proto/blit.proto message-level boundary questions, change_journal/fs_capability/win_fs platform modules, and Part 1.6-1.9 of the map beyond the progress section (cancellation/platform dimensions presumably owned by sibling agents). The map's 1.4 section claims about errors.rs being dead were independently re-verified by rg this session (zero consumers confirmed).
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:1167:Verified from code this session (not map trust): every finding's mechanism was re-read at the cited lines. Checked and found CLEAN (good single-owner patterns, valuable for Phase C): TRANSFER_STALL_TIMEOUT is declared once in stall_guard.rs and imported everywhere else (rg showed only use-sites in blit-core pull/data_plane and blit-daemon push/data_plane — the one liveness constant that did consolidate correctly); mDNS is genuinely owned by blit-core/src/mdns.rs (ServiceDaemon::new appears only there); relative_path_to_posix has a single definition in path_posix.rs with both the push client (helpers.rs:52) and daemon (util.rs:153) delegating to it — the normalize_relative_path 'twins' are thin wrappers, not duplication. MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES is single-sourced in tar_safety.rs and referenced (not re-typed) by pipeline.rs per the map; I did not re-verify every wire cap. Deliberately NOT reported (cross-referenced instead): the triplicated gRPC channel builder, client keepalive absence, tonic decode limit, and the Status->eyre stringification family (all inside queued slice-2 transport work, STATE.md Queue item 2); unbounded data-plane connects (design-3); the CLI pull byte double-count bug itself (design-1 — I filed only the structural folding-rule duplication around it); orphaned daemon data planes / AbortOnDrop-vs-bare-JoinHandle (design-2 territory). Dropped as low severity: tar-shard 1 MiB reservation duplicated twice; TUI byte-formatter ladder duplicated in f2.rs/f4.rs plus blit-app/display.rs (display-only; TUI light-pass rule); throughput smoothing triplication (per-layer cadences arguably legitimate); mpsc send-error fixed-string family (likely reshaped by queued error-chain work); the '1 MiB' five-constant family (its real risk is the decode-limit invariant, which is queued). Not covered in depth: blit-prometheus-bridge (map reports it self-consistent; spot-checked only), blit-tui internals beyond progress_accum (Phase 6 rule), the double-buffered send/receive loop twins in data_plane.rs (read but judged a FAST/design question for the adaptive-streams landing rather than a consolidation slice), and Windows casefold-key divergence (already tracked as h-paths-2 in docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-paths.md per the map; not re-filed).
docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:1171:Checked and re-derived from code this session: blit-core/src/errors.rs (full read), blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs (full read), blit-core/src/remote/pull.rs 75-135 (PullSyncError/format_status), push/client/helpers.rs (map_status, [push] prefix), all daemon service files via targeted rg + reads (Status constructor tally: 116 internal / ~199 total; {err} vs {err:#} counts 12 vs ~69), util.rs (full read), delegated_pull.rs 190-260 and 360-375, core.rs outcome_from_status and TransferError event construction, blit-cli/src/jobs.rs watch render paths, blit-app/src/admin/{rm,jobs}.rs, blit-app/src/transfers/remote.rs 698-760, proto/blit.proto error messages, sink.rs log::warn and mpsc sites, logger.rs (full read), workspace-wide rg for log backends (none exist). Found clean: run_with_retries loop logic and its tests (correct budget/classification semantics); format_status itself (the one well-designed converter — finding is that it is private); admin/jobs.rs cancel_job code branching (good pattern, includes code+message); delegated_pull's R37-F1 negotiation-phase preservation via PullSyncError downcast (works as documented); color_eyre installed in blit-cli main; prometheus bridge stderr usage is internally consistent; copy-engine errors propagate correctly despite the noop Logger (verified Err(e) returned at file_copy/mod.rs:215-223). Not covered per instructions: deep TUI internals (only the main.rs:5734 boundary site, kept as light-pass evidence); the queued slice-2 territory — connect-site chain stripping (pull.rs:245, push/client/mod.rs:313), the TODO(audit-h3c-2) retry no-op at pull.rs:780-788, Status→eyre chain preservation, and retry-classifier extension to tonic codes — cross-referenced in findings 4 and 3 rather than re-reported; the three already-filed design-1/2/3 findings. Not exhaustively audited: blit-app TUI-facing transfer progress text, Windows-only win_fs error paths, and the bug-report-style empty-message reachability of every individual tonic transport error variant (mechanism shown via the in-repo format_status guard plus core.rs unwrap_or(\"\") instead).
docs/audit/inventory/plan-wire.md:148:Slow consumers receive a `gRPC Status::Aborted` on the stream (the daemon's broadcast channel dropped events while they fell behind). TUI consumers handle this by re-subscribing and refreshing snapshot state via `GetState`.
docs/audit/inventory/plan-wire.md:630:`CancelJobRequest`: `transfer_id` (1). `CancelJobResponse`: `transfer_id` (1, echoed for confirmation; outcome encoded in gRPC Status not response body).
docs/audit/inventory/plan-tui.md:518:"Four primary screens, switchable with hotkeys. Status bar at the bottom; modal overlays for confirmation / option entry. [F1] Daemons [F2] Transfers [F3] Browse [F4] Profile/Verify"

docs/audit/inventory/code-tui-state.md:26:- **f1trigger-status-editing** — `crates/blit-tui/src/f1trigger.rs:48-72` — `F1TriggerStatus::Editing` carries source+dest strings, `focus` (Source|Dest), `kind` (Copy/Mirror/Move PullKind), an optional validation `error` string, and a `confirming` bool used for destructive push y/N gating (d-65). _(notes: state holds three orthogonal sub-modes inside one variant — editing, error display, confirming — only confirming is exposed via `is_confirming`; `is_confirming` must be checked **before** `is_editing` in the router or the confirm absorbs nothing.)_
docs/audit/inventory/code-tui-state.md:115:- **daemons-status-scanning-live-degraded** — `crates/blit-tui/src/daemons.rs:104-119` — `DiscoveryStatus`: Scanning (initial), Live{last_scan_at}, Degraded{message}. Degraded preserves prior rows + adds a banner. _(notes: no "Empty" state — a Live scan with zero remote results still uses Live, just with discovered_count()=0.)_
docs/audit/inventory/code-tui-state.md:120:- **profile-fetch-status** — `crates/blit-tui/src/profile.rs:18-29` — Idle/Pending/Loaded{fetched_at}/Error{message}. `note_fetch_error` preserves prior report — operator still sees last good snapshot + error banner. _(notes: shape duplicated across daemons.rs (DaemonDetail), browse.rs (BrowseFetchStatus), profile.rs.)_
docs/audit/inventory/code-tui-state.md:154:- **Duplication: BrowseFetchStatus / ProfileFetchStatus / DaemonDetail / DiscoveryStatus** — Four parallel "fetch lifecycle" enums in `browse.rs:95-107`, `profile.rs:18-29`, `daemons.rs:126-145`, `daemons.rs:104-119`. All have Idle/Pending/Loaded{fetched_at}/Error variants with subtle differences.
docs/audit/inventory/code-tui-main.md:9:- **f2-cancel-status-enum** — `crates/blit-tui/src/main.rs:337-415` — `F2CancelStatus` has 7 variants: Idle, Confirming{transfer_id, daemon}, ConfirmingBatch{targets}, ConfirmingClearRecent, Sending{transfer_id, request_id}, BatchInitiated{count, finished_at}, Done{outcome, finished_at}, Error{transfer_id, message, finished_at}. `targets` is frozen at prompt creation (d-30 R2 race fix).
docs/audit/inventory/code-tui-main.md:194:- **`F2CancelStatus::Sending` never carries `daemon` field** — Single Confirming has `daemon` but Sending doesn't. After `Confirming → Sending` transition, the daemon field is consumed; reply is matched via `request_id` only. _(Possible subtle bug if multiple daemons could plausibly send the same transfer_id, but identities scope it.)_
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-naming-flags-confirmations.md:23:1. `crates/blit-tui/src/main.rs:1788-1810` — TUI F2 `E` ClearRecent: arms `F2CancelStatus::ConfirmingClearRecent`, on `y` calls local `transfers.clear_recent` + fans `ClearRecent` RPC to every watched daemon. Hardcoded behavior; no `[transfer]` config knob to skip.
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-naming-flags-confirmations.md:100:1. `crates/blit-tui/src/transfer.rs:46-81` — F4 local transfer state machine: two distinct enum variants `TransferStatus::ConfirmingMirror` and `TransferStatus::ConfirmingMove`. Accessors: `is_confirming_mirror()` / `is_confirming_move()` / `begin_confirm_mirror()` / `begin_confirm_move()`.
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-naming-flags-confirmations.md:101:2. `crates/blit-tui/src/f3pull.rs:88-97` — F3 remote pull state machine: ONE variant `PullStatus::Confirm { dest, dest_root, kind }` where `kind: PullKind`. One accessor `is_confirming_destructive()` / `confirm_destructive()` / `cancel_destructive()`.
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-naming-flags-confirmations.md:107:**Recommendation**: Refactor `TransferStatus` to `Confirming { kind: TransferKind }` and unify accessors. Same applies to `F1TriggerStatus::Editing { confirming }` — promote `confirming` to a proper Confirm variant or to a `TransferStatus::Confirm` reuse.
docs/audit/inventory/code-tui-display.md:35:- **f1-push-status-bridge** — `crates/blit-tui/src/display_f1.rs:64-112` — Maps `F1PushStatus` Running/Done/Error into renderer-facing `PushStatusDisplay`; uses `push_present_verb` / `push_past_verb` for the verb tense based on `(delegated, kind)`. _(notes: a remote→remote delegated **mirror** falls through to `(_, Mirror) => "mirroring"` even when delegated — special-case only triggers for delegated Copy ("delegating"); see push-verb-table.)_
docs/audit/inventory/code-tui-display.md:37:- **f2-cancel-status-to-display** — `crates/blit-tui/src/display_f2.rs:16-82` — Big match from internal `F2CancelStatus` → `screens::f2::F2CancelDisplay`. Done/Error/BatchInitiated variants auto-hide once `now - finished_at >= ttl`. _(notes: state itself stays — renderer just returns `Hidden`. Sending has no deadline.)_
docs/audit/inventory/code-tui-display.md:39:- **f3-pull-to-display** — `crates/blit-tui/src/display_f3.rs:13-64` — Maps `F3PullStatus` → `F3PullDisplay` with verbs pulled from `PullKind::verbs()` and a hardcoded confirm-detail string. _(notes: Confirm/EnteringDest pass `kind.verbs().0` imperative; Running passes `.1` present-participle; Done passes `.2` past-tense — verb-triple defined upstream in `f3pull`.)_
docs/audit/inventory/code-tui-display.md:88:- **f1-push-lifecycle** — `crates/blit-tui/src/display_f1.rs:67-112` — Idle (None) / Running / Done / Error mapped to PushStatusDisplay. _(notes: kind + delegated flags drive verb tense; delegated mirror still labels "mirroring" (no "delegating-mirror" verb).)_
docs/audit/inventory/code-tui-display.md:185:- **renderer-reads-finished-at-without-mutation** — `display_f2.rs:33, 49, 73, 113` — F2 cancel display flips to Hidden post-TTL but does NOT clear the underlying `F2CancelStatus`. The state object retains the Done/Error variant indefinitely until next cancel; if `cancel_remaining_ttl` is wired right, the loop wakes once then never again. **Reviewer comment lines 47-50 explicitly says "state itself stays — we don't mutate from renderer".** Potential subtle bug if the event loop ever skips the wakeup.
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:9:### errors-1 — `--metrics`-disabled vs `--metrics`-enabled token rejection uses different Status codes
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:12:1. `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs:185` — push data-plane invalid token → `Status::permission_denied("invalid data plane token")`
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:13:2. `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs:740` — pull data-plane invalid token → `Status::permission_denied("invalid pull data plane token")`
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:14:3. `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:632` — pull_sync data-plane invalid token → `Status::unauthenticated("invalid data plane token")`
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:15:4. `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull_sync.rs:754` — pull_sync_resume data-plane invalid token → `Status::unauthenticated("invalid data plane token")`
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:17:**Canonical**: All four are the same logical event (peer presented wrong handshake token). All four should be the same gRPC code. `Status::unauthenticated` is the semantically correct one (token = bearer credential failure), but the code surface is inconsistent across just the daemon. Pick one — operator-facing parity matters because client retry logic and metrics may dispatch on `status.code()`.
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:21:**Recommendation**: Single helper `fn reject_invalid_token() -> Status` that returns one code (Unauthenticated) and one literal string for all four sites.
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:25:### errors-2 — Admin RPC clients drop Status code; jobs RPC clients preserve it — caller sees same daemon error in two shapes
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:40:**Recommendation**: Single helper `fn status_to_eyre(rpc_name: &str, status: Status) -> eyre::Report` used everywhere. Without it, `blit rm /server:/module/foo` against a read-only module shows `"module 'foo' is read-only"` (no code) while `blit jobs cancel ...` against same-condition daemon shows `"CancelJob failed (FailedPrecondition): ..."`. The shape divergence also blocks retry classification: see errors-3.
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:44:### errors-3 — `is_retryable` can never trigger on Status-class errors because admin clients erase the io::Error chain
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:48:2. `crates/blit-app/src/admin/{rm,du,df,list_modules,ls,find}.rs` — wrap tonic `Status` via `eyre::eyre!(status.message().to_string())` — NO io::Error in the chain, even when the underlying status is `Code::Unavailable` (transport-class) or `Code::DeadlineExceeded`.
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:51:**Canonical**: `Code::Unavailable` and `Code::DeadlineExceeded` from a tonic Status are equivalent to transport failures and should be retryable. Either retry the Status directly via a code-aware classifier, OR wrap status into an eyre with an io::Error source for transient codes.
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:53:**Recommendation**: Extend `is_retryable` to also walk `eyre::chain()` for `tonic::Status` and return true on `Unavailable`/`DeadlineExceeded`/`Aborted`. Today `run_with_retries` is silently a no-op for the most common transient remote-failure class.
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:81:**Recommendation**: Pick CLI form, port to daemon. Update the daemon Status to include the module name.
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:99:### errors-7 — TUI strips `Status::code()`, only keeps `.message()` — operator loses ability to distinguish transport from authz errors in the footer
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:108:**Recommendation**: When forwarding a Status into an Error fragment, format as `"stream: {code}: {message}"` so the operator can correlate with daemon logs.
docs/audit/findings/inconsistency-errors.md:185:- Grepped: `bail!`, `eyre!`, `Status::`, `map_err`, `with_context`, `wrap_err`, "containment", "read-only", "module root", "failed to delete", "stream:", "but failed to" across `crates/blit-{cli,tui,daemon,app,core}`.
docs/audit/inventory/code-tests-scripts.md:116:- **fake-server-trait-boilerplate-x2** — `crates/blit-cli/tests/remote_remote.rs:592-892` — `UnimplementedBlit` and `RejectingPullSyncBlit` both implement the full `Blit` trait (~150 lines each), returning `Status::unimplemented` for everything except the one method under test. _(notes: severe duplication; if a new `Blit` method is added, both fake impls + the SpyServer fake in blit-core must be edited)_
docs/audit/inventory/code-tests-scripts.md:178:3. **`UnimplementedBlit` fake trait stub is enormous** — `remote_remote.rs:592-726` is 134 lines of `Status::unimplemented` returns; `RejectingPullSyncBlit` (`:728-892`) is a near-duplicate 164 lines, and `SpyServer` in `pull_sync_with_spec_wire.rs:40-181` is a third 142-line copy. Adding any method to the `Blit` trait requires editing 3 fakes. A shared `tests/common/fake_blit.rs` with default impls + override hooks would shrink this 10×.
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:5:Blit ships a coherent, well-tested 0.1.0 surface (hybrid TCP + gRPC transport, unified receive pipeline, always-on F2 containment, delegated remote→remote with explicit-only fallback, robust per-RPC daemon RPCs), but the plan corpus has not kept up with what shipped. The single largest gap is **TUI**: the rework's "Pick-not-Type" dual-pane shell is the default screen yet has no transfer-execution wiring (action bar is render-only), and the design's `detach=true` for remote→remote TUI delegations is hardcoded `false` — both undermine the "single pane of glass survives initiator disconnect" promise. Lower-altitude but pervasive drift: the planner's documented "1 s heartbeat / 10 s stall detector" architecture was never built (we ship a synchronous orchestrator with a 30 s data-plane idle guard on pull only), `--detach` shipped despite being "out of scope" in REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN, the manpage omits eight transfer flags including data-loss-class ones (`--null`, `--detach`, `--delete-scope`, `--force`, `--ignore-existing`) and two whole verbs (`jobs`, `check`), and the architecture/whitepaper still narrate auth/TLS planning that was explicitly removed from scope. Cross-cutting inconsistencies cluster around five seams — path rendering (3 ad-hoc `Path→wire` helpers with different empty-path encodings), error wrapping (admin verbs strip Status codes while jobs verbs preserve them, breaking `is_retryable`), endpoint parse Err handling (TUI F1 confirm-detail silently classifies as Local, violating an explicit project-memory rule reopened 4×), timeout/keepalive symmetry (stall guard only on pull-receive, TCP keepalive only on push-receive accept, three independent `connect_with_timeout` implementations), and destructive-action prompts (CLI `perf --clear` fires silently while TUI confirms; no `--yes` parity for `jobs clear-recent`/`jobs cancel`). What's solid: the data-plane wire format, F2 containment chokepoint, delegation gate with DNS-rebinding mitigation, spec-version fail-closed, `pull_sync_with_spec` endpoint-isolation, BlitAuth removal, and the CLI's documented data-loss reject-gates on `move` are all faithfully implemented and tested.
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:92:### 7. `--metrics`-disabled token rejection uses different gRPC Status codes across data-plane paths
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:96:**Plan says / Canonical**: Same logical event (peer presented wrong handshake token) should map to one gRPC code. `Status::unauthenticated` is the semantically correct one (token = bearer credential failure).
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:97:**Code does**: Push and Pull use `Status::permission_denied("invalid data plane token")` and `Status::permission_denied("invalid pull data plane token")` respectively; pull_sync and pull_sync_resume use `Status::unauthenticated("invalid data plane token")`. Message text also drifts ("data plane token" vs "pull data plane token").
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:99:**Suggested remediation pointer**: Single helper `fn reject_invalid_token() -> Status` returning one code (`Unauthenticated`) and one literal string used by all four sites.
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:101:### 8. Admin RPC clients erase Status code; `is_retryable` therefore never fires on transport-class remote errors
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:106:**Code does**: Six admin verbs strip the code; two jobs verbs preserve it. The retry classifier walks the eyre chain for `std::io::Error` — but tonic Statuses wrapped as `eyre!(status.message().to_string())` have no io::Error source, so `is_retryable` returns false for the most common remote-failure class.
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:108:**Suggested remediation pointer**: Shared helper `fn status_to_eyre(rpc_name: &str, status: Status) -> eyre::Report` used everywhere, AND extend `is_retryable` to walk `eyre::chain()` for `tonic::Status` returning true on `Unavailable`/`DeadlineExceeded`/`Aborted`.
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:269:### 27. TUI strips `Status::code()` on stream errors; banner says only "stream: {message}"
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:272:**Where**: `crates/blit-tui/src/main.rs:5717-5720` (forwards Status as `format!("stream: {}", status.message())`, code discarded) vs CLI `jobs.rs:393-411` (distinguishes via exit code)
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:391:**Summary**: Three error-wrapping styles for tonic Status (preserve code+message, strip to message, swap to `with_context` chain) are scattered across the codebase. Admin verbs use one style, jobs verbs another, CLI completions a third. The result: `is_retryable` cannot fire on transport-class errors because the io::Error chain is gone after admin clients wrap a Status. Same daemon condition shows two different error shapes to the operator depending on which verb hit it. Data-loss-class "unreadable paths refusal" has four flavors across CLI move, TUI move, daemon push, daemon pull_sync — three preview-lengths (5/3/0) and three rationale phrasings.
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:394:- Admin clients vs `jobs::cancel`/`jobs::clear_recent` (only the latter preserves Status code)
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:396:- Four data-plane token rejection sites using 2 different Status codes and 2 different message strings
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:398:**Canonical pattern**: One helper `status_to_eyre(rpc_name, status) -> eyre::Report` preserving code, used everywhere. One helper `format_incomplete_scan_refusal(operation, paths, side)` for the data-loss guard. Extend `is_retryable` to walk `eyre::chain()` for `tonic::Status::Unavailable|DeadlineExceeded|Aborted`.
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:419:**Canonical pattern**: One shared `TRANSFER_STALL_TIMEOUT` constant. One `connect_with_timeout` + `CONNECT_TIMEOUT` in `blit-core::remote::client`. One token-rejection helper returning one Status code. Reconcile `retry.rs` and `errors.rs` to one classifier; extend it to handle tonic Status codes. Document HTTP/2 keepalive rationale at every server-side `stream.message().await` site.
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:443:- **Token cryptographic + per-stream** — 32-byte tokens via OS RNG (`SysRng::try_fill_bytes`); RNG failure surfaces as `Status::Internal` (no panic). Audit-3b honored.
docs/audit/AUDIT_REPORT_2026-06-04.md:524:**`crates/blit-cli/src/jobs.rs`** — Inventory: `code-cli.md` §state-machine + §format-output + §error-propagation. Drift entries: #8 (admin vs jobs Status preservation), `--interval-ms` dead flag (#58).
docs/audit/findings/drift-perf.md:51:**Notes**: Plan document carries no "Status: planning" header; reads as if shipped. This is the largest structural drift in the cluster. The plan should add a top-line "Status: planning — not yet shipped" banner and either commit to the refactor or formally pause it. Filter parity (`filter-parity-pull-bail` rejected per plan) is still active per `crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/remote.rs:357` (build_filter_spec for pull).
docs/audit/findings/drift-perf.md:66:**Plan says**: "Status: Draft. Not in 0.1.0 scope." (BENCH_VERB_PLAN.md header / nongoal-bench-not-in-0-1-0)
docs/audit/2026-05-04_roadmap_audit.md:99:**SUPERSEDED** by v6 (`Status: Active (supersedes v5)` at line 301).

docs/audit/2026-05-04_roadmap_audit.md:585:   delete it. Status quo carries dead state and misleads readers.
docs/audit/inventory/code-daemon.md:45:- **rpc-subscribe** — `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/core.rs:353-455` — `subscribe` handler: atomically obtains broadcast Receiver + ring snapshot via `subscribe_with_ring`. Spawns per-subscriber forwarder that races `tx.closed()` against `broadcast_rx.recv()` (c-5a round 3 — prevents leaked Receiver during quiet periods). Replays per-row ring events BEFORE live broadcast forwarding. Lagged → `Status::aborted` with re-subscribe hint.
docs/audit/inventory/code-daemon.md:137:- **generate-token** — `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs:52-58` — Uses `rand::rngs::SysRng::try_fill_bytes`; maps RNG failure to `Status::Internal`. audit-3b — pre-fix this panicked.
docs/audit/inventory/code-daemon.md:161:- **outcome-from-status** — `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/core.rs:1327-1332` — Helper translating `Result<_, Status>` into `(ok, Option<message>)` for push/pull/pull_sync. delegated_pull has its own shape and inlines equivalent mapping.

docs/bugs/copy-destination-semantics.md:3:**Status:** **RESOLVED.** Fixed by adopting rsync's exact trailing-slash algorithm (`flist.c:send_file_list` DOTDIR_NAME + `main.c:get_local_name`) uniformly across all four transport combinations. Resolution lives at `crates/blit-app/src/transfers/resolution.rs::resolve_destination` (Phase 5 A.0 move; the CLI's `crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/mod.rs` keeps a `pub(crate) use` re-export so the dispatch site reads the same as before). Local→local, local→remote, remote→local, and remote→remote all see the same resolved destination. Regression tests pin each rule and case: `crates/blit-cli/tests/single_file_copy.rs`, `crates/blit-cli/tests/remote_pull_subpath.rs`, `crates/blit-cli/tests/remote_move.rs`, and the unit tests in `crates/blit-app/src/transfers/resolution.rs`. Manpage section "Destination Semantics (rsync-style)" in `docs/cli/blit.1.md` documents the full table; `blit copy --help` now includes the PATHS summary inline.

docs/bugs/HANDOFF-remote-pull-single-file.md:3:**Status:** **RESOLVED.** The handoff items are complete:
docs/bugs/single-file-remote-push-crash.md:3:**Status:** **RESOLVED.** Fixed across client and daemon. Regression tests in `crates/blit-cli/tests/remote_push_single_file.rs` pin the container-dir and rename cases. The fix touches both ends because `PathBuf::join("")` preserves a trailing `/` on Unix in multiple places:
docs/bugs/single-file-source-silent-noop.md:3:**Status:** **RESOLVED.** Fixed in commit `6cf07bf` via a dedicated `execute_single_file_copy` branch in the orchestrator that handles file sources without routing through the dir-oriented enumeration pipeline. The "silent no-op" class of error was further guarded in commit `67bde6d` by distinguishing `TransferOutcome::UpToDate`/`SourceEmpty`/`Transferred` so a future regression to "0 files" would print a disambiguated message instead of a false "Copy complete". Regression test: `single_file_copy_idempotent` in `crates/blit-cli/tests/single_file_copy.rs`.

docs/plan/WORKFLOW_PHASE_3.md:12:**Status**: Shipped (was: In progress.  )
docs/plan/WORKFLOW_PHASE_3.md:71:*Status 2025-11-06*: Remote push TCP data plane is now resilient and metadata-preserving. Added a hidden `--trace-data-plane` flag for diagnostics, restored streamed-file mtimes/permissions on the daemon path, and verified a 256 k-file (~9.8 GiB) home-directory mirror runs to completion without resets (`logs/blit-cli.log`, `logs/blitd.log`). Remote-to-local mirrors now reuse the pull path plus a local purge pass, enabling `blit mirror skippy://module dest/` to delete extraneous files after copying. Throughput tuning continues, but repeated re-uploads caused by mismatched metadata are resolved.  
docs/plan/WORKFLOW_PHASE_3.md:73:*Status 2025-10-28*: Remote push manifest/need-list streaming now avoids Vec allocation and feeds the daemon incrementally; both the TCP data plane and gRPC fallback batch small files into tar shards, removing per-file overhead. The daemon side now parallelises tar-shard unpacking (`TarShardExecutor`, four blocking workers) so the data plane keeps flowing while shards decode—line-rate verification on `skippy`/`mycroft` is the next gating task. CLI now pipelines tar-shard preparation so TCP transfers start as soon as the first need-list batch arrives. Added verbose `[data-plane] …` logging around the daemon to diagnose the remaining “connection reset by peer” failures (current suspect: upload channel closes when the server hits an I/O error mid-stream). Purge RPC + `blit-utils rm` landed (with confirmation prompts). Remote mirror pushes reuse the purge helper to delete extraneous files (summary reports `entries_deleted`). Daemon advertises `_blit._tcp.local.` by default and `blit scan`/`blit-utils scan` consume the results. Admin RPCs (`Find`, `DiskUsage`, `FilesystemStats`, `CompletePath`) and the corresponding `blit-utils` verbs are in place. Windows USN journal checkpoints drive the incremental fast-path (3.3.6); macOS FSEvents verification remains pending. CLI and utilities entrypoints refactored into dedicated modules so each `main.rs` is now a thin dispatcher, and the daemon service has been split into `service/{core,push,pull,admin,util}` for better maintainability.

  1223	// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  1224	
  1225	// A transfer has a SOURCE role and a DESTINATION role. Which end
  1226	// initiated, and which CLI verb was used, select roles — never code.
  1227	enum TransferRole {
  1228	  TRANSFER_ROLE_UNSPECIFIED = 0;
  1229	  TRANSFER_ROLE_SOURCE = 1;
  1230	  TRANSFER_ROLE_DESTINATION = 2;

  1232	
  1233	// First frame BOTH directions. Exact-match same-build handshake
  1234	// (D-2026-07-05-2): any inequality in either field is a
  1235	// SessionError{BUILD_MISMATCH} naming both ids, then close. No
  1236	// negotiate-down, no advisory fields, no capability bits.
  1237	message SessionHello {
  1238	  // "<crate version>+<git commit>[.dirty]", composed at compile time.
  1239	  string build_id = 1;
  1240	  // Bumped on any wire-shape change; exact match required.
  1241	  uint32 contract_version = 2;
  1242	}
  1243	
  1244	// Initiator's second frame: the whole operation, roles included.
  1245	message SessionOpen {
  1246	  // Role the INITIATOR takes; the responder takes the other.
  1247	  TransferRole initiator_role = 1;
  1248	  // Responder-side module (empty = default root export) and path
  1249	  // within it. The initiator-side path never crosses the wire — the
  1250	  // initiator owns its local endpoint.
  1251	  string module = 2;
  1252	  string path = 3;
  1253	  FilterSpec filter = 4;
  1254	  ComparisonMode compare_mode = 5;
  1255	  // Mirror is explicit: enabled + scope. No implicit mirror.
  1256	  bool mirror_enabled = 6;
  1257	  MirrorMode mirror_kind = 7;
  1258	  ResumeSettings resume = 8;
  1259	  // Request the in-stream byte carrier (diagnostics / unreachable
  1260	  // data-plane environments). The responder may also force it via a

  1263	  bool ignore_existing = 10;
  1264	  bool require_complete_scan = 11;

  1266	  // receiver advertises capacity — D-2026-06-20-1/-2; absent/0 =
  1267	  // unknown hardware value, conservative, never "old peer").
  1268	  CapacityProfile receiver_capacity = 12;

  1271	// Responder's reply. Refusals are SessionError frames, never silent
  1272	// closes.
  1273	message SessionAccept {
  1274	  // Set iff the responder is DESTINATION.
  1275	  CapacityProfile receiver_capacity = 1;

  1277	  DataPlaneGrant data_plane = 2;
  1278	}

  1282	// direction on the sockets is set by role: SOURCE writes).
  1283	message DataPlaneGrant {

  1285	  bytes session_token = 2;

   223	async fn old_push_and_session_produce_identical_trees_and_counts() {
   224	    let src = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
   225	    write_tree(src.path(), &small_tree());
   226	
   227	    // Arm A: OLD push.
   228	    let daemon_a = Daemon::start(false).await;
   229	    let mut push_client = RemotePushClient::connect(daemon_a.endpoint.clone())
   230	        .await
   231	        .expect("old push client connects");
   232	    let report = push_client
   233	        .push(
   234	            Arc::new(FsTransferSource::new(src.path().to_path_buf())),
   235	            &FileFilter::default(),
   236	            false,
   237	            MirrorMode::FilteredSubset,
   238	            false,
   239	            false,
   240	            None,
   241	            false,
   242	        )
   243	        .await
   244	        .expect("old push succeeds");
   245	
   246	    // Arm B: NEW session.
   247	    let daemon_b = Daemon::start(false).await;
   248	    let summary = run_push_session(
   249	        &daemon_b.endpoint,
   250	        Arc::new(FsTransferSource::new(src.path().to_path_buf())),
   251	        PushSessionOptions::default(),
   252	    )
   253	    .await
   254	    .expect("session push succeeds");
   255	
   256	    // Both destinations equal the source and each other.
   257	    assert_trees_identical(src.path(), &daemon_a.dest_root);
   258	    assert_trees_identical(src.path(), &daemon_b.dest_root);
   259	    assert_trees_identical(&daemon_a.dest_root, &daemon_b.dest_root);
   260	
   261	    // Shared summary counters agree (transport-specific fields —
   262	    // tcp_fallback_used/bytes_zero_copy vs in_stream_carrier_used — have
   263	    // no cross analog and are not compared).
   264	    assert_eq!(report.summary.files_transferred, summary.files_transferred);
   265	    assert_eq!(report.summary.bytes_transferred, summary.bytes_transferred);
   266	    assert_eq!(report.summary.entries_deleted, summary.entries_deleted);
   267	
   268	    daemon_a.stop().await;
   269	    daemon_b.stop().await;
   270	}
   271	
   272	#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 4)]
   273	async fn read_only_module_refuses_the_session() {
   274	    let daemon = Daemon::start(true).await; // read-only
   275	    let src = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
   276	    write_tree(src.path(), &[("a.txt", b"alpha", 1_600_000_001)]);
   277	
   278	    let err = run_push_session(
   279	        &daemon.endpoint,
   280	        Arc::new(FsTransferSource::new(src.path().to_path_buf())),
   281	        PushSessionOptions::default(),
   282	    )
   283	    .await
   284	    .expect_err("read-only module must refuse the session");
   285	    assert_eq!(fault_of(&err).code, session_error::Code::ReadOnly);
   286	    assert!(
   287	        collect_tree(&daemon.dest_root).is_empty(),
   288	        "no bytes may land on a refused session"
   289	    );
   290	    daemon.stop().await;
   291	}
   292	
   293	#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 4)]
   294	async fn unknown_module_refuses_the_session() {
   295	    let daemon = Daemon::start(false).await;
   296	    let src = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
   297	    write_tree(src.path(), &[("a.txt", b"alpha", 1_600_000_001)]);
   298	
   299	    let err = run_push_session(
   300	        &daemon.endpoint_for_missing_module(),
   301	        Arc::new(FsTransferSource::new(src.path().to_path_buf())),
   302	        PushSessionOptions::default(),
   303	    )
   304	    .await
   305	    .expect_err("unknown module must refuse the session");
   306	    assert_eq!(fault_of(&err).code, session_error::Code::ModuleUnknown);
   307	    daemon.stop().await;
   308	}
   309	
   310	#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 4)]
   311	async fn same_size_newer_destination_is_skipped_not_clobbered() {
   312	    // The unified SizeMtime decision (finding doc compare section): the
   313	    // sole push/pull divergence is same-size + dest-NEWER. The session
   314	    // adopts the data-safe, converge-up behavior — SKIP, never clobber
   315	    // a newer destination file with older source content. (--force
   316	    // overrides; not exercised here.)
   317	    let daemon = Daemon::start(false).await;
   318	
   319	    // Seed the destination with a NEWER, same-size, different-content
   320	    // file plus a file that genuinely needs updating.
   321	    write_tree(
   322	        &daemon.dest_root,
   323	        &[
   324	            ("keep.txt", b"NEWER-destination", 1_600_100_000),
   325	            ("stale.txt", b"old-destination--", 1_600_000_000),
   326	        ],
   327	    );
   328	    let src = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
   329	    write_tree(
   330	        src.path(),
   331	        &[
   332	            // same size (17) as dest keep.txt, but OLDER → must be skipped.
   333	            ("keep.txt", b"older-source-here", 1_600_000_000),
   334	            // same size (17) as dest stale.txt, but NEWER → must transfer.
   335	            ("stale.txt", b"new-source-here--", 1_600_200_000),
   336	        ],
   337	    );
   338	
   339	    let summary = run_push_session(
   340	        &daemon.endpoint,
   341	        Arc::new(FsTransferSource::new(src.path().to_path_buf())),
   342	        PushSessionOptions::default(),
   343	    )
   344	    .await
   345	    .expect("session push succeeds");
   346	
   347	    // Only stale.txt transfers; keep.txt (newer on dest) is left intact.
   348	    assert_eq!(
   349	        summary.files_transferred, 1,
   350	        "only the stale file transfers"
   351	    );
   352	    assert_eq!(
   353	        std::fs::read(daemon.dest_root.join("keep.txt")).unwrap(),
   354	        b"NEWER-destination",
   355	        "a newer same-size destination file must NOT be clobbered"
   356	    );
   357	    assert_eq!(
   358	        std::fs::read(daemon.dest_root.join("stale.txt")).unwrap(),
   359	        b"new-source-here--",
   360	        "a stale destination file must be updated"
   361	    );
   362	    daemon.stop().await;
   363	}
