5108:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1879:**2026-05-14 14:00:00Z** - **FIX**: post-edc11aa review followups. Two findings: **#1 Medium (delegated_pull logs failures as ok).** `service/core.rs:291` used `ok = !cancelled`, but `handle_delegated_pull` returned `()` and converted `run_delegated_pull` failures into an error-progress message on `tx`. So a real handler failure with the client still connected logged `delegated_pull ok ... errors=N` with `N` unchanged — the operator-facing metric contradicted the actual RPC result. Fix: `handle_delegated_pull` now returns `bool` (true=success, false=failure). The phased error is still surfaced on `tx` as before; the bool is purely the success bit. Caller in `service/core.rs` consumes via `tokio::select!` into `Option<bool>` — `None` = client cancelled, `Some(true)` = handler ok, `Some(false)` = handler failed. `ok = matches!(outcome, Some(true))`; `inc_error` fires only on `Some(false)` (handler failure), not on client cancellation. New regression test `handle_delegated_pull_returns_false_on_handler_failure` drives a blank-host source locator to trip `DelegationRejected` and asserts `false` is returned — pins the return-bool contract without mocking the full pipeline. **#2 Low (release-plan inconsistency).** Top-tracker said §3.1/§3.2/§3.3 closed, but §6 commit-sequence table still labeled steps 9–11 "Not started," step 1 said D4/D5/D6 still open, step 13 still claimed tagging is blocked on "product-owner ack of P1 deferrals," and §7 heading still said D4/D5/D6 are open. All four sites rewritten to reflect 2026-05-13 reality: steps 9–11 carry closing commits (`0d76c4f`, `6e750b9`, `6e750b9`); step 1 says "all taken — D5 modified by owner"; step 13 shows tagging blocked on step 2 only; §7 heading says "all D1–D9 decided, nothing owed anymore" with the section name retained for historical reference. **Tests**: `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean; `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo test --workspace` all pass (+1 delegated_pull regression test).
5109:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1881:**2026-05-14 12:00:00Z** - **FIX**: post-6e750b9 review followups against the --metrics work. Four findings: **#1 Medium (active-gauge race).** `format_completion_line` was reading `active_transfers` while the just-finished RPC's `_guard` was still in scope, so a single completed transfer logged `active=1` — misleading for an end-of-RPC summary. Fix: rename `let _guard = guard;` to `let guard = guard;` and `drop(guard);` explicitly before the `log_completion` call in all three spawn closures (push / pull / pull_sync at `service/core.rs`). Now `active=N` reflects state AFTER the just-finished RPC is removed from the gauge. **#2 Medium (missing RPC coverage).** `delegated_pull` increments `pull_ops` + the active gauge inside `run_delegated_pull` (`delegated_pull.rs:227`) but never emitted its own completion line — the field would only become visible on a later push/pull log. Same for `purge`. Fix: (a) wrapped the delegated_pull spawn closure in core.rs with a start-time capture and a `log_completion("delegated_pull", ...)` call after the cancellation `select!`; `ok = !cancelled` per the cancellation-race semantics. (b) Extracted `BlitService::purge_inner` so the trait `purge` method can wrap a single call site with start-time capture + `log_completion("purge", ...)` + `inc_error` on `Err`. Both paths now contribute to `--metrics` per-RPC summary symmetrically. **#3 Medium (rustfmt clean).** Already clean on `HEAD` (the prior `cargo fmt --all` at the tail of `6e750b9` had landed); reviewer was working off an older snapshot. No action. **#4 Low (stale scope text).** Three sites still claimed `--metrics` was dormant / scaffolding-only after the §3.1 closure: (a) `crates/blit-daemon/src/main.rs:82` comment block rewritten to reference `log_completion` and call out the new operator-facing summary lines; the `--metrics` `eprintln!` info line now says "per-RPC summary lines on stderr." (b) `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md:500` — old "No reader / dormant" analysis paragraph quoted as historical context inside a "Resolved 2026-05-13" callout, so the section reads consistently as closed without losing the audit-time reasoning. (c) `TODO.md:248` — AI telemetry line struck through with explicit `Removed from scope 2026-05-13` callout pointing at §5.4 + deletion of the doc; predictor note added to clarify perf-history collection is unaffected. **New regression test:** `completion_line_reflects_active_after_guard_drop` constructs an in-flight transfer + a finishing transfer, drops the finishing guard, and asserts `active=1` in the formatted line — pins the call-site contract that the formatter sees post-drop state. **Verified:** `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean, `cargo test --workspace` all pass (+1 new metrics test, 9 total in `metrics::tests`).
5112:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1891:**2026-05-07 14:00:00Z** - **DOCS**: Followup #2 catching review of `8d43e4d` — the RELEASE_PLAN_v2 itself was still presenting closed P0s as open work, which was a serious risk because `docs/plan/README.md` points new agents at it as the live source of truth for what remains before shipping. **Medium**: added a top-of-file "Closure tracker (last updated 2026-05-07)" block with a one-row-per-section table showing closing commits for §2.1/§2.2/§2.3/§2.4/§2.5/§2.7/§2.8 and §4, plus pending markers for §2.6 (hardware-bound benchmark — last open P0) and §3.1/§3.2/§3.3 (P1, doc-only). Each P0 section now has its own per-section `**Status (2026-05-07):** ✅ Closed <commit>` block at the top so a reader landing on a specific section sees current state without scrolling back to the top. §2.5 line 265 also still showed the old `blit completions remote <prefix>` syntax in the recommendation paragraph — corrected to the actual `<REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` form, matching the existing line-21 manpage. §6 commit-sequence rewritten as a status table with closing commits per row (P0 done except step 2 §2.6; P1 deferrable). §7 decisions table flipped from "Default recommendation" / "Owner" columns to "Default" / "Outcome" with closing commits where taken — D1/D2/D3/D7/D8/D9 ✅ taken, D4/D5/D6 still open. Test totals updated from the 2026-05-04 baseline (383) to current (407, after R41-R45 review-fix coverage). Footer "Last updated" + "Next review" rewritten. **Low**: `docs/plan/REMOTE_TRANSFER_PARITY.md:83` Success Criteria still said `blit-cli mirror -p -v local remote`; updated to `blit mirror`. Unlike POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md, this file is listed as still-applicable design in `docs/plan/README.md`, so a stale binary name there would mislead. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
5113:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1893:**2026-05-05 22:00:00Z** - **DOCS**: Followup to the §2.2+§4 sweep — three doc misses caught by review of commit `aac13bf`. **Medium**: `docs/plan/BENCHMARK_10GBE_PLAN.md` lines 36 + 99-100 still pointed users at `./target/release/blit-cli` and `/tmp/blit-cli` for the active 10 GbE benchmark playbook (the plan that's currently driving the §2.6 capture). Replaced with `blit` paths. **Medium**: `docs/WHITEPAPER.md:738-739` build/run example used the same stale `target/release/blit-cli`; rewritten. WHITEPAPER:7 also still described `blit-cli` as the "user binary" without noting the `[[bin]] name = "blit"` rename + admin-verb merge — corrected. **Low**: `README.md:149` documented the daemon-backed completion fallback as `blit completions remote <prefix>`, but the actual CLI signature is `blit completions remote <REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` (matching `docs/cli/blit.1.md:21`). Rewrote the README line to show the correct positional `<REMOTE>` plus `--prefix` flag form. Two intentionally-left-alone files: `docs/plan/POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md:85` (real-world incident report dated 2026-05-01 capturing the exact pre-rename command that triggered the bug — historical evidence) and `docs/bugs/{single-file-source-silent-noop,copy-destination-semantics}.md` (bug reports also dated against the pre-rename binary). Verified by `grep -rn 'release/blit-cli\|/tmp/blit-cli\|--bin blit-cli'` across `docs/`, `scripts/`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md` — only the three intentional historical references remain. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
5115:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1897:**2026-05-05 20:30:00Z** - **FIX**: Round 45 review of commit `f83a208`. **R45-F1 (High)**: while plumbing scanned features I left a `let total_bytes = scanned_bytes` alias in `crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs:480` and the streaming summary at `:567` then read that local for `summary.total_bytes`. Net effect on the wire: `summary.total_bytes` (contracted as "bytes the pipeline wrote") was reporting scanned bytes, so any incremental skip-unchanged run with all-up-to-date files would have reported the full source size as bytes-written and overcounted throughput. Fix: removed the alias; streaming summary now reads `pipeline_outcome.bytes_written` from `SinkOutcome` directly. The verbose "Planning enumerated" line correctly references `scanned_bytes`. **R45-F2 (Low)**: planner + transfer sub-predictions were still passing `(all_headers.len(), total_bytes)` (which were aliases of scanned_* but only by accident) while the total prediction passed `(scanned_files, scanned_bytes)` explicitly. Made all three predictor calls share the explicit `(scanned_files, scanned_bytes)` feature vector so a future maintainer editing one branch can't reintroduce drift by missing another. New regression test `incremental_run_total_bytes_excludes_skipped_files` in `orchestrator::orchestrator::async_runtime_tests` runs the full local mirror pipeline twice (mirror=true forces streaming planner; skip_unchanged=true means run 2 writes 0 bytes) and asserts `summary.total_bytes == 0` on the second run. Verified the test catches the bug by reintroducing the alias — assertion fires with `total_bytes=4096, scanned_bytes=4096` matching the pre-R45 state. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (was 406, +1 new R45 regression test).
5116:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1899:**2026-05-05 19:30:00Z** - **FIX**: Round 44 review of commit `da6ced2`. **R44-F1 (Medium)**: predictor train/query feature mismatch. The pre-pipeline query in `crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs` used `(all_headers.len(), total_bytes)` (scan-side workload), but `record_performance_history` recorded `summary.copied_files` for `PerformanceRecord.file_count`. On any incremental run the predictor saw "predict 1000 files" then trained on "5 files actually copied" — estimates drifted badly. Fix: added `scanned_files: usize` and `scanned_bytes: u64` to `LocalMirrorSummary` (`crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/summary.rs`) with a load-bearing rustdoc explaining the scan-vs-copy split; populated at every summary construction site (streaming pipeline, NoWork, Tiny, Huge, JournalSkip, single-file copy); `record_performance_history` now reads `summary.scanned_{files,bytes}` for the record's feature fields. The bucket-shape counters (`tar_shard_*`, `raw_bundle_*`, `large_*`) still reflect actual writes — they feed `derive_local_plan_tuning` which heuristically sizes destination buckets from past apply behavior, not scan size. Extracted `build_performance_record` from `record_performance_history` so the contract is unit-testable without polluting the global JSONL. Two new tests in `orchestrator::history::tests`: `record_uses_scanned_features_not_copied` (would have failed pre-fix on a 1000-scanned/5-copied incremental) and `bucket_counts_still_reflect_actual_writes` (regression guard against accidentally rerouting bucket counters through scan stats). **R44-F2 (Low)**: `blit profile --json` shape was unasserted. The pre-fix commit message claimed top-level `"predictor": null` for the empty-state, but actual output emits `{"copy": null, "mirror": null}` (predictor file loads even when no profile yet has training data). Picked the structured shape — strictly more informative — and pinned it in `test_utils_profile_json` (`crates/blit-cli/tests/blit_utils.rs`) with assertions covering both empty and trained states. **R44 plan note**: §2.8 of `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md` is correctly framed now as "predictor observability and training," not "adaptive planning behavior" — the predictor is queried, surfaced via `--verbose` and `blit profile --json`, and audit-able, but the Tiny fast-path still does not consult it for routing. Added a Status block to the plan section documenting this. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 406 pass / 0 fail (was 404, +2 history regression tests).
5120:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1907:**2026-05-05 16:30:00Z** - **FIX**: Round 42 review of commit `96cbb10`. **R42-F1 (Medium)**: §1.1 sweep was incomplete — three sites still silently dropped metadata errors. (a) `crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/tar_safety.rs::write_extracted_file` mtime + permissions (used by both pull tar-shard receive and the daemon gRPC push fallback); (b) `crates/blit-core/src/orchestrator/orchestrator.rs::execute_single_file_copy` mtime — single-file copy fast-path was bypassing the warn-don't-silence policy. All three converted to `if let Err(e) = ... { log::warn!(...) }`. Final sweep confirms no `let _ = ` over `set_file_mtime` / `set_permissions` / `set_keepalive` / `.flush()` remains in production paths (the two leftovers — `diff_planner.rs::sync_mtimes` test helper and `win_fs.rs::clear_readonly_recursive` cleanup utility — are appropriately best-effort). **R42-F2 (Low)**: the new pipeline-streaming regression test only exercised `execute_sink_pipeline_streaming` directly, leaving `MultiStreamSender::queue`'s producer-side join-error-drain logic untested. Extracted the load-bearing match arms into a free `drain_pipeline_error(handle: JoinHandle<...>) -> eyre::Report` helper in `crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/mod.rs`. `MultiStreamSender::queue` and `MultiStreamSender::finish` both now route their failure paths through it, so the wrapping reads consistently as "data plane pipeline failed: <cause>" or "data plane pipeline panicked: <join>" regardless of which branch noticed the failure first. Three new unit tests in a fresh `drain_pipeline_error_tests` mod: `drain_returns_pipeline_error_with_failed_prefix` (Ok(Err(_)) path), `drain_returns_panic_message_for_aborted_task` (Err(JoinError) panic path), `drain_diagnoses_clean_close_race` (Ok(Ok(_)) clean-close-race diagnostic — proves we don't silently swallow it). **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 396 pass / 0 fail (was 393, +3 drain-helper tests).
5124:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1915:**2026-05-04 22:00:00Z** - **FIX**: Round 41 review (R41-F1, R41-F2, R41-F3) of commits `0ca489b` + `090f5cd`. **R41-F1 (High)**: binary-rename sweep was incomplete — five test files (`diagnostics_dump.rs`, `remote_tcp_fallback.rs`, `remote_remote.rs`, `remote_checksum_negotiation.rs`, `remote_pull_mirror.rs`) still constructed `bin_dir.join("blit-cli[.exe]")`, so `cargo test --workspace` on a clean target tree failed with `spawn: No such file or directory`; the previous "385 pass" report only held because stale `target/release/blit-cli` was still around. All five updated to `"blit" / "blit.exe"`. Cosmetic panic-message strings in `remote_resume.rs`, `remote_regression.rs`, `remote_parity.rs`, `remote_push_single_file.rs` swept for consistency. **R41-F2 (High)**: bench scripts still pointed at the old binary — `scripts/bench_remote_remote.sh` (BLIT default + comment), `scripts/bench_10gbe.sh` (BLIT path), `scripts/bench_local_mirror.sh` (build invocation `--bin blit-cli` and resulting BLIT_BIN path) all updated. **R41-F3 (Medium)**: `find --pattern` glob set `literal_separator(false)`, letting `*` cross `/`. With the basename fallback this caused over-matches like `foo*.csv` matching `foo/bar.csv`. Switched to `literal_separator(true)` (POSIX shell-glob semantics; `*` does NOT cross `/`); basename fallback still gives "find files at any depth" via `*.csv`. Added regression test `test_admin_find_glob_star_does_not_cross_path_separator`. CLI `FindArgs.pattern` rustdoc rewritten to spell out the no-cross-separator behavior and the basename fallback. **Tests**: clean `target/`, `cargo test --workspace` 386 pass / 0 fail (was 385, +1 new R41-F3 test).
5132:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1933:**2026-05-02 22:15:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 15 finding R15-F1 (Low) — F11's user-facing closure. The previous F11 fix added daemon ack-mismatch handling in `RemotePullClient::pull_sync`, but `transfers/endpoints.rs::ensure_remote_transfer_supported` rejected `--checksum` for ALL remote transfers before the new code could fire. F11's CLI behavior was therefore unreachable. Made the gate direction-aware: `ensure_remote_pull_supported` allows `--checksum` (the F11 ack negotiates), `ensure_remote_push_supported` keeps it rejected for push/remote-remote (no equivalent capability negotiation in the push protocol yet). New integration test `remote_checksum_negotiation.rs` spins up a daemon with `--no-server-checksums`, runs `blit copy --checksum server:/test/file.txt dest`, and asserts (a) the client refuses with a clear ack-mismatch error, (b) no file is copied via silent fallback. Companion test confirms the same command succeeds against a daemon with checksums enabled. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (188 lib + 23 daemon + 27 metrics-test bin + 2 new checksum-negotiation integration tests, all green).
5133:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1935:**2026-05-02 21:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed F5, F11, F12, F9 from `docs/reviews/codebase_review_2026-05-01.md` — the lower-priority items from the baseline triage. **F5** (Medium): replaced inc_active/dec_active scattered around `service/core.rs` with `TransferMetrics::enter_transfer() -> ActiveGuard`, an RAII handle that decrements on Drop so panics/cancellation can't leak the gauge. Moved `inc_purge` to dispatch boundary so it counts attempts (matching push/pull semantics) instead of successes. Module doc updated with the attempts/errors/gauge contract. 5 metrics tests including panic-recovery assertion. **F11** (Medium): pull_sync receive loop now stores `ack.server_checksums_enabled` on `RemotePullReport` and bails early at the ack if the user requested `--checksum` but the daemon has checksums disabled — silent fallback to size+mtime would lie about the comparison strength the user asked for. **F12** (Low–Medium): documented `blit check` equivalence model in CLI `--help` (new `CHECK_SEMANTICS_HELP` constant, `after_long_help` on `CheckArgs`) and `CheckArgs` rustdoc. Pinned the documented behavior with 6 unit tests covering: matching-files-zero-diffs, empty-dirs-not-in-equivalence, symlinks-skipped, file-vs-dir-mismatch-on-file-side, missing-on-dest, --one-way ignores extras. **F9** (Medium): split `execute_local_mirror` into a sync wrapper + new `execute_local_mirror_async`. The async core is callable from any Tokio runtime; the sync wrapper just builds a runtime and block_ons. 2 tests prove async-from-async-context works and the sync wrapper is unchanged. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (188 lib + 23 daemon + 27 metrics-test bin + integration suites, all green).
5134:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1937:**2026-05-02 20:15:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 13 finding R13-F1 (Medium). The F2 fix protected per-file writes via `resolve_contained_path`, but the push-mirror purge path enumerates `module.path` (post-mutation by `destination_path`) before the per-entry containment check fires. If the supplied `destination_path` traversed an in-module symlink pointing outside the module root, the purge would enumerate outside the canonical root before refusing any individual delete. Two-layer fix: (1) `push/control.rs` now calls `verify_contained(&canonical_root, &new_path)` immediately after the destination_path mutation, rejecting the push at handshake; (2) `purge_extraneous_entries` calls `verify_contained` before enumeration, defense-in-depth for any future caller that bypasses the handshake check. New integration test `f2_push_destination_path_symlink_rejected` spins up a daemon, places `module/escape -> /sibling`, attempts `blit mirror src/ server:/test/escape/`, and asserts both the daemon refusal and that `/sibling`'s files were not touched. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (186 lib + 20 daemon + 3 F2 integration tests, all green).
5136:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1941:**2026-05-02 18:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 11 finding R11-F1 (High) — completed F7 properly. The previous F7 fix bounded the *reservation* via `try_reserve_exact` but the actual `read_to_end` call would still grow the Vec past that bound if the remote source streamed extra bytes. A peer could declare `header.size = 4` and stream 1 GiB; the post-read length check would catch the discrepancy but only after the buffer had grown past the bound. Fix: extract `read_remote_entry_bounded` as a generic helper that wraps the reader with `tokio::io::AsyncReadExt::take(size + 1)` so the read itself is capped; the +1 is the over-read canary that triggers the post-read length check. Helper is `R: AsyncRead + Unpin` so tests use `std::io::Cursor` instead of a real `RemotePullClient` mock. 5 new tests in `remote_bounded_read_tests`: happy path, under-read, over-read (verifies buffer caps at size+1 even when source sends 1 MiB), defensive cap rejection, empty file. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (176 lib + 20 daemon + integration suites, all green). F7 now fully closed.
5137:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1943:**2026-05-02 17:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed F7 + F8 from `docs/reviews/codebase_review_2026-05-01.md` (the original baseline review) in one small hardening pass. **F7** (now elevated to High in the triage): `RemoteTransferSource::prepare_payload` was doing `Vec::with_capacity(header.size as usize)` from a daemon-controlled `FileHeader.size` — the send-side mirror of R6-F1 that affects remote→remote transfers. Extracted `validate_remote_tar_shard_sizes` as a private helper that bounds both per-entry and cumulative shard sizes against `tar_safety::MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES`, uses `checked_add` for the sum, and is unit-testable without a `RemotePullClient` mock. Replaced the unbounded `Vec::with_capacity` with `try_reserve_exact`, plus an explicit length-match check after `read_to_end` so a remote source that lies about a file's size produces a hard error rather than a malformed tar. **F8**: `MAX_WIRE_TAR_SHARD_BYTES` in pipeline.rs was 1 GiB while the helper's `MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES` is 256 MiB — the wire reader accepted shards the helper would reject. Single source of truth: `MAX_WIRE_TAR_SHARD_BYTES = tar_safety::MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES as usize` (const). 5 new tests in `remote_tar_size_tests` (within bounds, at cap, per-entry above cap, cumulative above cap, u64 overflow). **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (171 lib + 20 daemon + integration suites, all green).
5138:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1945:**2026-05-02 16:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 9 finding R9-F1 (Low). The R8-F2 fix (rejecting stream EOF without UploadComplete) was previously verified by code review only — the new framing-helper unit tests covered R8-F1 size/cap rules but not the actual EOF path through `receive_fallback_data`. To make the function unit-testable without a real gRPC server, generic-ified its signature over `tokio_stream::Stream<Item = Result<ClientPushRequest, Status>>` (tonic::Streaming already implements this trait, so production callers are unchanged) and switched the loop body from `.message()` to `.next().await.transpose()` — semantically identical. 3 new regression tests in `data_plane::tests` drive the function over `tokio_stream::iter` of synthetic message vectors: EOF after `FileManifest`, EOF after `TarShardHeader`, and EOF on an empty stream (no UploadComplete ever). All three assert the rejection now lives in the test suite, not just code review. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (166 lib + 20 daemon + integration suites, all green).
5139:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1947:**2026-05-02 15:45:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 8 review findings R8-F1 and R8-F2 from `docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md`. Both bugs were in the daemon's gRPC push fallback receive loop (`receive_fallback_data` in `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs`) — the framing layer that runs *before* the shared `tar_safety` helper sees the buffer. **R8-F1** (Medium — DoS): the chunk accumulator was capped only by an 8 MiB initial reservation; the actual `Vec` grew unbounded. `archive_size == 0` skipped the overflow check entirely, and `archive_size > MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES` was bypassed at the framing layer. Reject zero archive_size when files are present, reject `archive_size > MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES` at TarShardHeader, and enforce both the declared size and the local cap on every chunk via a new `check_fallback_chunk_overflow` helper. **R8-F2** (Medium — silent partial success): stream EOF was treated as graceful end. But FileManifest and TarShardHeader remove entries from `pending` before bytes arrive, so a client could send a header, close the stream, and the final `pending.is_empty()` check would pass despite no data ever landing. Track `upload_complete_seen` and bail on EOF without UploadComplete or with `active.is_some()`. Extracted `validate_fallback_shard_archive_size` and `check_fallback_chunk_overflow` as private helpers so the rules are unit-testable without spinning up a full gRPC server. 7 new framing tests pin every boundary condition (zero size, at-cap, above cap, declared overflow, local-cap overflow, u64 overflow, within-bounds). **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (166 lib + 17 daemon + integration suites, all green).
5140:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1949:**2026-05-02 14:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Round 7 cleanup — extracted shared `tar_safety` primitive in `blit-core/src/remote/transfer/tar_safety.rs` and migrated all three tar-shard receive sites onto it: `pull.rs::apply_pull_tar_shard` (gRPC fallback), `sink.rs::write_tar_shard_payload` (TCP data plane + local-local), `blit-daemon/src/service/push/data_plane.rs::apply_tar_shard_sync` (daemon push receive). The migration closed a previously-latent High-severity bug on the daemon side: `apply_tar_shard_sync` was calling `Entry::unpack`, so an authenticated push client could ship a tar shard with a Symlink entry at a benign path pointing at `/etc/passwd` and a later push to that path would write through the symlink — same R5-F2 class as the pull side, but server-side. New regression test `apply_tar_shard_rejects_symlink_entry` proves the daemon now rejects non-regular entries up front. The shared helper also brought R6-F1 size bounds and R6-F3 mtime/permissions preservation to `sink.rs` and the daemon, where they were missing. 8 new unit tests in `tar_safety::tests`; net +8 lib tests workspace-wide (166 total). **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` all green.
5141:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1951:**2026-05-02 13:15:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 6 review findings R6-F1 through R6-F3 from `docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md`. **R6-F1** (Medium — DoS via per-entry size): `apply_pull_tar_shard` was preallocating with `Vec::with_capacity(header.size as usize)`, where `header.size` came from the daemon-authored `TarShardHeader.files`. A daemon could keep the shard `archive_size` under the 256 MiB cap while putting `u64::MAX` in a per-file header, forcing a huge initial allocation. Now validates `entry.size() == header.size` and `header.size <= shard.declared_size <= MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES` before any allocation, then uses `Vec::try_reserve_exact` so a still-pathological size returns `AllocError` instead of aborting. **R6-F2** (Medium — silent partial success): if the gRPC stream ended with `active_shard = Some(_)` (Header sent, no Complete), the receive loop dropped the buffered shard and returned success. Extracted the post-loop check into `ensure_no_open_shard` and made it bail with a wire protocol error — same treatment as `FileData` without a preceding `FileHeader`. **R6-F3** (Medium — metadata desync): manual tar-shard writes weren't applying `header.mtime_seconds` or `header.permissions`, so a forced-gRPC pull of many small files succeeded byte-for-byte but left destination mtimes at "now", causing the next size+mtime sync to re-transfer everything. Now applies mtime via `filetime::set_file_mtime` when `> 0` and Unix permissions when nonzero, mirroring `FsTransferSink`. 3 new unit tests in `tar_shard_safety_tests` (rejects oversized per-entry size, rejects open shard at end-of-stream, preserves mtime). **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (158 lib tests + integration suites, all green).
5142:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1953:**2026-05-02 11:30:00Z** - **ACTION**: Closed Round 5 review findings R5-F1 through R5-F5 from `docs/reviews/followup_review_2026-05-02.md`. **R5-F1** (High — data-loss vector): `delete_listed_paths` used a lexical `target.starts_with(dest_root)` check that lets `dest_root.join("../victim")` pass. Routed every wire-supplied path through `path_safety::safe_join`, switched `RemotePullReport::paths_to_delete` from `Vec<PathBuf>` to `Vec<String>` so wire input never gets blessed into a path before validation, and added 4 unit tests in `delete_list_safety_tests` covering parent-traversal, absolute paths, root-self-reference, and the happy path. **R5-F2** (High — wire-controlled symlink/hardlink creation): `apply_pull_tar_shard` was calling `Entry::unpack` which honors tar's symlink/hardlink/device entry types. Rewrote to reject anything that isn't `EntryType::Regular` or `EntryType::Continuous`, validate the path through `validate_wire_path`, copy bytes manually, and verify per-entry size matches the corresponding `FileHeader.size`. Added 4 unit tests including a hand-crafted malicious tar with `../escape.txt` in its header. **R5-F3** (Medium — DoS via huge `archive_size`): `Vec::with_capacity(header.archive_size as usize)` was a daemon-controlled allocation. Added `MAX_TAR_SHARD_BYTES = 256 MiB` cap, modest initial reservation (capped at 1 MiB), per-chunk overflow check against `declared_size` and the local cap, and a final length check on `TarShardComplete`. **R5-F4** (Low — comment lied about validation): `filter_from_spec` claimed to validate glob patterns but only copied them into `FileFilter` for later silent dropping. Now compiles each include/exclude pattern through `globset::Glob::new` at the spec normalizer; 2 new tests pin rejection of malformed brackets. **R5-F5** (Low — DEVLOG ordering): added this header and moved newest entries to the top so review workflow doesn't depend on knowing how to find appended-at-the-bottom changes. **Tests**: `cargo fmt`; `cargo test --workspace` (152+ lib tests, all integration suites green).
5149:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1975:**2026-05-01 23:43:57Z** - **REVIEW**: Completed a repo-level codebase review and saved it to `docs/reviews/codebase_review_2026-05-01.md`. Highest-priority findings: receive-side path sanitization is incomplete across streamed files, tar shards, and resume block records; daemon `use_chroot` is documented/configured but not enforced; filtered mirror delete semantics need an explicit product decision; metrics lifecycle and endpoint behavior need hardening. Added a short `TODO.md` follow-up section and marked Phase 4 as in progress with the review artifact linked. **Verification**: `cargo check --workspace` passed; `cargo test --workspace` passed with 185 tests after rerunning outside the sandbox to allow local test port binding.
5154:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2027:**2026-07-04 21:33:31Z** - **CODER (w4-4-blocking-work-off-runtime, claude)**: Landed w4-4 through the codex loop (same session, standing "reviewloop" go → topmost ratified open row after w6-1/w6-2). Both audit halves re-verified at HEAD before coding — the 2026-06-11 sites moved: daemon pull.rs died at ue-r2-1h, its enumeration relocated into pull_sync.rs; the push manifest loop's per-entry blocking is control.rs `file_requires_upload` (canonical-containment ancestor walk ≥2 canonicalize syscalls + stat, inline on a tokio worker — ~3M+ blocking syscalls for a 1M-file push). Slice `0feca34`: **(A)** manifest entries buffer into `PendingManifestEntry` and drain through `drain_manifest_checks` — ONE spawn_blocking per chunk runs the batch's checks (batch moved in/out, no clones), need-list pushes follow in async context in manifest order; `MANIFEST_CHECK_CHUNK` = FILE_LIST_EARLY_FLUSH_ENTRIES (128); the mid-manifest TCP data-plane spin-up moved from per-entry to post-chunk-drain (same guard — design-4's no-mid-manifest-fallback-negotiation invariant untouched); ManifestComplete drains the sub-chunk remainder (no spin-up — post-manifest negotiation owns it); the spec's lexical-containment alternative was REJECTED as weakening the F2 canonical posture (an in-module symlink could redirect the stat outside; check stays canonical, now on a blocking thread). **(B)** `collect_pull_entries_with_checksums` now runs entirely on one spawn_blocking thread (thin async wrapper + `collect_pull_entries_sync`; the directory branch's inner spawn_blocking unwrapped): previously the single-file branch ran its metadata probes and — with --checksum — a full synchronous Blake3 of an arbitrarily large file inline on a runtime worker, plus the top-level is_file/is_dir probes. Review: codex **NEEDS FIXES (1 Medium, accepted — a genuinely good catch)**: chunk-only draining muted the need-list batcher's 64 KiB/5 ms early-flush triggers between chunk boundaries (they only evaluate inside push(), which chunking confines to drain time) — a slowly-enumerating client's first FilesToUpload + TCP spin-up would wait for 128 trickled entries instead of ~5 ms; fixed `768e7e3` with `manifest_drain_due(pending_len, oldest_buffered)` — drain on chunk-full OR oldest-entry age ≥ MANIFEST_CHECK_MAX_DELAY (= the batcher's own delay bound), evaluated on next arrival exactly like the batcher's push-time semantics; fast streams still hit the cap first. Tests: +4 `manifest_check_batch_tests` (decision parity incl. POSIX wire paths + order, empty-drain no-op, containment-escape rejection through the batched path, trigger contract), mutation-verified (all-true check → parity pin fails); half B rides existing pins (single_file_filter_tests; the 500-file design-5 e2e drives ≥3 chunk drains + remainder + spin-up end-to-end). Validation both commits: fmt/clippy clean, `cargo test --workspace` 1472 → 1476/0/2 across 37 suites. All on master, unpushed. Next: strict design-queue order gives w9-3 (test-harness builder) as topmost ratified open row; filed alternatives w6-2a/b/c + design-3 + relay-1, coder's pick.
5157:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2035:**2026-07-04 15:24:23Z** - **CODER (w2-2-stream-ladder-owner, claude)**: Landed w2-2 through the codex loop (owner go: "continue" → topmost open row per the 12th handoff). The row as filed (2026-06-11) predates REV4, which already delivered its three stream-count legs: the `determine_remote_tuning` ladder died at ue-r2-1e (live dial), daemon `desired_streams` at ue-r2-1f (`engine::initial_stream_proposal` — byte- AND file-count-keyed, satisfying the spec's "takes file_count"), and `pull_stream_count` with the Pull RPC at ue-r2-1h; D-2026-06-20-1 recorded the absorption in v1 slice IDs. The remaining leg — the transfer_plan 16/32 MiB chunk ladder — turned out to be **entirely dead policy**, established by a 5-agent audit workflow + hand verification: every remote path overrode it with `Some(dial.chunk_bytes())` (push client 5 refresh sites + ensure_dial, pull_sync both literals); the only paths where the ladder won (local engine, test callers) discarded the value (`PlanUpdate` carries payloads only); the single workspace read of `PlannedPayloads.chunk_bytes` sat behind a `chunk_bytes == 0` guard no live caller can trigger (all pass the dial value, floored ≥ 64 KiB). The spec's "make transfer_plan take chunk_bytes as input" predates the dial — with zero consumers, threading a value through the planner would be plumbing with no reader, so the honest single-owner outcome was deletion. Slice `01209bc`: ladder + `Plan` wrapper deleted (`build_plan` → `Vec<TransferTask>`); `PlannedPayloads` deleted (`plan_transfer_payloads` → `Result<Vec<TransferPayload>>`, ripple through diff_planner/streaming_plan/pipeline tests/re-exports); `PlanOptions.chunk_bytes_override` + all refresh sites deleted (push `plan_options` now immutable default; two arms keep bare `ensure_dial` calls — first-need creation and first-wins ceilings unchanged); unreachable fallback guard in `stream_fallback_from_queue` deleted; `plan_to_daemon_format` deleted (git log -S: never called in repo history — its "server pull mode" comment was never true); orphaned `TuningParams` deleted (producer died at ue-r2-1e); write-only kickoff histogram collapsed to the `total_bytes` accumulator that was its only read. Comment-truth sweep: dial.rs mutability-model doc no longer claims chunk/prefetch are "read at each use site" (consumers snapshot at session/pipeline/batch setup; steps reach epoch-N sockets and later fallback batches); buffer.rs example cites the dial, not `TuningParams`. Behavior byte-identical on every live path. Tests: +4 transfer_plan unit pins (module had zero) — tier classification/interleave, single-small-file no-tar, force_tar single-file, count-target shard splitting with the 128 clamp; deletions are compile-guarded (w2-1 evidence shape); zero tests deleted. Review: codex **NEEDS FIXES (1 Low)** — the first bare ensure_dial comment said "fallback batch" inside the `TransferMode::DataPlane` branch; accepted (mislabel sits exactly on the invariant under review), fixed `27f53a0` (one word). W3.1's "after W2.2 settles the tuning owner" prerequisite is now settled: the owner is `engine::TransferDial`. New discoveries → STATE Open questions: `725aa07` tracked a 236-file stale worktree snapshot (`.claude/worktrees/vigilant-mayer/`) into the repo; WHITEPAPER still describes the pre-dial tuning world (stale since ue-r2-1e, w10 territory). Validation both commits: fmt/clippy clean, `cargo test --workspace` 1452/0/2 across 37 suites (baseline 1448). All on master, unpushed. Next: w3-1 (memory-aware BufferPool) tops the open queue; design-3 remains the sanctioned smaller alternative.
5158:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2037:**2026-07-04 14:25:35Z** - **CODER (w4-5-supports-cancellation-flip, claude)**: Landed w4-5 through the codex loop (owner go: "continue" → topmost open row per the 11th handoff). D-2026-07-04-3 executed: `ActiveJobKind::supports_cancellation` flipped from DelegatedPull-only to `!matches!(self, Pull)` — every kind that can hold an active row is now cancellable; only history-only `Pull` stays gated (its RPC died at ue-r2-1h, so the Unsupported/FailedPrecondition/exit-2 arm survives end-to-end purely as the contract's escape hatch). `blit jobs cancel` and the TUI `K`/`Shift+X` now fire the row token for attached Push/PullSync; the w4-3 dispatcher race makes teardown + client `Status::cancelled` real. Slice `05a8b39`. A 3-agent workflow sweep (prod code / tests / docs) enumerated every surface encoding the old policy before editing: confirmed the TUI has NO kind-based cancel gating and the CLI 0/1/2 mapping is outcome-based — the daemon-side predicate is the entire behavior change; zero CLI/TUI logic edits. Comment surfaces updated: `supports_cancellation` rustdoc rewritten as the decision requires (old "disconnecting IS the cancel" rationale recorded as superseded), `CancelOutcome::Unsupported` + blit-app `CancelJobOutcome::Unsupported` docs, `cancel_job`'s FailedPrecondition message de-rationalized, `resolve_streaming_outcome`'s "production-unreachable" note now says the arm is live, `proto/blit.proto`'s CancelJob wire-contract comment (the last flat old-policy statement), `jobs_lifecycle.rs` header, w4-3 finding doc scope note annotated per the decision's Supersedes line. Mutation-verified: reverting the predicate fails exactly those four tests (4/15 under the `cancel` filter), restore → all 19 green. Validation both commits: fmt/clippy clean, `cargo test --workspace` 1448/0/2 across 37 suites (baseline 1446). All on master, unpushed. 5172:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2101:**2026-05-14 14:00:00Z** - **FIX**: post-edc11aa review followups. Two findings: **#1 Medium (delegated_pull logs failures as ok).** `service/core.rs:291` used `ok = !cancelled`, but `handle_delegated_pull` returned `()` and converted `run_delegated_pull` failures into an error-progress message on `tx`. So a real handler failure with the client still connected logged `delegated_pull ok ... errors=N` with `N` unchanged — the operator-facing metric contradicted the actual RPC result. Fix: `handle_delegated_pull` now returns `bool` (true=success, false=failure). The phased error is still surfaced on `tx` as before; the bool is purely the success bit. Caller in `service/core.rs` consumes via `tokio::select!` into `Option<bool>` — `None` = client cancelled, `Some(true)` = handler ok, `Some(false)` = handler failed. `ok = matches!(outcome, Some(true))`; `inc_error` fires only on `Some(false)` (handler failure), not on client cancellation. New regression test `handle_delegated_pull_returns_false_on_handler_failure` drives a blank-host source locator to trip `DelegationRejected` and asserts `false` is returned — pins the return-bool contract without mocking the full pipeline. **#2 Low (release-plan inconsistency).** Top-tracker said §3.1/§3.2/§3.3 closed, but §6 commit-sequence table still labeled steps 9–11 "Not started," step 1 said D4/D5/D6 still open, step 13 still claimed tagging is blocked on "product-owner ack of P1 deferrals," and §7 heading still said D4/D5/D6 are open. All four sites rewritten to reflect 2026-05-13 reality: steps 9–11 carry closing commits (`0d76c4f`, `6e750b9`, `6e750b9`); step 1 says "all taken — D5 modified by owner"; step 13 shows tagging blocked on step 2 only; §7 heading says "all D1–D9 decided, nothing owed anymore" with the section name retained for historical reference. **Tests**: `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean; `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo test --workspace` all pass (+1 delegated_pull regression test).
5173:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2103:**2026-05-14 12:00:00Z** - **FIX**: post-6e750b9 review followups against the --metrics work. Four findings: **#1 Medium (active-gauge race).** `format_completion_line` was reading `active_transfers` while the just-finished RPC's `_guard` was still in scope, so a single completed transfer logged `active=1` — misleading for an end-of-RPC summary. Fix: rename `let _guard = guard;` to `let guard = guard;` and `drop(guard);` explicitly before the `log_completion` call in all three spawn closures (push / pull / pull_sync at `service/core.rs`). Now `active=N` reflects state AFTER the just-finished RPC is removed from the gauge. **#2 Medium (missing RPC coverage).** `delegated_pull` increments `pull_ops` + the active gauge inside `run_delegated_pull` (`delegated_pull.rs:227`) but never emitted its own completion line — the field would only become visible on a later push/pull log. Same for `purge`. Fix: (a) wrapped the delegated_pull spawn closure in core.rs with a start-time capture and a `log_completion("delegated_pull", ...)` call after the cancellation `select!`; `ok = !cancelled` per the cancellation-race semantics. (b) Extracted `BlitService::purge_inner` so the trait `purge` method can wrap a single call site with start-time capture + `log_completion("purge", ...)` + `inc_error` on `Err`. Both paths now contribute to `--metrics` per-RPC summary symmetrically. **#3 Medium (rustfmt clean).** Already clean on `HEAD` (the prior `cargo fmt --all` at the tail of `6e750b9` had landed); reviewer was working off an older snapshot. No action. **#4 Low (stale scope text).** Three sites still claimed `--metrics` was dormant / scaffolding-only after the §3.1 closure: (a) `crates/blit-daemon/src/main.rs:82` comment block rewritten to reference `log_completion` and call out the new operator-facing summary lines; the `--metrics` `eprintln!` info line now says "per-RPC summary lines on stderr." (b) `docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md:500` — old "No reader / dormant" analysis paragraph quoted as historical context inside a "Resolved 2026-05-13" callout, so the section reads consistently as closed without losing the audit-time reasoning. (c) `TODO.md:248` — AI telemetry line struck through with explicit `Removed from scope 2026-05-13` callout pointing at §5.4 + deletion of the doc; predictor note added to clarify perf-history collection is unaffected. **New regression test:** `completion_line_reflects_active_after_guard_drop` constructs an in-flight transfer + a finishing transfer, drops the finishing guard, and asserts `active=1` in the formatted line — pins the call-site contract that the formatter sees post-drop state. **Verified:** `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean, `cargo test --workspace` all pass (+1 new metrics test, 9 total in `metrics::tests`).

Unlike POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md, this file is listed as still-applicable design in `docs/plan/README.md`, so a stale binary name there would mislead. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
